## 2026-07-07 Long/short corporate actions in SimBroker (fix/corporate-actions)

Task: close the 4th-audit High "多头公司行为完全缺失" (and the documented-disabled short side): raw-price replay booked ex-date gaps as pure long losses and short windfalls, dividends were absent from total/long/short returns, and PIT-visible dividend announcements taught the Agent to dump holdings before ex-dates.

Design (approved by user; explicit ex-date events, NOT adjusted prices — limits/lots/uptick/QMT live all operate in raw price space):
- Replay slot gains `corporate_actions.parquet` (`snapshot._build_corporate_actions`): implemented (`div_proc=="实施"`) dividend rows from the PIT fundamental events with `ex_date` inside the window, read WITHOUT a lower available_at bound (announcements can precede the window); guards drop rows missing `ex_date` or announced after their own ex-date (revision artifacts), latest announcement wins per (ts_code, end_date, ex_date), same-day events per code are summed. Columns: ts_code/ex_date/record_date/pay_date/div_listdate/cash_per_share (gross 税前, cash_div_tax with cash_div fallback)/stock_per_share (stk_div with bo+co fallback). Environment-side market truth — NOT agent-visible; agent dividend visibility stays announcement-gated (no PIT leak; data doc updated).
- `SimBroker.roll_to_date` applies the date's actions once, before the day's first tick (盘前到账; entitlement = overnight position = record-date close holding): longs get cash × (1 − `dividend_tax_rate`) and floor(qty × ratio) bonus shares (entry_price rebased so entry value/cost stay continuous; bonus locked until `div_listdate` via the T+1 lock when it lags the ex-date); shorts pay the GROSS cash compensation to the lender and owe the post-conversion count — each open 融券 contract scales in place via new `broker_core.scale_slo_contracts` (shares×open_price fee basis preserved; position/contract invariant holds so covers repay cleanly). `last_price` rebases to (prev − cash)/(1 + ratio) so marks/maintenance/equity stay continuous even on suspended ex-dates. Events: dividend_cash / bonus_shares / corporate_action_calendar_gap (record_date not the previous replay trade date — audited, still applied).
- Profile: inert `short_corporate_actions="disabled"` replaced by consumed `corporate_actions="modeled"` (+"disabled" research switch) and `dividend_tax_rate=0.0` (flat research haircut; the real differential 0/10/20% tax settles at sale and is not modeled; old manifests are safe — `_profile_kwargs` filters unknown keys and missing keys take the new defaults, while slots without the parquet load as {} = old behavior).
- Attribution: `compute_return_stats` folds dividend_cash amounts into long_pnl/short_pnl (not trade_count/win_rate) and reports `dividend_cash_received`/`dividend_compensation_paid`; equity/total_return pick the cash up automatically.
- Documented residuals: 配股 not modeled (raw gap remains), pay-date cash lag (credit at ex-date), 送转零碎股 rounding.

Wiring: run_main_ctx_replay(corporate_actions_by_date=) -> SimBroker; backtest tool + replay_benchmark load via new `load_corporate_actions_by_date(replay_dir)` (missing file -> {}). Prompts: new 公司行为 paragraph + `corporate_actions`/`dividend_tax_rate` facts (sample profile in export_prompts updated); template README broker-rules paragraph extended; PROMPTS.md re-exported. Docs: environment_design §3.2 (new 公司行为模型 block + profile row + constraint bullet), parameters_reference §4, data_documentation dividend rows.

Validation: full suite 539 OK (528 baseline after committing the prior session's timeview-text WIP as 8d5ac20; +10 CorporateActionTest incl. equity-continuity across the roll, tax haircut, listdate lock/unlock, short compensation+liability scaling+clean cover, disabled/unheld no-ops, calendar-gap audit, suspended ex-date, loader round-trip, replay attribution ≈ realized+dividend; +1 snapshot builder test covering 实施 filter, revision dedupe, same-day sum, late-announcement drop, manifest meta). Loader->broker parquet round-trip smoke: qty 1000->1500, cash +500, JSON-clean events. `git diff --check` clean. Empirical grounding recorded in-session: dividend dataset complete (11,716 codes, current through 2026-02), e.g. 600900.SH 20250718 ex 0.733 税前.

## 2026-07-07 After-hours fixed-price session tick (feat/afterhours-fixed-price)

Task: model the 2026-07-06 exchange rule revision (沪深北《交易规则（2026年修订）》, effective yesterday) that extended 盘后固定价格交易 to ALL A-shares: matching 15:05–15:30 at the day's official closing price, time priority; the genuinely new capability is deciding AFTER seeing the confirmed close and still executing at it. Companion analysis (same session): the revision's ST band change (main-board ST/*ST 5%→10%) needs NO code change — limits are fully data-driven from `stk_limit` absolute prices (broker.py consumes up_limit/down_limit verbatim, no ratio/ST branch anywhere) and the feed was empirically verified to carry ~5% ratios for the same 150 main-board ST names on 20260703 vs ~10% on 20260706. Fund closing call auction / ChiNext market makers / block-trade confirmation changes don't touch this system (no fund data, no order-book modeling).

Design:
- New knob `afterhours_decision_time` (default "15:05", None = off) threaded config -> `_replay_config_fields` -> manifest -> backtest tool -> engine -> runtime facts whitelist -> prompt facts. Backward-safe consume: `manifest.get("afterhours_decision_time") or None` with NO fallback default, so manifests predating the knob replay without the tick (frozen-eval reproducibility); new experiments get 15:05 from config.
- Tick plan (`_day_tick_plan(afterhours_time=)`): one `is_afterhours` tick after the last real bar, carrying the close-bar group (the confirmed close prints are ctx-visible); the evening off-session grid starts after it (no duplicate minute key even on a 5-minute grid). Always a decision tick; exit day has no ticks, so no after-hours on the liquidation day.
- Execution (`_execute_afterhours_action`): no order-book passage — by 15:05 the day book is already day-end cancelled, and the real session is a single fixed price, so eligible orders settle immediately via `broker.execute(raw_price=close, apply_slippage=False, price_label="afterhours_fixed")` under the full constraint set (suspension conveys the "still suspended at 15:00 -> no after-hours" rule; price limits kept at a limit-locked close as a conservative counterparty-scarcity approximation; T+1/cash/margin/lots unchanged). Per-code board/date eligibility via new `broker_core.afterhours_available` (STAR 20190722, ChiNext 20200824, all remaining incl. BSE 20260706) -> reject `afterhours_not_available`. A limit worse than the close is an invalid submission per the rule -> `afterhours_price_invalid`. `short`/`fin_buy` conservatively unsupported (`afterhours_op_unsupported`) — real availability of leveraged opens through the after-hours session is unverified. `direct_repay` settles as usual; `close` resolves via the extracted `_resolve_close` helper (shared with `_submit_order`). `_is_star_market` moved to `broker_core.is_star_market` (single board-predicate home).
- Docs: environment_design §3.3 orderable-tick bullet + §3.5 new 盘后固定价格 tick block; parameters_reference row; QMT open question #8 (in-client passorder support for 收盘定价申报 unverified — live executor must ignore/reject after-hours orders until verified); data_documentation §4 two rule-revision rows (daily vol/amount includes after-hours fills from 2026-07-06 while minute bars don't -> minute-sum != daily-vol break; ST 5%->10% regime break kills pct_chg≈±5% heuristics, stk_limit is the correct source). Fold prompt: orderable-tick line + a dedicated 盘后定价 bullet; template README updated; PROMPTS.md re-exported.

Validation: full suite 546 OK (539 -> +7 AfterhoursFixedPriceTest: board/date availability matrix incl. BJ, exact-close fill with no slippage and single settled order, knob-off absence + 5-minute-grid uniqueness, ineligible-date reject, limit-validity (invalid vs fills-at-close-not-limit), leveraged-open rejects, T+1 binding at 15:05 then next-day close working). `git diff --check` clean.

## 2026-07-07 Broker fill-model realism batch (fix/broker-fill-realism)

Context: follow-up to the in-session broker defect review. Partial fills were evaluated first with real data: all 272 filled orders across the test2 experiment family joined against their fill days' actual 1-minute bars — median order ¥33k (max ¥50k), median 0.007% of day volume (p99 0.08%), median 2.8% of a median minute bar (p90 15%, p99 42%, max 68%), ZERO fills exceeding a full bar; a 25% participation cap would touch ~5% of fills. Verdict (user-approved): DEFER partial fills — at the ¥1M account scale the full-fill error is second-order vs the flat-slippage assumption; re-trigger conditions recorded (capital ~¥10M+ / concentrated single-name sizing / micro-cap focus), then implement only the participation-cap tier (never queue/impact pseudo-precision on OHLCV).

Fixes landed instead (each probe-verified as a real defect before changing):
1. Limit fills now require strict trade-through (`_limit_fill_price`): buy fills at P only when bar low < P (sell: high > P); a bare touch counts as queued-unfilled. The favorable-open branch (open<=P fills at open) is unchanged. Existing fixtures already traded through (9.78 vs 9.80), so only semantics-specific tests were added.
2. Market orders no longer expire when their code prints no bar in the activation minute (`match_bar` restructure): they keep working and fill at the day's next traded bar, with the day-end sweep as backstop; an unmatched auction order rolls into continuous matching and loses the slippage-free auction treatment (mirrors real 集合竞价未成交委托). Limit orders' `valid_bars` still counts printless minutes (documented time-in-force semantics). New buying-power gap closed: resting market orders now carry a `reserve_price` (decision-time estimate, threaded engine→passorder→WorkingOrder.to_record; `_reservation_price` already consumed the key) so they keep reserving cash/bail while resting.
3. Forced liquidation proceeds now repay 融资 debt (`close_position` forced branch only): release the sold shares' financed attribution then `repay_fin` interest-first FIFO from the proceeds, `debt_repaid(via="forced_close")` event — matching real broker 强平 flow and stopping the phantom interest accrual on idle proceeds. Voluntary 担保品卖出 (only `sell_repay` repays by choice) and the exit-day mandatory liquidation (equity netting) are unchanged.
4. BSE lot rule: `.BJ` codes now accept >=100 shares with 1-share increments (new `broker_core.is_bse_market`); SH/SZ keep the 100-multiple rule, STAR keeps 200+1.
5. Doc-only assumption records (no code change): interest /360 day-count with NO monthly settlement cycle (accrued on contract, paid at repayment/close; risk formulas already include accrued interest — cash-timing difference only), and fixed 5bps slippage independent of order size and spread. Recorded in env_design §3.2 profile table + parameters_reference.

Uptick timing trade-off (user-requested analysis; decision: NO code change): the "2-bar-lag reference" is not a defect but the latency model working as designed — `execution_lag_bars` models decision→exchange transmission, and the exchange checks 申报价 vs the latest trade AT ARRIVAL, which the activation bar's open approximates to within seconds. Relocating the check to decision time would introduce look-back unfaithfulness (no real exchange checks at the trader's decision instant), and the empirically observed rejects (21 in test2 — shorts into 2-minute bounces) reflect genuine latency risk, exaggerated only insofar as minute-granularity lag overstates seconds-level reality — for which the honest lever is `execution_lag_bars=1`, not moving the check. Rationale added to env_design §3.5 融券 bullet; prompt guidance (buffer the limit) already shipped earlier.

Validation: full suite 554 OK (546 -> +8 FillRealismTest: strict-through buy/sell incl. touch-no-fill, market-order resting + reservation while resting + fill at next print with slippage, auction rollover to continuous, limit time-in-force on printless minutes, forced-close repay with remaining-principal arithmetic, voluntary credit_sell non-repay, BSE/SZ lot matrix). `git diff --check` clean; PROMPTS.md re-exported (order-type semantics + lot rules).

## 2026-07-08 Frontend-server deployment of the console (feat/hitl-webui)

Task: stop the ad-hoc 38888 service; rename QMT_documentation to the deployment documentation covering WebUI/frontend/QMT; design and deploy the three-machine architecture; keepalive + friendly startup.

Facts discovered: the stated ~/.ssh/ed_25519.pub does not exist — B's default id_ed25519 already authenticates as root@121.41.5.179; frontend is Debian 12 (1.6G RAM), no nginx/rsync initially (apt-installed nginx 1.22 + rsync); B has autossh but no rsync (sync uses tar-over-ssh).

Architecture (deployment_documentation.md §10): MacBook --ssh -L 8888:127.0.0.1:8080--> frontend sshd; nginx loopback :8080 serves the SPA from /opt/cornerhead/static and proxies /api to 127.0.0.1:38889; the compute hub (CERNET, outbound-only) maintains autossh -R 127.0.0.1:38889:127.0.0.1:38888. Public surface = sshd only; every HTTP listener is loopback; access control is per-key: nologin user `cornerhead` with restrict,port-forwarding and permitlisten=38889 (hub key) / permitopen=8080 (Mac key). Shell access verified refused.

Ops (ops/webui/): nginx-cornerhead.conf (SSE unbuffered, 3600s timeouts, 502/504 -> 503 Chinese JSON "hub offline"), frontend_setup.sh (idempotent provisioning incl. removing the default site), webui_stack.sh (start/stop/status/ensure/sync/install-cron; autossh -M 0 + ServerAlive self-heal; pidfiles under .runtime/webui; logs under logs/webui). Keepalive = autossh for network drops + crontab managed block (*/2 ensure with flock + @reboot) for process crashes; nginx/sshd under systemd on the frontend. Workers are detached: console/tunnel restarts never touch experiments.

Deploy defects found and fixed during bring-up: tar preserved B-side 600/700 modes so nginx got EACCES (sync now extracts --no-same-owner/--no-same-permissions + chmod a+rX); /static/* fell through try_files to index.html (added location /static/ alias; verified content-type application/javascript).

Verification: end-to-end health via frontend loopback OK (nginx -> tunnel -> local API); SPA + assets 200 with correct types; killing the console produced the 503 hub-offline JSON and `ensure` restarted it; cornerhead shell attempt rejected ("account not available"); keepalive cron installed and visible in crontab.

Docs: QMT_documentation.md -> deployment_documentation.md (git mv), retitled 部署文档 with new §10-§12 (network architecture / frontend deployment & access control / keepalive, startup, troubleshooting incl. the Mac ssh-config snippet); QMT chapters §1-§9 kept verbatim; references updated in the four living docs + parameters_reference + CLAUDE.md; logbook history untouched.

## 2026-07-08 Console stability audit + WebUI UTC+8 display (feat/hitl-webui)

Task (post-login): thorough stability audit of the deployed stack, optimizations optional; switch all WebUI-displayed times to UTC+8.

UTC+8: `app.js` gained `fmtTs`/`fmtTsTime` (Intl.DateTimeFormat, timeZone Asia/Shanghai, hour12 false — renders UTC+8 regardless of browser locale) and every raw-ISO display site was converted: experiment-card 创建 time, trace-event head time, analysis-meta 生成于. Countdown math already used Date.parse epoch deltas (timezone-agnostic). Verified all backend timestamps are offset-aware (`utc_now_iso` = `datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()` → `+00:00`); the one naive producer — registry `_created_at`'s mtime fallback — now emits aware-UTC (`fromtimestamp(..., tz=utc)`), since `Date.parse` would interpret a zone-less string in browser-local time. Storage stays UTC ISO. Synced to the frontend via `webui_stack.sh sync`.

Audit method: one Opus subagent audited process lifecycle/resource growth/tunnel edge cases/control-plane consistency across server.py, manager.py, interactive.py, traces.py, webui_stack.sh, nginx conf; in parallel, live inspection of the running stack (pids/fds/RSS, keepalive+console+nginx logs, crontab, sshd -T, disk).

Defects FIXED (each verified as real before changing):
1. SSE thread starvation (agent's top finding, CRITICAL): every route is sync `def` → anyio threadpool (40 tokens, process-wide); `stream_trace` was a sync generator whose `time.sleep(1)` poll ran inside `iterate_in_threadpool`, pinning one token per connected/lingering stream continuously. Mac-sleep + EventSource auto-reconnect accumulates half-open streams over days → pool exhaustion blocks every endpoint incl. control pause/stop. Fix: `stream_trace` is now an async generator (`await asyncio.sleep`); idle streams cost no thread; file reads are small local-disk ops on the event loop; yields suspend at the ASGI sender's pace so replay can't monopolize the loop.
2. Manager lifecycle races (HIGH): control.json mutations were unlocked read-modify-write across server pool threads (two concurrent actions → last-writer-wins, approvals/directives silently lost); `start_worker`'s cap-check-then-Popen could double-spawn one experiment (double-click resume/rerun) → two workers appending one ledger (torn lines crash the worker's own resume, `ExperimentLedger.read` has no tolerance by design); delete-vs-resume TOCTOU. Fix: one `threading.RLock` (`_mutate`) serializing create/start_worker/control/delete via thin public wrappers (mutations are rare + fast; RLock because create/control re-enter start_worker).
3. `write_json_atomic` shared temp name (HIGH): fixed `.name.tmp` meant two concurrent writers interleave `write_text` chunks and `os.replace` a torn JSON into place — worker's gate poll then raises and the experiment is marked failed. Fix: unique temp per write (`.<name>.<pid>.<uuid8>.tmp`) + unlink in finally.
4. PID-reuse liveness (HIGH, two layers): (a) worker: status.json now records `pid_start_ticks` (/proc/<pid>/stat field 22 — kernel start time; (pid,ticks) uniquely identifies a process incarnation) and `status_pid_alive` compares when present (statuses without the field keep old semantics — backward compatible with pre-existing experiments); previously a recycled pid made a dead experiment read ACTIVE forever (blocks resume, eats the 4-cap). (b) stack script: `alive()` now also greps /proc/<pid>/cmdline for run_webui/autossh; previously a post-reboot stale pidfile + recycled pid made `ensure` believe the console was up → silent permanent outage.
5. Frontend sshd ClientAlive (MEDIUM, verified live: `clientaliveinterval 0`): an uncleanly dropped tunnel (CERNET blip, no FIN) left sshd holding 127.0.0.1:38889 until TCP timeout (hours) while autossh's `ExitOnForwardFailure` retries kept colliding. Fix: `98-cornerhead-tunnel.conf` (ClientAliveInterval 30 / CountMax 3) written by frontend_setup.sh, applied + verified via `sshd -T`.

Ops hardening (webui_stack.sh): start/stop/ensure share `ensure.lock` via in-script `flock -w 30` (manual ops can no longer race the cron; external flock removed from the cron line to avoid self-deadlock — cron block reinstalled); `ensure` silent when healthy (keepalive.log had been growing 2 lines/2min of noise); console.log/keepalive.log copy-truncate rotate at 10MB keeping one `.1` generation (uvicorn's append fd stays valid); `start_console` verifies the process survived 1s and reports FAILED + removes the pidfile otherwise (a failed bind no longer leaves a dead pidfile while an orphan holds :38888). During this work an `alive()` call-site regression (`$2` unbound under set -u at start_tunnel's post-check) was caught by the live restart and fixed.

Reported-only optimization opportunities (not implemented; ranked): `trace/stats` re-reads the whole JSONL per 5s poll and SSE reconnects re-stream from offset 0 (idea: incremental stats cache keyed (path,size), client resumes by byte offset); EventSource `onerror` has no backoff (reconnect storm against a down hub); `list_experiments` re-reads every ledger per 5s homepage poll (cache on mtime/size when experiment count grows); strategy-zip temp files (`NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False)` in /tmp) can leak on mid-download disconnect; `.runtime/sandboxes/<id>/run_*` accumulate per rerun (test2: 9.3G across 3 run dirs) and `StatusReporter` re-globs them every 3s heartbeat (retain latest-N + track live run dir explicitly); `AnalysisService._pending` is in-memory only (restart mid-regeneration silently drops the job); sandbox agent-uid-owned files defeat `delete_experiment`'s rmtree(ignore_errors=True) — deletion silently leaves most of the work root (cleanup needs a container pass or driver-side chown-back; orphaned `sandboxes/Test` from the pre-cleanup era was removed via the sandbox image this session); /Data at 94% (609G free) with ~8-17G per experiment (experiments/ + sandbox views) — prune old experiments periodically.

Healthy by design (agent-verified, no action): os.replace atomic publish (reader side), status.json single-writer with zombie detection, SIGCHLD=SIG_IGN no-zombie reaping, detached workers surviving console restarts, registry's defensive ledger reads, orders served only from immutable collected run_ids, path-traversal guards, nginx 503 degradation + SSE unbuffered, autossh self-heal parameters, ledger resume hash-verification, cron flock reentrancy.

Validation: full suite 555 OK (+1 `test_recycled_pid_counts_as_dead`); stack stop/start cycle clean; cron reinstalled; local SSE and frontend-path SSE (nginx→tunnel→API) stream verified live; `sshd -T` shows clientalive 30/3; frontend e2e health ok. Resource check: hub 414G RAM available, console RSS 144M/15 fds; frontend 1.6G RAM ok, disk 20%, nginx logrotate present. Docs: deployment_documentation §12 (locking, rotation, cmdline-verified liveness, ClientAlive, UTC+8 display convention).

## 2026-07-08 Console local access control: Unix-socket bind (feat/hitl-webui line)

Task: restrict WebUI access on the compute hub to user lzp, blocking all other local users; adjust the frontend as needed. Proposal presented with options; user chose hub-only scope (frontend `admin` is their own account; the proposed nftables owner-match rules for the frontend loopback ports were documented but not applied).

Threat verified before designing: the hub has 30 login-capable users (4 logged in at the time); the console's 127.0.0.1:38888 was plain loopback TCP with no auth — any local user could curl the full control API. Toolchain verified: OpenSSH 10.0 on the hub (remote-TCP→local-unix -R supported), uvicorn 0.50.2 `uds=` supported, socket path 47 chars < 108 limit.

Design (kernel-enforced, zero secrets): console binds `.runtime/webui/console.sock`; the run dir is chmod 700 (uvicorn chmods the socket itself to 0666 — the DIRECTORY is the enforcement boundary, same convention as PostgreSQL socket dirs). The autossh reverse forward becomes `-R 127.0.0.1:38889:<socket>` — ssh connects to the socket as lzp on the hub and still binds TCP 38889 on the frontend loopback, so nginx, the Mac path, and the `permitlisten` key restriction are all untouched. A frontend unix-socket hop was considered and rejected: `permitlisten/permitopen` only govern TCP, so allowing streamlocal forwards would weaken the per-key scoping, plus StreamLocalBindUnlink/nginx-socket-permission complexity.

Changes: `run_webui.py` + `server.run()` gained `--uds` (overrides host/port; TCP retained for explicit local debugging only, docstring rewritten); `webui_stack.sh` — SOCK var, chmod 700 on RUN_DIR at init, `rm -f $SOCK` before spawn (stale socket file blocks the bind), tunnel targets the socket, `status` health check via `curl --unix-socket $SOCK http://console/api/health`; PORT var removed from the stack script. Docs: deployment_documentation §10 diagram + §11 new 计算主机侧本地访问控制 paragraph (incl. the warning not to bridge the socket back to TCP with socat) + §12 command table; pipeline_design §5.3.

Self-introduced defect caught during cutover: `start`'s spawned console/autossh inherited grab_lock's fd 9 and held `ensure.lock` indefinitely — every cron `ensure` for ~25 min timed out with "another stack operation holds the lock" (stack itself stayed healthy; confirmed holders via `fuser -v`). Fix: `9>&-` on both spawn commands. Recovery required killing the fd-holding pids directly since `stop` itself couldn't take the lock.

Validation: `ss -ltn` shows no 38888 listener; run dir `drwx------ lzp`; `fuser` shows the lock free after restart; `ensure` silent-clean; frontend end-to-end incl. SSE streaming verified through nginx→tunnel→socket; tests.unit.test_webui_backend + test_interactive_pipeline 41 OK; py_compile clean. Console restarts do not require a tunnel restart (the forward targets the socket by path; the recreated socket is picked up on the next connection).

## 2026-07-08 Frontend sshd designated-keys whitelist (feat/hitl-webui line)

Task: restrict frontend-server SSH access to designated keys only, elegantly.

Audit first (sshd -T + per-user authorized_keys + live sshd sessions/listeners): password/kbd-interactive already off globally, but AuthorizedKeysFile was the default user-writable dotfile (any account — or its compromiser — could self-grant access), no AllowUsers, authenticationmethods any. Designated-key inventory: 8 entries — root 4, admin 2, cornerhead 2 (restricted). Some pre-existing root keys were unrestricted, and the Mac key granted a full root shell. Tightening those broader keys and dropping the Mac key from root were proposed; user chose core-only scope.

Design (all native sshd, one drop-in): `/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-designated-keys.conf` (low sort = first-obtained wins) with `AuthorizedKeysFile /etc/ssh/authorized_keys.d/%u` (key designation becomes root-owned config; every `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` inert), `AllowUsers root admin cornerhead` (all other present/future accounts refused at auth), `AuthenticationMethods publickey`. All 8 keys migrated verbatim (cornerhead's per-key restrict options preserved) into root-owned 0644 files in a 0755 dir (passes StrictModes).

Lockout-proof application: since each Bash call is a discrete SSH connection (no held session), the change was applied with a 120-second revert guard (`nohup sleep 120; [ -f /run/dk-ok ] || rm drop-in && reload`) armed in the same command as the reload; a NEW root connection then touched /run/dk-ok to disarm. Verification: `sshd -T` shows the three directives effective; tunnel restarted successfully with cornerhead's home dotfile temporarily moved aside (proves the central store is the sole source); `sync@` refused (AllowUsers); `frontend_setup.sh` re-run is a no-op reproducing the same state (cornerhead file md5 stable); end-to-end health ok. Mac-side login left for the user to confirm. Caveat documented: cloud-vendor web terminals that inject temp keys into `~/.ssh` will no longer work; VNC rescue (local tty login) is unaffected.

frontend_setup.sh now provisions the central store idempotently: rewrites `authorized_keys.d/cornerhead`, bootstraps root/admin files only if missing (never clobbers), writes the drop-in, `sshd -t` before the final reload. Docs: deployment_documentation §11 new 指定 key 白名单 paragraph.

## 2026-07-08 Frontend WebUI local-user isolation (feat/hitl-webui line)

Task (user): on the frontend server, only the researcher's own account and the vendor console may log in and control the WebUI; all other frontend-local users denied. Login side was already closed by the designated-keys work (AllowUsers root/admin/cornerhead + central key store; vendor VNC rescue uses local tty, unaffected). Control side was the previously deferred gap: any local account could curl loopback 8080 (nginx → full console) or 38889 (raw API) — the console has no auth by design.

Implementation: nftables owner-match on loopback output (`/etc/nftables.conf`, table inet cornerhead, policy accept + targeted rejects; `systemctl enable --now nftables`): dport 8080 allowed only for skuid {root, admin(1000), cornerhead(1001)} — root covers the vendor console shell and stack health checks, cornerhead covers sshd's forwarded connections for the MacBook; dport 38889 allowed only for skuid {root, www-data(33)} — the nginx proxy hop (admin controls via 8080, not the raw API). Everything else gets `reject with tcp reset`. Numeric uids on purpose: a name-resolution failure at boot would abort the whole ruleset load and fail open (policy accept). Managed idempotently by frontend_setup.sh (uids resolved at provisioning time via id -u).

Verification (all live): root→8080 ok, www-data→38889 ok, cornerhead→8080 ok (runuser -u works for the nologin account since it execs without the shell), admin→8080 ok; sync→8080 refused, admin→38889 refused; provisioning re-run reproduces the same 2-reject ruleset and the refusal persists; hub-side end-to-end (nginx→tunnel→unix socket) healthy throughout. Docs: deployment_documentation §11 前端侧本地访问控制 paragraph.

## 2026-07-09 Console feature batch: benchmark equity charts, Barra-lite, run controls (feat/hitl-webui line)

Task (user, 7 items): CSI300 benchmark + daily line charts + drawdown subplot replacing bar charts (ref external_references/example_reports.png); evaluate Barra validation and implement if warranted (dataset + viz); early-terminate → Held-out; rollback to any completed fold; single-ZIP download; inherit Agent Output from an existing experiment at creation; GPU status + per-fold GPU allocation. Keep concise, avoid over-engineering.

Data findings that shaped the design: every replay result window already stores a daily NAV series (`detailed_return.json → equity_curve {YYYYMMDD: value}` + `initial_cash`) — no reconstruction from orders needed; CSI300 daily (000300.SH, 2020–2026.06) is local in `data/raw/index_daily`; `daily_basic` (total/circ_mv, pe/pb/ps, turnover) covers 2010→now for style proxies; `index_member_all` is the Shenwan L1 classification (time-aware in/out dates); NO CSI300 constituent membership locally; no stored daily holdings (reconstructable from orders.parquet fills).

1. Equity charts (`src/autotrade/webui/equity.py` + `equityChart` in app.js): server sends daily simple-return series only — validation sub-windows (valid_000..N, each an independent replay resetting to initial_cash) chain by date; fold test months chain across folds; held-out periods chain; benchmark aligned to the union dates. Client compounds into cumulative curves + running drawdowns. Chart: 2px lines, benchmark dashed neutral ink, filled drawdown subplot (16% opacity, benchmark line-only), per-date invisible hover columns feeding the shared tooltip (date + each series' cum/dd), legend carries final returns (example-report style), ≤7 date ticks with year on change. Held-out takes categorical slot 3 (yellow #eda100 light / #c98500 dark) — validator run on both surfaces: PASS (light aqua/yellow in the documented sub-3:1 relief band, carried by tables/tooltips). Placements: homepage card mini (no drawdown), hero + detail overview (980px full), fold detail validation chart, guarded test chart INSIDE the collapsed audit block (leak-consistent), heldout panel. Old bar/cumulative chart functions removed (singleSeriesBarChart retained — ordersNode still uses it). Endpoint payloads cached client-side keyed on (folds_recorded, heldout_recorded, cum metrics).
2. Barra evaluation → verdict: full Barra NOT warranted (no licensed factor returns/covariances locally; ~20-trading-day fold windows cannot support multi-factor regressions; no CSI300 membership for strict benchmark-relative attribution). Implemented Barra-lite (`src/autotrade/webui/style_analysis.py`): (a) CSI300 single-factor OLS on daily returns → beta, annualized alpha (×244), R², flagged 仅供参考 when n<15; (b) holdings-based style exposure — positions reconstructed from filled orders (signed action map incl. credit/fin actions) and CARRIED FORWARD across all equity-curve dates (first version only sampled fill days: 5/23 → fixed to 23/23), valued at daily_basic close, cross-sectional percentile ranks for circ_mv/pb/turnover_rate → signed position-weighted tilt in [-1,1] vs market median, plus SW L1 industry net weights (top 8) and avg gross long/short; sidecars persisted under `experiments/<id>/hitl/analysis/style/<run>_<prefix>.json` (recompute only if missing). Endpoint `GET /style?run_id&prefix` (run ownership validated); UI card (tiles + centered tilt bars + industry line) in fold validation, guarded test block, and heldout. test2 live: test month β 0.258 / α_ann −7.9% / R² 0.288, size tilt +0.28 (large-cap lean), 11.6 names/day; valid month β 0.337 / R² 0.54.
3. skip_to_heldout: ControlState flag + `skip_now()` checks in the runner loop at the exact points where a session would RUN (recorded sessions still restore to keep the parent chain); breaks both meta and fold paths; held-out then uses the latest frozen parent; manager action validates ≥1 recorded fold and respawns a dead worker; cancel action; control-bar button + badge.
4. rollback_fold (manager `_rollback_to_fold`): drop rule — fold records with session keys AFTER the target in schedule order, meta_learning records of later epochs, ALL heldout records; unparseable ledger lines are never discarded; original ledger backed up as `experiment_ledger.rollback_<stamp>.jsonl` then rewritten from kept RAW lines (byte-preserving); dropped records' frozen dirs (strategy + .models + meta artifacts by id) moved to `strategy_artifacts/_archive/rollback_<stamp>/` (avoids both `_require_no_orphan_artifact` and `_freeze` FileExistsError on re-run); approvals/reruns/gpu_counts for dropped keys + heldout withdrawn; worker respawned (gates at the next fold). Composes with rerun_fold (target becomes latest). UI panel on every recorded non-frontier fold with a danger confirm modal.
5. ZIP-only: per-file listing + `strategy-file` endpoint + registry `strategy_files` removed; single `strategy.zip` (output+models) button remains.
6. inherit_from: creation param (choice; server fills options with experiments having ≥1 recorded fold) → manager `_import_inherited_artifact` copies the source's LATEST fold frozen dirs into `strategy_artifacts/_inherited/strategy_inherited_<src>` (+`.models`), verifies both hashes against the source ledger record (artifact_hash excludes manifest.json so the copy hash is stable), stores `_inherited_artifact` in params (underscore ⇒ ignored by resolve_options), cleans up the half-created experiment dir on failure; runner `_load_inherited_parent` re-verifies via `_verified_artifact` on every start and seeds the first fold's parent (meta epoch_001 then sees has_parent=True — by design).
7. GPU: `GET /api/gpus` (environment.gpu.list_gpus, error-tolerant for CPU hosts); ControlState.gpu_counts {session_key: int} with coercing reader; manager `set_gpu_count` (1..16, empty clears); runner passes `sandbox_gpu_count` → `run_fold` → `_start_sandbox(gpu_count=...)` → `dataclasses.replace(spec, gpu_count=n)`; "auto" selection by free VRAM unchanged; gate UI shows per-GPU free/total table + count select (default = spec default 1).

Validation: full suite 564 OK (+9 tests: skip-after-first-frozen-fold, gpu passthrough [2, None], inherited-seed parent + tamper→RuntimeError, rollback drop/archive/backup/second-rollback-refused, skip/gpu control guards, equity chaining math incl. window reset semantics, inherit import + no-fold refusal); console restarted on the unix socket and all new endpoints exercised live against test2 (equity 20/23/20 pts + 63 benchmark; fold equity; style fresh compute; /api/gpus lists 8 L20s; parameter-schema inherit choices ['', 'test2']); `webui_stack.sh sync` pushed the SPA (frontend grep confirms equityChart/styleCard ×8) and end-to-end health ok. Docs: pipeline_design §5.1/§5.3 (new controls, chart/style/ZIP conventions, Barra verdict), parameters_reference §9 (+ stale console-bind row fixed to the unix socket). User WIP files (broker/engine/prompts/agent_design + the Timeview wording hunk in pipeline_design) deliberately left unstaged.

## 2026-07-09 Per-Step Barra-lite attribution in the backtest loop (feat/hitl-webui line)

Task: user asked for an objective analysis of running Barra-lite at every Step and recording it in Step results to help the Fold Agent interpret backtests; analysis delivered (verdict: benchmark context = high value/near-zero risk; style tilts = moderate value via file; annualized alpha/R² excluded — ~12× noise amplification on 20-day windows; anti-target-chasing framing needed), then approved for implementation.

Key data fact that shaped the design: the replay slot's own `daily.parquet` already carries the full ~5400-code cross-section INCLUDING circ_mv/pb/turnover_rate (33 columns), so per-Step style exposure computes entirely from data the Agent already sees — no host-data reach-around and a clean PIT story. Only the CSI300 series + SW industry table are host-side reads from the raw lake (window dates only), threaded via a new `raw_dir` run-manifest key resolved through the snapshot provider chain (`_snapshot_raw_dir`: CachingSnapshotProvider._provider → RawSnapshotProvider.builder.raw_dir; None for synthetic test providers).

Implementation: module moved webui/style_analysis.py → environment/style_analysis.py and generalized — pure core (`_daily_returns_from_curve`, `_benchmark_regression` [now also emits window benchmark_return], `_positions_from_fills`, `_style_exposures` over injected basics_for/industry_of callables, `_rank_cross_section`) with two entries: `replay_style_analysis(replay_daily, order_records, stats, raw_dir)` for the tool (in-memory, ~2s measured on a real 20-day window, dominated by per-date pandas ranks) and the file-based `style_analysis(experiment_dir, run_id, prefix, raw_dir)` for the console (sidecar-cached as before; webui/server.py + webui/equity.py now import from the environment module; benchmark loader keyed on raw_dir). BacktestTool (mode=="valid" only): writes `style_analysis.json` next to detailed_return.json, attaches it to the step-tree node, and puts the compact block into the summary as `benchmark` = {label, benchmark_return, excess_return, beta, n_days, size_tilt} — deliberately excluding alpha/R². The summary flows to the Agent's tool result, the trace event, manifest backtest_summaries, and (via `_metrics` + "benchmark" key) the fold ledger step summaries. frozen_eval/heldout produce no agent-visible attribution. Everything degrades to None blocks on missing inputs (advisory analytics must never fail a backtest — tools-flow fixtures without style columns pass unchanged).

Prompt: one bullet added to the backtest guidance (benchmark context is how to read absolute returns; diagnostics are descriptive attribution, NOT optimization targets — do not tune for a β or tilt value); PROMPTS.md re-exported. Console: Step history table gains 超额(vs 300) and β columns.

Validation: full suite 568 OK (+4 unit tests: cross-section tilt boundary math [smallest-of-4 → exactly −0.5], benchmark compounding + excess from a fixture raw lake, missing-column degradation, regression math incl. beta-2/r2-1 invariant and empty-raw-dir benchmark); real-data check on test2 valid_003: 沪深300 −2.46% vs strategy → excess +3.17%, β 0.44, size tilt +0.51, 20/20 days, 1.99s first / 1.88s repeat; tools-flow 79 OK (real tool path exercises the hook); console restarted, post-hoc style endpoint (fresh heldout compute: β 0.135, benchmark +1.65%) and equity endpoint verified, SPA synced to the frontend, end-to-end ok. Docs: environment_design §3.7 (逐 Step 归因 paragraph), parameters_reference style row extended. environment_design staged with the user's 4 unrelated WIP hunks excluded.

## 2026-07-09 Raw-coverage audit landing: 14 datasets into Agent decision inputs (feat/hitl-webui line)

Task: implement the recommended tiers from the check.md coverage-audit analysis (76 raw datasets: 48 exposed / 28 not — verified exactly against SnapshotConfig + universe/replay derivations before starting) under strict PIT / zero-leakage.

Mechanics that shaped implementation (verified in code): events/macro/text datasets need NO DatasetContract — row-level `available_at`/`available_at_rule` are stamped at download by `augment_*_frame` as pure functions of source date/time fields (backfill-safe; text has ±(-1,+3)-day plausibility clamps with conservative fallback), so the only exposure switch is the `SnapshotConfig` domain tuple plus an `EVENT/TEXT_DATASET_REFRESH_NODES` mapping when the real landing cron isn't the 23:35 evening node. Visibility is a two-factor gate (row available_at AND node completion) — a wrong node mapping is conservative-only, never leaky. `_build_available_at_domain` fails fast on a missing dataset dir or missing available_at column.

Batch 1 — events board/sentiment cluster (kpl_list, limit_step, limit_cpt_list, limit_list_ths, ths_hot, dc_hot, hm_detail, hm_list) + macro background (repo_daily, us_tycr, us_trycr): tuples + node mappings (`kpl_list`/`limit_step`/`limit_cpt_list` → (evening, cn_preopen_board_backfill_0850) — exactly the datasets that pre-open cron job refreshes; hot lists/hm land evening-only so the default node is already correct). Measured volume: ~6.7k rows/day added (ths_hot 2.3k, dc_hot 3.4k dominate) vs existing events ~10k/day — no caps needed. Real-data build over 2025-11..12: 188,515 event rows across the 7 per-day datasets + hm_list; visibility spot-checks: kpl cutoff 08:50 at 08:56 / prior-evening at 08:40; dc_hot evening ✓. cn_schedule was CUT from the plan during verification: only 7 of 79 month partitions are non-empty (source keeps no history) → zero backtest value; stays raw/cron-only.

Batch 2 — shibor_quote: worse than the audit's "drift risk" — it was absent from BOTH evening tier dataset lists (never refreshed since the one-off backfill; last row 2026-05-25). Added to MACRO_REGIME_DEFAULT_DATASETS, gap backfilled 0520→0708 (+557 rows, revision ledger recorded the append), exposed in macro.

Batch 3 — news with guards: `SnapshotConfig.news_sources` (default cls/wallstreetcn/eastmoney — the high-signal subset of 9 sources totalling ~4k rows/day) and `news_window_months` (default 2 — flash news beyond a couple of months is noise). `_build_text` reads only configured src= subdirs (missing configured source fails fast), clamps the window, and dedups by truncated-body sha1 keeping the earliest visible copy. Real-data run at the full 21-month text window: news adds 63,700 rows (index total 3.88M → +1.6%), zero duplicate hashes, avg body 161 chars (~10MB library shard). TEXT_DATASET_REFRESH_NODES already carried news → (evening, preopen_0855).

Batch 4 — index_daily program: previously ONE hand-backfilled index (000300.SH benchmark, no spec/cron/sentinel, no available_at). Added MACRO_SPECS["index_daily"] (date_year_by_ts_code, available_at=conservative_date_eod like index_global), DEFAULT_CN_INDEX_CODES = 000001.SH/000016.SH/000300.SH/000905.SH/000852.SH/399006.SZ/000688.SH, `--cn-index-code` CLI, dispatch branch, INTEGRATED_DOC_REFS + schedule.json interfaces entry, MACRO_REGIME_DEFAULT_DATASETS (evening cron). Backfilled 2020-01-01..2026-07-09: 49 partitions, 11,039 rows; existing benchmark partitions rewritten WITH available_at (webui equity benchmark reader verified unaffected — column-selective reads). Exposed in macro. Synergy note: the Agent now sees index series to act on the per-Step β feedback added earlier the same day.

Prompt: visibility table rows updated (board pre-open row added; hot lists/index/news slotted into their nodes) + a paragraph framing board/hot/seat data as descriptive sentiment weak-signals (never truth source for fills/tradability/cash/risk) and index series as timing/beta/relative-strength reference; PROMPTS.md re-exported. Docs: data_documentation §1.4/§1.6/§1.7 exposure notes (incl. why limit_list_d and cn_schedule stay out), parameters_reference §1 news knobs + dataset-list note. data_summary needed no change (dataset lists flow from manifest meta).

Validation: full suite 571 OK (+3 tests: default-exposure drift guard incl. cn_schedule exclusion, news source/window/dedup/fail-fast fixture test [one fixture bug found & fixed: f-string hour "010" produced NaT → PIT-filtered], board pre-open cutoff semantics); refresh-node drift guards 15 OK (node names ↔ real cron jobs); real-data domain builds pass for all additions.

Follow-up same day (user review): (1) news guards relaxed on the maximize-agent-data principle — defaults now ALL sources (`news_sources=()` auto-discovers src= partitions; explicit lists stay fail-fast) and the FULL text window (`news_window_months=None`), keeping only body-hash dedup. Measured at full window/all sources: 4.56M rows → 2.60M after dedup (43% duplicates), ~0.4GB library, 45s scan+dedup — acceptable in testing; knobs retained for tightening. The original 2-month clamp was a live-trading intuition — for research the Agent legitimately needs historical flashes across the whole input window (event studies). (2) Per the new "no historical-experiment compatibility" directive (recorded in memory), `status_pid_alive` now REQUIRES a matching `pid_start_ticks` (statuses without it read dead; the optional-check back-compat branch removed); fixtures updated. Full suite re-run 571 OK; PROMPTS.md re-exported (news row simplified).

## 2026-07-09 Metric computation unified: compute-at-replay, read-only web, render-only frontend (feat/hitl-webui line)

Task (user proposal, verified sound): Barra-lite benchmark/industry and the console's post-hoc calculations still read the raw lake, and the frontend compounded curves in JS. Unify to a single computation point at Step/replay completion over snapshot data; the frontend only fetches final results. Strongest argument beyond layering: the raw lake MUTATES (revision ledger exists because sources rewrite history), so post-hoc recomputation could disagree with what the Agent saw — replay-time computation over hash-frozen snapshots is the only reproducible convention.

Feasibility verified up front: replay slots carry macro.parquet with a dataset column (index_daily rows present for new runs since the coverage batch); decision-snapshot universe.parquet carries SW L1 (l1_code/l1_name) as-of the decision day; test AND held-out replays both go through BacktestTool.run(mode="frozen_eval") — one hook covers all modes; run_heldout creates a fresh run+sandbox per period so per-period rollup+collect is write-once; ARTIFACT_TOP_LEVEL includes "results" so root-level rollup files are collected.

Implementation: environment/style_analysis.py rewritten — inputs are frozen run data only (_slot_benchmark from replay macro rows, _snapshot_industry from universe, cross-section from replay daily; all raw readers and their lru_caches deleted); per-window sidecar now persists strategy_daily + benchmark_daily so every downstream consumer is artifact-only; new write_style_rollup aggregates <prefix>_* sidecars into results/style_<prefix>.json (regression re-run on chained series, exposures merged days-weighted via a shared _style_block builder). BacktestTool computes for every mode (test/held-out run post-session, so Agent visibility stays validation-only) and the manifest raw_dir key + _snapshot_raw_dir were removed. ExperimentPipeline._write_style_rollups (per-prefix isolation, errors returned) is called after the fold's test eval and after each held-out period; failures land in the ledger as style_rollup_error instead of stdout. webui/equity.py: strategy from detailed_return.json, benchmark from rollups, cumulative+drawdown computed SERVER-side ({dates,cum,drawdown,final} per series); webui/server.py /style serves the rollup verbatim via read_json; app.js equityChart is a pure renderer (compounding loops deleted). Old experiments without rollups degrade per the no-compat directive: strategy curves render, benchmark line absent, style 404 — verified live on test2.

Full-review pass (user-requested): six finder angles launched; two completed (reuse/simplification, conventions), four died on a session limit and their critical traces were re-run manually in the main loop (removed-symbol sweep, payload-consumer trace across app.js, collect-path verification, manifest raw_dir consumers). 8 findings, 7 fixed: shared _style_block extraction; dead BasicsFor/IndustryOf abstraction + ignored date param removed; no-op benchmark filter dropped; run_series now reuses _chain + SERIES_LABELS hoisted; server endpoint switched to read_json (json import removed); rollup failures isolated per prefix and recorded durably. 1 refuted on verification: the flagged torn-read race on rollup writes cannot occur (console reads only write-once collected copies). Version info swept from code per the user directive (payload schema_version keys, dated comments).

Validation: full suite 572 OK (style tests rewritten to frozen-input fixtures incl. slot-benchmark/universe adapters, degradation, and rollup days-weighted merge + missing-sidecar cases; equity test asserts server-side cum/drawdown math and rollup-sourced benchmark); py_compile + node --check clean; console restarted, live checks on test2 (strategy-only curves, benchmark absent, style 404), SPA synced to the frontend, end-to-end healthy. Docs: environment_design §3.7 (frozen-input口径, all-modes sidecars, rollups; kept the user's expanded metric table), pipeline_design §5.3 single-computation-point principle, parameters_reference style row.

## 2026-07-07 Broker API alignment: remove `valid_bars`

Task: align the Agent-facing broker order API with real QMT semantics. QMT `passorder` does not expose a `valid_bars` / bar-count time-in-force parameter, so stale-order handling should be expressed by strategy logic through `pending()` and `cancel()`, not by a synthetic broker argument.

Changes:
1. Removed `valid_bars` from the Sandbox driver methods `buy/sell/credit_buy/credit_sell/fin_buy/short/cover/sell_repay`; generated broker action records no longer carry the field.
2. Removed `valid_bars` from `SimBroker.passorder` and `WorkingOrder`. Limit orders now rest as day orders until filled, explicitly cancelled, or swept at day end; unfilled limit orders no longer decrement a synthetic bar counter or emit `expired_unfilled`.
3. Updated replay translation, unit fixtures, environment docs, Fold prompt text, exported `PROMPTS.md`, and `configs/agent_output_template/README.md` to document the new pattern: read `pending().age_minutes` inside a substep and call `cancel(order_id, reason=...)` for policy-driven stale-order expiry.

Validation:
- `rg -n "valid_bars|remaining_bars|expired_unfilled|限价窗口" src configs docs tests -g '*.py' -g '*.md' -g '*.json' -g '!docs/logbook/**'` -> no matches.
- `python3 -m py_compile src/autotrade/environment/broker.py src/autotrade/environment/main_ctx_driver.py src/autotrade/environment/main_ctx_engine.py tests/unit/test_main_ctx_replay.py tests/unit/test_broker_engine.py` -> OK.
- `~/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest tests.unit.test_broker_engine tests.unit.test_main_ctx_replay` -> 143 OK.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.

## 2026-07-09 Fifth external audit: verification, ruling, and full remediation

Task: verify GPT's fifth full audit (check.md, 22 findings P0-P2) against the code, rule each item against the three project principles (streamlined architecture / max Agent freedom / realistic simulation), and implement everything adopted.

Branch: `fix/audit5-remediation` (off `fix/broker-fill-realism`), five commits:
`8c3aa92` (batch A replay correctness), `c7f8f12` (batch B validation gates), `b265c37` (batch C run evidence), `c8312ef` (batch D runtime robustness), `8da5125` (batch E ops/doc convergence). Not pushed.

Verification: five parallel read-only subagent sweeps produced file:line evidence per claim before any ruling. Notable verification outcomes: acceptance-gate Day-0 blindness confirmed at `backtest_engine.py:304-317` with the webui path already correct (`style_analysis.daily_returns_from_curve`); 09:25 leak confirmed as first-bar-of-day-regardless-of-time (`main_ctx_engine.py:791`); afterhours BSE eligibility confirmed as documented+tested design (audit claim rejected); universe name leak confirmed empirically (000004.SZ name="国华退" vs name_asof="*ST国华" in a live snapshot; namechange coverage measured 100% at 2024-01-01, enabling as-of-only publication); macro/event domain audit "error" statuses confirmed as calibration noise (index_daily expected_files=7 vs 49 backfill partitions), justifying warn-only gating for research domains.

Rulings (rejected items): #6 afterhours BSE (by design per 2026-07-06 rule revision; B-shares/malformed codes unreachable in data and fail-safe on missing_price); #19 dividend pay_date lag (documented accepted residual, second-order vs 5bps slippage; re-trigger criteria stand); #22 host RPC state layer (in-process io.open already blocked by driver monkeypatch; persistent driver process makes in-memory globals bypass ANY file/RPC protocol; no PIT/cross-run leakage — documented the enforceability boundary in env docs instead); #12d partial-fill DEALs (standing empirical deferral); #4's three replay modes, #13's hot-read hash verification, #8's killable NL worker processes (lightweight equivalents implemented instead).

Key implementation notes beyond the concise log:
- Two-phase marks live in `SimBroker.match_bar` (open before matching for admission, close after for ctx.account); EOD interest/forced-close schedule unchanged. Test sizes the bail-balance rejection via a collateral gap-down (credit cash 25k, 1000x20 collateral, open 6.0 -> fin_buy 1000x10 rejected).
- Single-price matching applies to close-auction orders AND any synthetic bar (flag column `synthetic=True` from `_synthetic_daily_minutes`); day range stays visible in ctx.bars at 15:00 (legitimately known post-close).
- `positions_eod.parquet` written per replay from `SimBroker.positions_eod_records()` (replace-by-date; engine refreshes after exit-day close_all); `style_analysis` consumes it and `_positions_from_fills`/`_group_fills`/`_ACTION_SIGN` were deleted.
- Reporting: `build_experiment_report(ledger, out)` reads each record's frozen `benchmark` block; `_attach_benchmark_returns`/`_infer_raw_dir`/`_load_benchmark_frame`/`_benchmark_period_return` deleted along with `--benchmark-code/--benchmark-raw-dir/--no-benchmark`.
- DuckDB retrieval: `TextRetriever` title/code match via registered-frame `regexp_matches`, body grep via `read_parquet([shards]) ... LIMIT` with snippet substr in-query; snippet cache replaces the resident `_bodies` dict. Pattern gate `_validate_pattern` (256 chars, RE2 compile check via duckdb). Verified duckdb 1.1.3 supports list-param `read_parquet(?)` and rejects backrefs.
- NL deadline: `MainPolicyRunner._request` sets `nl_service.deadline_at = deadline`; `_StrategyNLService.run` fails exhausted requests pre-provider; `NLSubAgentEngine._call` clamps per-round timeout to remaining and passes `max_retries=0` when clamped (new optional param on `LLMProxy.complete_tools`).
- flock migration removed `pid_is_alive`/`lock_is_stale`/`DEFAULT_LOCK_STALE_SECONDS` and the `default_lock_stale_seconds` config key; lock file is never unlinked (unlink races a concurrent opener onto a dead inode).
- Image identity: `resolve_image_identity()` (docker image inspect, fail-fast) recorded in `allocation_record` and `sandbox_image_update`; base image pinned to `python:3.11-slim@sha256:b27df584...`; DuckDB CLI zip pinned to sha256 `efd0fccd...` (fetched and hashed from the official release).
- PARAM_DEFAULTS single-sourcing overlays dataclass defaults after the literal dict; `run_experiment.build_parser()` extracted; `DefaultsDriftTest` pins CLI<->PARAM_DEFAULTS<->dataclasses.

Resource checks: pre-run `free -h` showed ~301Gi available; work was CPU-light (unit tests only, ~100s per full-suite run), no GPU workload.

Validation: full suite grew 572 -> 597 tests, all OK after each batch; `git diff --check` clean per commit; PROMPTS.md re-exported in batches A/B/D/E; living docs updated in the same commits (environment_design §1.4/§3.x/§7, data_documentation §3.1 gates + sidecar contract, pipeline_design §2.2/§4.1/§4.2, deployment_documentation webui extra, parameters_reference §2/§3/§7/§8).

## 2026-07-10 Architecture cleanup: responsibility re-homing and boundary splits

Task: full architecture review of the core code (four parallel Opus subagent sweeps over environment core / tushare data layer / pipelines+agent / tools+nl+webui, rulings by the main agent), then implement every adopted refactor. Branch `refactor/architecture-cleanup` (stacked on fix/audit5-remediation), commits `7c32a2a`, `f9255bd`, `218136a`, `b701d13` + docs/logbook commit. Not pushed.

Review verdict recorded up front: package layering was already clean (zero environment->pipelines/webui imports); the real defects were misplaced responsibilities in four >800-line files, one cross-module private-symbol import web (backtest_engine), one safety-relevant duplicated decision rule (close-op), and four hand-copied domain auditors with real drift.

New modules: environment/replay_market.py, environment/replay_stats.py, environment/sandbox_images.py, nl/service.py, nl/retrieval.py, agent/experiment_facts.py, llm/conversation_log.py, pipelines/hitl_state.py, webui/analysis.py, webui/prompt_preview.py. Deleted: environment/backtest_engine.py.

Key mechanics worth remembering:
- backtest_engine helpers were renamed public on the move (minute_sort, empty_minute_rows, minute_rows_with_daily_fallback) - the private-import smell was the point of the split.
- ExperimentPipeline._finalize_run(sandbox, run_id, record_builder, raise_after) now owns the collect -> ALWAYS ledger.append -> re-raise ordering for all three run kinds; record builders are closures receiving (collected, collect_error). Fold deliberately records (not raises) test_eval_error; meta raises collect first then image error via raise_after.
- maybe_rebuild_sandbox_image returns (result, active_spec); the pipeline swaps self._active_sandbox_spec only on success. The vestigial run_id parameter was dropped.
- The driver no longer computes close op hints (main_ctx_driver._close_op_type deleted); engine _pending_view resolves close actions through _resolve_close + account_op_for_action. Driver change => sandbox image rebuilt (digest-pinned base + DuckDB sha check verified in the same build).
- hitl_state split: webui imports only the protocol module; interactive.py keeps the worker. write_json_atomic canonical in runtime.py (unique pid+uuid temp name).
- Audit consolidation verified by running macro / event-flow / board-trading CLI audits and a text-completeness harness against the real lake with --end-date 20260709 before and after: all four payloads byte-identical modulo created_at (scratch baseline under the session scratchpad audit_baseline/). Net -62 lines in audit.py (less than the ~-330 projected: the per-domain details-key contract had to be preserved verbatim), but the four families now share one implementation so per-domain drift is structurally impossible.
- Explicitly rejected refactors are listed in LOGBOOK to prevent relitigating: no unified tool loop, no run-scaffold mega-builder, no reporting/search/app.js/driver splits, broker matching split deferred until the fill model grows.

Resource checks: CPU-only test runs (~100 s per full suite); docker image rebuild logged under logs/sandbox_image_rebuild_*.log.

Validation: full suite 596 OK after every batch; PROMPTS.md export byte-identical; git diff --check clean; four-domain audit output parity on real data; sandbox image rebuilt from the digest-pinned base with the SHA-256-verified DuckDB CLI.

## 2026-07-10 Step-tree branching rollback: agent tool + console panel

Task: make the step tree an actual search tree. Storage was already tree-shaped (full per-node output/models snapshots, parent pointers, set_position), but nothing could restore a snapshot or reposition, so lineage was linear by construction. Branch `feat/step-tree-rollback` (stacked on refactor/architecture-cleanup after the user's doc-reconcile commit 6097070), commits `dfa9400` (agent side) + `d3a2e2f` (console side) + logbook commit. Not pushed.

Agent side (dfa9400):
- New trusted tool `environment/tools/step_rollback.py`: restores output/ (+ models/ unless include_models=false) from steps/<node_id>/, verifies restored hashes against the node record (corruption fail-fast), then StepTree.set_position(node_id). Registered in runner spec/handler maps (lock-step test covers it). Rejections: unknown node (retry_hint points at tree.txt), failed nodes (no snapshot), tree disabled, write-locked.
- Node layout: steps/<node_id>/{output/,models/} + attachments at node root. Old flat layout silently merged an output-level models/ dir into model artifacts and let attachments shadow same-named output files; record_step now also rejects attachment paths under output/ or models/.
- backtest.py attaches orders.parquet (when present) next to detailed_return.json/style_analysis.json — node-level full source + detailed results per user requirement.
- StepTree.save() writes tree.json/tree.txt via tmp+rename (new inode). Rationale: link_copytree hardlinks the fold-level tree to the experiment-level tree; Path.write_text truncates the shared inode in place, so an aborted fold could leave the experiment tree referencing never-copied-back node dirs.
- Prompt STEP_TREE_SECTION documents the layout, the tool, the constraint caveat (diff budget still vs fold parent), and the wrap-up recipe (restore a validated node then finish_fold). PROMPTS.md re-exported.

Console side (d3a2e2f):
- webui/steps.py: step_tree_view() recomputes agent_visible_ref for every fold id from schedule.json + ledger and maps fold_ref_* back to real fold ids; frozen markers via (artifact_hash, model_hash) match against latest fold records; node_export_dir() validates node ids for the zip route (no raw path joins).
- server.py: GET /api/experiments/{id}/steps and GET .../steps/{node_id}/source.zip (zips the whole node dir: output/, models/, detailed_return.json, style_analysis.json, orders.parquet).
- hitl_state.ControlState gains parent_overrides {session_key: node_id}; manager action set_parent_override validates fold session + restorable node, empty clears; rollback_fold drops overrides of dropped sessions.
- interactive.py: at fold session start, control.parent_overrides.get(key) -> _parent_from_step_node() builds a hash-verified FrozenArtifact from the node snapshot (artifact_id stepnode_<node_id>, auditable in the fold ledger record); status.json carries parent_override for visibility. Mid-session forced rollback deliberately rejected (agent context/disk divergence).
- app.js/style.css: step-tree panel on the detail page (also read-only experiments): DOM tree with hover metric cards, failed nodes dimmed/struck, current-position + frozen badges, node modal with metrics table, source.zip download, and the parent-override modal (target fold selector; set / set+rerun / clear).

Validation: full suite 605 OK (9 new tests across test_tools_flow / test_step_tree / test_interactive_pipeline / test_webui_backend); node --check on app.js; live smoke test on the deployed console after webui_stack.sh sync + restart: /api/experiments/test2/steps de-opaques fold_202512, old-layout test2 nodes degrade to has_snapshot=false (no download/rollback affordance) per the testing-phase no-compat directive; frontend end-to-end ok. No sandbox image rebuild needed (driver untouched).

Doc deltas (environment_design tool table + steps layout, agent_design workflow bullets, pipeline_design §5 step-level rollback, parameters_reference §9 row) are in the working tree, left uncommitted to ride with the researcher's in-flight documentation reconciliation pass.

## 2026-07-10 Step-tree GPT audit: verification rulings and remediation

Task: verify 5 GPT audit claims against the step-tree rollback feature; fix confirmed defects. Branch feat/step-tree-rollback, commit feb8739. Full suite 607 OK.

Claim 1 (CONFIRMED, pre-dated this feature): node id = epoch__fold_ref__result_name, but result_name is run-local (valid_000 restarts every run). Any re-execution of the same fold (rerun_fold since 2026-07-09, or a fresh session after rollback_fold) collided on the first complete validation: StepTree.record_step raised ValueError AFTER append_backtest_summary, the runner's broad except returned 'internal tool failure' to the Agent for every successful validation, and no nodes were recorded. Fix: record_step takes run_id (backtest passes manifest run_id, agent-visible and calendar-free); node id is now epoch__fold_ref__run__result. Regression tests: step_tree unit (same result different runs OK, same run collides) + tools_flow rerun simulation (second sandbox, link_copytree'd tree, run_id updated -> ok, two nodes, rerun chains onto previous frontier).

Claim 2 (CONFIRMED leak): rollback_fold edits only ledger + frozen dirs; experiments/<id>/steps kept the dropped folds' nodes - full strategy snapshots plus validation metrics from periods FUTURE relative to the re-run frontier - and _install_step_tree hardlinks the tree into the next fold's sandbox. Fix: _prune_step_tree in the rollback path (drop nodes of dropped fold sessions by recomputed fold_ref + transitive descendants; move node dirs and a tree.json backup into the same rollback_<stamp> archive; null current_node_id if pruned - next fold start repositions by parent hash). Plus past-only guard in _validate_parent_override: schedule-order comparison rejects overriding an earlier session with a later session's node (own session allowed - the rerun-from-node use case). Tests extended in test_webui_backend (rollback asserts pruned_step_nodes/tree contents/archive layout; override test asserts 400 with 泄漏 message and own-session 200).

Claim 3 (REJECTED): include_models=false does not corrupt lineage - every node records its own artifact AND model hashes at validation time; the parent pointer is search lineage; the code/params pairing warning is already in the tool schema and result note. Keeping the flag preserves a real research workflow (revert code, keep trained weights).

Claim 4 (REJECTED): restore atomicity would be a special case - copy_artifact is delete-then-copy across the whole pipeline (parent installs, freeze). Failure recovery = re-invoke the tool (all validated states live in the read-only tree); set_position runs only after both hash verifications. Documented in the tool docstring instead of adding a transactional swap.

Claim 5: pipeline_design §5 and parameters_reference §9 updated (tree pruning on rollback, past-only override constraint); doc changes remain in the working tree alongside the researcher's in-flight documentation pass.

## 2026-07-10 Step-tree panel redesign (hover fix, legacy downloads, scale)

Task: user reported (a) hover popups showing broken/incorrect content and (b) downloads unavailable on historical nodes; mid-task addendum: design for potentially large experiment/step volumes with full data visibility. Branch feat/step-tree-rollback, commit 7479d4c. Full suite 608 OK.

Root causes: (a) per-row hover cards were position:absolute children INSIDE .step-tree { overflow-y:auto; max-height:420px } - absolutely positioned descendants are clipped by the scroll container, so cards rendered truncated or invisible and flickered while moving across rows; rows also showed bare HH:MM for days-old nodes. (b) pre-2026-07-10 flat-layout node dirs (all of test2, the only real experiment) have main.py at the node root, so has_snapshot=(node/output/).is_dir() was false and rows were fully inert - no modal, no download, even though the source files exist on disk.

Fixes/redesign:
- webui/steps.py node_layout(steps_root, node_id) -> split|flat|None; has_snapshot = any layout, new 'restorable' field = split only; node_export_dir accepts both (legacy zipped verbatim). manager._validate_parent_override additionally requires split (error 旧格式节点仅支持查看与下载); worker _parent_from_step_node was already split-only.
- app.js: one shared #step-tip on document.body (position:fixed, viewport-clamped via getBoundingClientRect, flips above when near the bottom, pointer-events:none, hidden on scroll/click/toggle); rows use fmtTs (full date+time); collapsible subtrees with per-node toggle and expand/collapse-all (collapsed rows show +N chip); search filter over node_id/fold/result/frozen_for keeping ancestor chains for lineage context, with hit counter and filter-overrides-collapse; inline row actions (download anchor with stopPropagation, rollback button when restorable && hitl); modal gains model hash + frozen_for rows and legacy/failed hints. CSS: bordered 62vh scroll area, chips, toggle, tip styles; @media (hover:none) hides the tip; narrow screens drop the time column.
- Tests: test_legacy_flat_nodes_stay_downloadable_but_not_restorable (fixture rewrites a node dir to the flat layout; asserts view flags, zip contains root main.py, override 400 with 旧格式).

Live verification after webui_stack.sh sync + restart: /api/experiments/test2/steps shows all three nodes has_snapshot=true/restorable=false, valid_007 frozen_for=['epoch_001/fold_202512'] (hash matching confirmed working); source.zip for valid_007 returns 17.6KB with README/candidate/main/trading/nl_prompt/detailed_return.

## 2026-07-10 Console sweep: launching state, equity correctness, live-view fixes

Task: (1) analyze the create->running dead window and add a launching indicator if warranted; (2) inspect every WebUI component and fix what's broken/optimizable. Branch feat/step-tree-rollback, commit 0a77e02. Full suite 611 OK; deployed (static sync + console restart, end-to-end ok).

Launching state: manager writes an atomic {state:"launching", launched_at, +progress fields} stub to status.json strictly BEFORE Popen (no concurrent writer exists yet, so the worker remains the single steady-state writer); registry.experiment_state keeps "launching" while fresh and degrades stale stubs (>LAUNCH_GRACE_SECONDS=180) to interrupted; launching counts toward MAX_RUNNING_EXPERIMENTS and blocks start_worker/resume/delete during the import window (closing a real double-spawn hole); UI: STATE_LABELS launching=启动中 (pulsing badge), starting relabelled 初始化. Trade-off: one file write per spawn vs seconds of misleading "created" - implemented.

Inspection (read-only Opus agent exercised every endpoint against test2 + code cross-check). Confirmed and fixed:
- equity.py blended ALL valid_* windows of a run (overlapping attempts by different strategy versions): test2 curve -3.92% vs ledger +0.98%. run_series(window=...) + fold_valid_window(record) (selected step's validation_result_ref, fallback last validated step; None -> empty, never blend). Both payload builders updated; test fixture rewritten to the corrected contract (selected window only).
- app.js renderDetailPage assigned detailView AFTER building panels: heldoutPanel's equity chart + style card silently skipped on first render, and a stale detailView could chart the previous experiment. Assign before building.
- traces.trace_stats re-read the whole file per 5s poll: incremental per-path cache (offset of last complete line + running aggregates, additive; truncation detected by size shrink -> rescan; LRU-ish cap 32). Parity-verified cold vs warm on the real 2MB trace.
- SSE stream_trace: emits retry:5000 and a page-boundary "id: <next_offset>" on the last event of each page; server honors Last-Event-ID on reconnect (max with explicit offset). Reconnects resume near the tail (at most one page re-sent, never lost) instead of restreaming from 0.
- analysisPanel setTimeout chains now registered in liveTimers (route() cancels them; previously they polled a detached DOM forever while pending).
- Both zip endpoints unlink the NamedTemporaryFile if archive construction raises (BackgroundTask only covers the happy path).
- styleCard: /style 404 (rollup never persisted) renders as 该运行未落盘风格归因数据 instead of 加载失败.

Deferred (rationale in commit): list_experiments memoization; persistent analysis-pending marker.

Verified working per the inspection: health/parameter-schema/gpus, experiments list/detail/status, fold detail + strategy.zip, steps + node zip, orders(+csv incl. traversal guard), analysis GET, trace page/download, prompt-preview POST, timezone rendering, dark-theme vars, no 500s on any GET.

## 2026-07-10 test5 failure remediation, console details, agent-loop audit

Commit fe9ec20 on feat/step-tree-rollback. Full suite 611 OK; console synced+restarted.

test5 diagnosis: worker died in _accept_or_fallback -> copy_artifact into finish_fold-locked output/ (PermissionError unlink nl_prompt.md). Fix: _chmod_tree(0o666/0o777) on output+models before the fallback restore (experiment.py); covers host-lock and Docker-subuid ownership. Second bug: interactive.py used ANALYSIS_DIR_NAME without importing it after the hitl_state split (analysis hook path untested) - import added.

Console: themed .input class (step-tree filter was white in dark mode - class simply didn't exist); phase-head selected inset stroke removed; orders decision_time ISO strings (transfers) render via fmtTsTime; gpuAllocationRow rewritten - panel styling, per-GPU memory bar + utilization_pct + temperature_c (gpu.py nvidia-smi query extended, _int_or_none for [N/A]), 15s live refresh registered in liveTimers, timestamp chip. Answer to the user's question: the panel previously detected GPUs ONCE per render; now it re-detects periodically while open.

Agent decision-process audit (read-only Opus agent over test2/test5 traces, manifests, ledgers, orders, strategy code) - system fixes: (F1) probe P&L not-comparable note in backtest summaries (probe_note) + spec wording; (F2) max_untracked_substep_wall_s 0.05->0.25 + actionable error (threshold named); regression fixture sleeps 0.4s; (F3) prompt: absolute imports mandatory (main.py loaded as non-package), replay-visible data must live in output|models (workspace not mounted in replay); (F5) freeze selection = latest complete validation matching current hash (reverts/step_rollback need no redundant re-validation; last-run-only was also a latent last-not-best trap). Recorded-not-changed: F6 per-tick fail-fast (design principle), F7 ctx.nl unused in both real runs (watch), F8 margin_secs collateral proxy (needs real data), F4 analysis length-stop already mitigated by the retry escalation.

Economics (audit): fold wall ~90-100min dominated by ~16min full validations; explore phase ~250K tokens/fold flat; both experiments overfit valid->test (2.92->-1.23, 0.72->-0.74).

## 2026-07-11 Frontend appeared stale: nginx static caching fix

Report: no UI changes visible despite repeated syncs. Verified /opt/cornerhead/static/{app.js,style.css,index.html} md5-identical to the repo (sync fine). Root cause: the cornerhead nginx site served /static/ and / with no Cache-Control, so browsers heuristically cached app.js/style.css (and index.html) without revalidating. Fix: add_header Cache-Control "no-cache" on both locations (ETag revalidation keeps repeats 304-cheap); nginx -t + reload; verified both paths now emit the header. Repo template ops/webui/nginx-cornerhead.conf updated to match; original config backed up on the frontend (/root/cornerhead.nginx.bak.<ts>). One hard refresh needed client-side to escape the already-cached copy.

## 2026-07-11 Console detail follow-ups (transfer time, dark scrollbars, GPU interval)

fmtOrderCell now renders ISO decision_time (transfers) as plain Shanghai HH:MM matching engine-written order times (the row already has a date column). Dark-mode "white background" in the orders scroll box was the UA scrollbar: style.css had no color-scheme declaration, so browsers painted light scrollbar tracks; added color-scheme light/dark on the theme roots plus an explicit var(--panel) background on .orders-table-box. GPU allocation panel re-detection interval 15s -> 60s. Static synced (no-cache headers make it visible on a normal reload).
