## 2026-07-14 `lap-test16` Q1 completion and focused UI/diagnostic follow-up

- Q1 full Valid completed 60 strategy days in 1230.1s with 377 orders / 156 fills / zero strategy rejects. Phase split: strategy compute 473.9s, IPC 284.7s, Timeview 161.6s, host replay overhead 277.9s, Broker 31.4s. Minute prefetch wait was 0.001s.
- Frozen Q1 test returned +1.93%, Sharpe 0.85, max drawdown 2.90%, and +3.14% excess versus CSI 300, with 276 orders and no state mutation during test. The pending stop request took effect after the advisory Fold analysis; Meta + Q1 are recorded and Q2 did not start.
- UI transition root cause: the Fold ledger appears before post-Fold analysis finishes, so `running=true` and `done=true` briefly render live Trace immediately above the result card. The result gets `section-gap` only when a previous sibling exists; the settled first-card layout is unchanged.
- Acceptance warnings are generated as percentage/two-decimal strings; the browser also formats old raw numeric warning strings without mutating historical ledgers.
- Modification-check AST now emits a non-blocking `blind_auction_price_lookup` advisory when a 09:15/09:25 `ctx.cur_time` branch directly reads `ctx.price()` or calls a helper that does. A zero-order replay links it to a fixed repair hint; acceptance remains unchanged.
- Validation: 113 Pipeline config, ToolFlow and WebUI backend tests passed in 25.0s; JS syntax, Python compilation and `git diff --check` passed. The live UDS serves the updated JS. The `quant` environment currently has no pytest module, so validation used the repository's unittest-compatible test suite without installing dependencies.


## 2026-07-14 Strategy-owned frozen-feature reuse

- `lap-test16` spent 473.9 of 1230.1 seconds in strategy compute. The accepted strategy repeatedly processed a large event history even though its frozen research baseline did not change; minute prefetch wait remained about 0.001 seconds, so loader concurrency was not the target.
- The default strategy now reads `snapshot_dir/daily.parquet` once per replay, keys the module cache by the immutable snapshot path, and projects only `ts_code`. The advanced rolling example also projects its required column.
- Prompt, template README and living Agent/Environment docs now state that `ctx.asof_version` is global and can advance with minute data. Heavy rolling daily/event work should run at a fixed research time and cache by the date/data node or another strategy-owned dependency key, rather than invalidating on every global version bump.
- This is guidance plus a reusable example, not an Environment cache, decision-frequency fence or new API. Agent-authored strategy semantics remain under Agent control.
- Validation: Artifact and ToolFlow suites passed 119 tests in 29.642 seconds. Prompt export was regenerated and idempotent; Python compilation and `git diff --check` passed. A regression test verifies two frozen screens cause one projected parquet read.


## 2026-07-15 Audit remediation batch (check.md implementation)

- Implemented the consolidated three-round audit list (`check.md`) on branch `fix/audit-remediation` in nine commits (36a8ac4, bcca58c, 7243b32, 736b6ee, fd79b69, 64475a5, 3f76167, 3cd0e20, bc9ec93 + this docs/logbook commit).
- Execution correctness: reduce-side orders accept whole lots or the one-shot odd tail (bonus-share positions are exitable; `broker_core.reduce_amount_reject`); the after-hours path validates amounts like every other submission path; delayed `transfer`/`direct_repay` bypass the exchange orderable-window triple check and `direct_repay` no longer needs a fill bar; `ctx.substep` rejects non-finite budgets; annualization unified on 244 days.
- PIT correctness: `_apply_screen` only screens A-share-coded rows (index/concept datasets survive screening); replay slots take the fold decision anchor as the availability floor (weekend/holiday rows enter at their true pre-open node; `SNAPSHOT_CACHE_FORMAT_VERSION`=3); `adj_factor` duplicate keys rejected; NaT-dropped rows counted into domain meta; Timeview warns when replay columns would be dropped by the frozen schema.
- Freeze/recovery integrity: acceptance matches strategy+model hashes; non-finish_fold exits quiesce agent processes and read-lock artifacts before hashing; `_freeze` asserts post-copy hashes (`FrozenArtifactMutatedError`); frozen-test hash changes record `state_changed_during_test=true` and terminate after the ledger persists; resume fails fast on failed derived-image builds and verifies recorded image ids; orphan checks cover rerun-tagged and meta ids; worker revalidates rerun-latest and parent-override chronology (`assert_node_not_from_later_fold` shared with the console).
- Console safety: `POST /api/experiments` rejects HIDDEN_KEYS and forces manager-owned roots; style/orders/CSV routes and result-name enumeration answer like missing artifacts for `test/heldout` prefixes until reveal.
- QMT/live ops: bridge and monitor dedup keys day-scoped with state pruning; strict JSON (NaN/Infinity rejected, boolean-typed gates, finite price/volume/caps); per-order intent→terminal journal; scp pulls to temp files with return-code+JSON validation; stale-snapshot (180s) and repeated-pull-failure alerts with recovery cards; fills acked only on delivered notifications; `qmt_monitor.sh` checks /proc cmdline; one shared .env parser.
- Agent session: `_trim` no longer rewrites the cacheable prefix when nothing drops; malformed compaction responses raise into the safe-failure path; `finish_status=llm_call_limit` distinguishes call-budget exhaustion; LLM observations drop the static `tool_spec` echo (~47KB/session) while traces keep it; Explore usage aggregated into the session token summary; facts include `intraday_decision_minutes`, auction knobs and `timeview_enabled`; `modification_check` reports `complete_validation_covered`/`covered_by`.
- Data layer: manual `tushare_download.py` runs against production `data/raw` take the cron flock non-blocking (`TUSHARE_UPDATE_LOCK_HELD` marks cron children); range_month text retention keys on the date column; event/board shrink guard (`blocked_shrink_overwrite` at >20 keys and >20%); repair rewrites refresh sidecar hashes; `share_float_complete` uses repo-relative `source_file`, declared-business-key dedup and key-coverage rebuild guard; partial-window minute `--force` merges instead of replacing the year file; dead `news_src_month`/event-`day` strategies removed.
- Performance: per-tick bars ship as base64 little-endian float64 buffers (NaN=None); stdlib driver decodes via `array.frombytes`. Round-trip benchmark at 4,816 codes: 23.32→2.21 ms/tick (x10.5), ≈390 s per 61-day replay across valid/test_000/held-out. First-fold prefetch submits at the meta GATE (overlaps researcher approval); fundamental-events sidecar audit reads each distinct file once.
- Deferred with recorded triggers (annotated in check.md): quality-report `changed_datasets` precise gating; SSE-holiday-aware Timeview nodes (doc wording corrected to "optimistic" now); full resolved-config persistence in params.json (must land before the next PARAM_DEFAULTS change).
- Resources: host had 407 GiB available before the full suite; no GPU work involved.
- Validation: full `~/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest discover -t . -s tests` -> 826 tests OK (~104 s); real Docker E2E (`DockerizedFoldE2ETest` + `DockerSandboxE2ETest`) -> 2 OK against the rebuilt image (in-image driver md5 94cf42b96b2d99b5ed48cb2001eb14df == source); `docker build -t autotrade-sandbox:latest -f ops/docker/sandbox.Dockerfile .` succeeded; PROMPTS.md re-exported twice; `git diff --check` clean on every commit.


## 2026-07-15 lap-test17 stk_auction crash fix and compliance re-audit

- **Crash**: `lap-test17` aborted at ~11:35 UTC while the pre-gate prefetch built `fold_2025Q3`'s test_replay (20250701..20250930): `snapshot.py::_build_auction` raised `ValueError: stk_auction has invalid quantity combinations` on rows with price/vol/amount all NaN (five `.BJ` codes shown; 274 rows on 20250818).
- **Root cause (first-hand + Opus case study)**: the 2025-08 BSE ticker renumbering window. On 20250818–20 the source lists every BJ auction row as all-NaN — retired 43/83/87xxxx aliases (absent from daily) AND 29 trading 920xxx codes (daily vol>0) — plus ordinary suspended SH/SZ names (suspend_d `S`); 20250908 has one suspended-stock row. Whole lake: 4 partitions, 821 rows. This is a source "no auction observation" shape, not corruption; capture-side validation only guards the same-day live capture, and these partitions came from the 2026-07-11 bulk backfill.
- **Fix**: `_build_auction` drops all-three-NaN rows and counts them as `price_quality.unobserved_rows_dropped`; partial-NaN/negative/other inconsistent combinations still fail the build; raw partitions (hardlinked) untouched. Data doc §1.3 documents the contract. Regression tests: unobserved rows dropped+counted; partial-NaN still raises. Real-lake rebuild over 20250815–20250910: 103,554 rows kept, 821 dropped, zero crash codes remaining.
- **Resume**: ledger-based relaunch of the lap-test17 worker resumes at fold_2025Q3; Meta/Q1/Q2 records and frozen artifacts intact; snapshot_cache reusable (only Q3 test_replay rebuilds); the fix changes no cache key.
- **Case-study observations (Opus)**: trim fix confirmed effective — cache_hit_ratio Q1 91.9% / Q2 84.2% (meta 71.9%), no compaction pressure; pre-gate prefetch fired as designed (and surfaced the crash before any Q3 agent spend); Q1 1 transient backtest error (recovered). Follow-up applied: the new Timeview schema-drop warning now fires once per domain and exempts the `available_at_rule` gating annotation (was 469 warnings per replay). Known noise left as-is: 99 pandas concat FutureWarnings (deprecation only).
- **Compliance re-audit (Opus, 10 commits × three principles + datasets/docs sweeps)**: batch judged substantially compliant; 8/10 commits clean. Three findings fixed in this session: (1) HIGH `write_share_float_union` NameError on the non-shrinking success path (`existing_rows` binding restored; success-path regression added); (2) MEDIUM QMT bridge `processed_payloads` now day-scoped (`YYYYMMDD:payload_id`) and pruned with the other dedup maps (stale payloads still rejected by the `trade_date==today` gate); (3) LOW deployment doc §2.2/§3 "double-confirm" wording reconciled to the implemented triple gate.
- **Validation**: full `~/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest discover -t . -s tests` -> 830 tests OK (~103 s); `tests.unit.test_data_sources_tushare` + `test_notify_live` 97 OK; bridge `ast.parse(feature_version=(3,6))` + ASCII checks OK; real-lake `_build_auction` window rebuild OK; `git diff --check` clean. Host memory ample throughout; no GPU use.


## 2026-07-15 Console "沙箱镜像" false error prompt (lzp-test18)

- **Symptom**: on the lzp-test18 detail page, the 元学习结果 panel's 沙箱镜像 row displayed the full `sandbox_image_update` JSON — dominated by a ~4KB `stderr_tail` build log — reading as a fatal error.
- **Root cause**: two composing facts. (1) Docker BuildKit writes its normal progress log to stderr even on success (`stdout_tail` is empty by design), and `maybe_rebuild_sandbox_image` records the tails unconditionally as audit data. (2) `webui/static/app.js::metaResultPanel` rendered the record with a raw `JSON.stringify`, with no status-aware presentation. Verified nothing failed: ledger record `status="ok"`, `returncode=0`, image identity resolved (`acaf76f67a28`), derived image active for fold_2025Q1, worker `error=null`, heartbeat current.
- **Fix**: added `sandboxImageNode(update)` in app.js — `ok` renders one line (构建成功 ｜ tag ｜ digest12 ｜ seconds ｜ GC count); `skipped_empty`/`skipped_local_dev`/`disabled` render a one-sentence note; `failed`/`timeout`/`rejected` render a `form-error` banner plus a `pre.code-view` tail (reason or stderr, last 2000 chars). No data-layer change: the host-side manifest/ledger tails are the intended audit trail and `_agent_visible_sandbox_image_update` already withholds them from the Agent.
- **Validation**: `node --check` OK; DOM-shim render test against the real lzp-test18 ledger record → `构建成功 ｜ autotrade-sandbox:lzp-test18-epoch_001-7f234393c220 ｜ acaf76f67a28 ｜ 13.4s`; failed/timeout/rejected/skipped paths asserted. Deployment correction: in production the browser loads the SPA from the FRONTEND host's nginx copy (`/opt/cornerhead/static`), not from the hub console — pushed via `ops/webui/webui_stack.sh sync` (frontend copy verified to contain the fix; nginx serves `Cache-Control: no-cache`, so a normal reload revalidates). Also restarted the console stack (`stop`+`start`): the old process (up since 07-14) predated this batch's server.py changes, so the API gating and the `no-store` static middleware were not live; post-restart UDS probe shows `cache-control: no-store, max-age=0` and end-to-end health ok. The lzp-test18 worker runs in its own session and was untouched (heartbeat current, error=null).


## 2026-07-15 Ground the strategy-analysis reviewer in the PIT ctx contract

- **Trigger**: the lcd-test1_ step analysis (`hitl/analysis/step__…__valid_001.md`, model deepseek-v4-pro) proposed switching candidate filtering from `ctx.snapshot_dir` to `ctx.asof_dir` "to measure look-ahead bias", treating the frozen snapshot as a possible future-data leak.
- **Why it happened**: `build_fold_analysis_messages` feeds the reviewer only the guarded validation record + frozen strategy files; it never states the ctx data-visibility contract, so the reviewer guessed at snapshot_dir/asof_dir semantics. Verified the claim is false — this fold's decision anchor is 2025-12-31T23:59:59+08:00, the validation window is 20260101..20260131, and `ctx.snapshot_dir` is built with available_at ≤ the anchor, so it cannot contain January data (env doc lines 93/675; the strategy's own candidate.py docstring says "frozen at Fold decision time"). The real cause of the Sharpe 5.16 / DD 1.05% / long −0.33% anomaly is near-cash under-investment (the analysis's own hypothesis #2).
- **Change**: extracted a shared `_ENV_DATA_INVARIANTS` constant in `pipelines/fold_analysis.py` and injected it into both `STEP_ANALYSIS_SYSTEM_PROMPT` and `FOLD_ANALYSIS_SYSTEM_PROMPT` (converted to f-strings). It states the invariants the Environment already enforces (snapshot_dir = frozen pre-decision PIT baseline; asof_dir = per-tick rolling PIT; live quotes via bars/price, not persisted; PIT/T+1/substep-window/frozen-screen enforced) and redirects the reviewer from alleging Environment leakage to judging strategy misuse and overfitting-vs-luck. Prompt-only: no new guardrail, no data/contract change, output sections unchanged — living design docs remain accurate.
- **Validation**: module imports; both prompts contain the block with no stray `{`/`}` (f-string safe); the preserved section headings (`下一 Step 可检验假设`, `下一 Fold 可探索方向`) keep test_interactive_pipeline passing (44 OK); `scripts/dev/export_prompts.py` runs clean.


## 2026-07-16 Vectorize NL retrieval PIT visibility mask (lzp-test18 backtest speedup)

- **Symptom**: lzp-test18 `fold_2025Q2` step `valid_002` backtest ran ~54 min. The Q2 strategy adopted heavy general (unscoped, no ts_code) `ctx.nl()` usage — ~21 calls/backtest, ~163s each.
- **Diagnosis (measured on the live corpus)**: per ctx.nl() = ~32s LLM + ~128s non-LLM. The non-LLM cost was NOT the LLM nor the body regex (with LIMIT the body grep is ~0.1-1.9s). It was `TextRetriever._visible_index()` rebuilding the PIT visibility mask over the 4,653,385-row frozen+replay text index via a per-row `pd.Timestamp()` Python lambda (`self._datasets.map(lambda d: pd.Timestamp(cmap[d]) ...)`) = ~48s, recomputed on every decision tick because `as_of` advances and the cache key is `as_of.isoformat()`.
- **Fix** (`src/autotrade/environment/nl/retrieval.py`): convert each of the ~9 unique dataset cutoffs once into `cutoff_by_dataset`, then broadcast with a vectorized dict `.map()`. Measured 47.9s -> 0.86s (~53x) on the real corpus; the visible-row set is byte-identical (2,982,163 rows; re-verified at multiple as_of and against the original lambda via `Series.index.equals`). Per ctx.nl() drops ~163s -> ~70s, ~halving backtest wall-clock. Remaining per-call cost (title regex over ~3M rows, LLM latency) is inherent; not optimized further (a persistent text index would be over-engineering).
- **Reverted dead-end**: first tried a DuckDB hash SEMI JOIN in `_grep_bodies` to skip regexing PIT-invisible replay bodies. Benchmark showed 0% benefit (the asof/replay library is already tick-trimmed — 388,090/388,090 major_news rows visible, no future rows to skip), and an Opus cross-audit found it a regression: DuckDB 1.1.3 does not push the registered-relation semi-join below `regexp_matches`, so it still scanned all rows AND replaced the stock-scoped IN-list pushdown (regex on ~40 company rows) with a whole-shard regex, risking NL timeouts. Fully reverted; `_grep_bodies` back to its two-branch IN-list form.
- **Test**: added `test_general_body_search_excludes_pit_invisible_replay_rows` (replay row with far-future available_at excluded from a general body search at a mid-replay as_of; frozen row still returned) — direct coverage of the `_visible_index` path.
- **Known pre-existing latent edge (not introduced, not fixed)**: if EVERY unique dataset resolves to a None cutoff, `available_at <= cutoff` compares tz-aware vs an all-NaT tz-naive Series and raises TypeError. Identical in old and new code, and practically unreachable (a completed evening node almost always exists within the 10-day lookback). Recorded for awareness; out of scope for this change (no real trigger).
- **Principles**: minimalist (a 3-line vectorization, no new subsystem/cache/interface); realism preserved (identical evidence set and PIT gating); Agent autonomy untouched (no NL budget or `ctx.nl()` interface change). The running worker still executes pre-fix code; the fix applies on the next worker (re)start — the live experiment was NOT restarted.
- **Validation**: full `unittest discover` 831 OK; `test_nl_scoring` 34 OK; real-corpus mask equivalence verified at multiple as_of; Opus xhigh cross-audit verdict: exactly semantics-preserving and safe.


## 2026-07-16 Console: resume button missing for `terminated` experiments

- **Symptom**: after lzp-test18's worker self-terminated during Q2 (state=`terminated`), the console offered no 恢复运行 (resume) button, only 删除 (delete).
- **Root cause**: a frontend/backend allowlist drift. Backend `webui/manager.py:43` `_TERMINAL_RESUMABLE_STATES = ("stopped","failed","interrupted","terminated","created")` and `control(action="resume")` relaunch a dead worker from a ledger resume for any of those. But `webui/static/app.js` gated both resume buttons (home list card and detail control bar) on a stale literal `["interrupted","stopped","failed","created"]` that omitted `terminated` — so a terminated (and backend-resumable) experiment had no resume button. The terminate dialog even promises "恢复时将重跑该 Fold", making the omission a clear bug.
- **Fix**: extracted a single `RESUMABLE_STATES` constant in app.js mirroring the backend tuple, and pointed both button sites at it (prevents future drift). UI-only; the backend already supported the operation. Pushed via `ops/webui/webui_stack.sh sync`; a browser reload shows the button (static SPA is served from the frontend nginx copy).
- **Validation**: `node --check` clean; frontend copy contains `RESUMABLE_STATES`; backend resume path unchanged and already accepts `terminated`.


## 2026-07-16 lzp-test18 event-valid NL reuse and candidate-linked retrieval

- **Operational state during implementation**: the user suspended the run before changes. `lzp-test18` remained terminal (`terminated`, worker/container gone) throughout code changes, tests, and benchmarks; experiment artifacts were not mutated. Resume is deliberately deferred until the validated local commit. The preserved latest workload is `.runtime/sandboxes/lzp-test18/run_4caab624069f/agent/workspace/.nl_tool_52818ae56e5b/`: 25 completed stock requests, 18 distinct codes, 130 retrieval attempts, and 89 provider calls. Historical retrieval durations sum to 2309.140s (17.763s/tool mean, 115.099s max); the 25 NL task spans sum to 2778.870s and direct provider time to 489.228s.
- **Event-driven execution**: `StrategyNLService` keeps only successful stock analyses, keyed by code + Prompt + canonical kwargs + frozen company context. The prior Sub Agent's valid substantive retrieval patterns become the next-call event filter. Pure entity-discovery patterns are ignored; the common leading `entity.*event` / `entity event` qualifier is broadened only after RE2 validation. Any remaining/unsafe entity-qualified regex falls back to the entire candidate revision instead of risking stale reuse or a cache-induced backtest failure. The revision hashes only PIT-visible matching rows; a new matching title/body forces execution. Failed/timeout/general requests bypass storage. Every invocation remains in `nl_requests.jsonl`; hits receive a new `nlreuse` task ID/source reference and do not fabricate search/evidence/provider records.
- **Candidate-linked retrieval**: `TextRetriever` builds an exact code→row map once and registers one persistent DuckDB title view. A stock scope is the union of exact-code rows and literal code/name title matches, then PIT-filtered per `as_of`; broad background evidence is available only when `ts_code` is omitted. A 128-entry LRU retains each candidate's static index scope and incrementally loads only bodies visible at the current replay clock. Later regex patterns execute on that small frame, and newly visible rows are appended on demand. General retrieval retains its broad on-disk scan behavior. A persistent locked DuckDB connection removes per-query connection setup and is closed deterministically after replay.
- **Audit surface**: result cache metadata records hit/miss/bypass, evidence revision, event-filter count and source task on a hit. Backtest activity adds `nl_cache_status`; summary adds `nl_executed_calls`, `nl_cache_hits`, and `nl_cache_misses` while preserving `nl_calls` as the strategy invocation/budget count.
- **Correctness closure / coverage**: a stock task now always uses the strategy-requested code even if the Sub Agent supplies another tool-level code. Tests cover that boundary, blank-code news linked only by company title, unrelated-background exclusion, one body load across patterns, incremental PIT-visible body loading, unsafe-regex fallback, general PIT-invisible replay exclusion, full/event revisions, unrelated-new-text reuse, matching-new-body invalidation, failure non-reuse, cache activity/audit counts, and Probe counters. The cache remains an optimization: event-filter validation failure conservatively uses the whole candidate revision. Exact code/name context remains internal; no strategy-facing option was added.
- **Benchmark method**: replayed all 130 recorded `text_retrieve` argument objects against the exact Q2 frozen snapshot `decision_c9bac940202c0310` and replay slot `replay_84aaf6ab554b5fb7`. The request log has no sim timestamp, so its known strategy cadence (three calls/day) was mapped in order to replay trade dates from 20250102 at 08:00. Cache checks, executed retrieval, and post-analysis revision storage were timed separately; the harness made no LLM/API calls. Resource check before: 351GiB RAM available; GPUs occupied by unrelated jobs but unused by this benchmark. Afterward 284GiB remained available, still safe; concurrent host activity accounts for the system-level change after the benchmark process exited.
- **Retrieval result**: 2309.140s old recorded search wall → 118.808s replay wall, **19.44x** faster with strict incremental PIT body loading. Retriever initialization was 15.928s for 4,542,608 rows. Repeated candidates' complete historical multi-pattern requests took 0.178–0.618s. All five original invalid-RE2 errors and the aggregate 1,172 returned hits were reproduced. The 18 cached candidate scopes held 10,557 index rows and 8,537 visible body rows, about 12.9MiB combined; process RSS was ~4.7GiB and dominated by the full text index, not the candidate LRU.
- **Invocation result**: the conservative event gate hit requests 10, 12, and 17: 3 hits / 22 executions, avoiding 62.314s of measured provider time. Cache checks + executed retrieval + revision storage totaled 123.540s; remaining provider time was 426.914s. Estimated 25-task NL time is therefore 550.454s versus 2778.870s observed (**5.05x**), or about 62 minutes for the strategy's 168 quarterly calls versus ~5.2 hours. This remains an estimate until formal restart.
- **Remaining bottleneck / next plan**: provider work is ~77.6% of the estimate. If the restarted run remains too slow, first A/B a lower provider-round plan against answer/evidence quality. Cross-backtest content-addressed caching adds invalidation/reproducibility burden, while an indexed body sidecar attacks at most the remaining retrieval share; neither is justified before formal measurement.
- **Validation**: `py_compile` clean; `tests.unit.test_nl_scoring tests.unit.test_tools_flow` = 152 OK; full `PYTHONPATH=src ~/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest discover -t . -s tests` = 841 OK in 119.816s; `scripts/dev/export_prompts.py` and `git diff --check` clean. Full-suite memory was 314GiB available before and after; no GPU workload was launched.


## 2026-07-16 lzp-test18 explicit event gate, bounded enum path, and Q4 cost validation

- **Operational boundary**: the frontend gracefully terminated the suspended `lzp-test18` worker (`run_66bf9ce1fbe1`, session `epoch_002/fold_2025Q1`) before implementation. Status remained `stopped`, worker dead, throughout source edits and validation; completed ledgers and the accepted Q4 artifacts were left untouched. The interrupted Q1 session will be restarted only from the validated commit, with a strategy-owned optimization directive.
- **Root-cause baseline**: accepted Q4 `run_5edd307ed585/artifacts/results/valid_006` took 1693.590s over 65 strategy days. `phase_seconds.nl_service=763.858s` and strategy compute was 538.606s. Its 30 logical requests produced 29 misses/1 hit, 105 provider calls (570.338s), 112 retrieval attempts (184.405s), 846,904 prompt tokens, and 47,955 completion tokens. Two raw answers began with `DOWNGRADE` but later mentioned `REJECT`; substring parsing classified both incorrectly.
- **Explicit event contract**: stock calls may pass `event_filter={"patterns":[...],"lookback_days":N}`. The host validates the aggregate RE2 pattern, applies normal PIT visibility first, then the rolling interval, and matches only the code/name-linked candidate index/body scope. Zero matches return audited `status=ok,state=no_matching_evidence,content=""` without provider or model retrieval. Completed and no-evidence results are keyed by the exact request/context and matching evidence revision; an event entering or aging out changes the revision. Generic stock analysis still works but conservatively refreshes once per simulated date; general market calls remain uncached.
- **Structured decision path**: `response_format={"type":"enum","values":[...]}` configures one tool round plus one forced final response, 128 output tokens, at most 5 retrieval results, and 1,000 provider-visible snippet characters while retaining full evidence in audit. The first standalone permitted value is canonicalized, so a leading `DOWNGRADE` cannot be overwritten by a later explanatory `REJECT`. Invalid contracts and invalid provider answers are explicit audited failures. Default free-form mode retains three tool rounds and 3,000 tokens.
- **Candidate retrieval/reuse**: the existing 128-entry candidate LRU, exact code/title-linked scope, incremental PIT-visible body cache, and persistent DuckDB connection remain the only retrieval structure. The new gate reuses this scope and scans no global index/body. No database, service, persistent cross-backtest cache, or body sidecar was added; the prior 19.44x candidate-retrieval improvement therefore remains the complexity/performance baseline.
- **Telemetry and Probe**: provider call duration/usage, retrieval duration, event-filter wall, evidence count, cache/no-evidence counts, and structural max calls are emitted under `nl_cost`. Probe still withholds all NL content and performs no data/provider work; it now scales observed logical-call density to the full strategy-day count and multiplies by the maximum per-call structure (2 enum, 4 generic). This is a call-count upper bound, never a wall-time extrapolation. Agent-visible manifest summaries retain these fields.
- **Q4 offline A/B**: read-only scan against decision snapshot `sha256:c9236e132270805b52ea265e278712741a2ac2f5310c1f47a13a832f02ec023e` and valid replay `sha256:805add107d02bfe49baadce8b50690b1517a88cc0dfd527372d09c8e5183caa5`. The category-specific 30-day filter covered investigation, fraud/misstatement, litigation/arbitration, delisting/risk-warning, and accounting-restatement synonyms; it skipped 26/30 calls and found four event-positive requests. Cold filter wall was 51.523s; structural bound is 8 provider calls and 4 retrievals versus 105/112. Using observed provider latency plus the enum/retrieval caps projects NL around 110.7s. The separate exact-tail strategy benchmark preserved factor values within `2.3e-16` and reduced one daily factor build from 7.119s to 1.608s (4.43x), projecting Q4 Valid around 682s. No provider/API call and no experiment artifact mutation occurred during the benchmark.
- **Gates / deferred work**: require zero provider/retrieval work for no-evidence calls, at most two provider calls and one retrieval per event-positive cache miss, exact-tail factor/rank/candidate/plan equivalence at `atol<=1e-12`, median exact-tail speedup at least 3x, Q4 NL phase <=350s and full Valid <=900s (investigate >1050s). Add a persistent cache/body sidecar or host-preloaded single-round path only if those measurements fail; current projected benefit does not justify their invalidation and reproducibility cost.
- **Validation**: `tests/unit/test_nl_scoring.py tests/unit/test_tools_flow.py` -> 158 passed, 17 subtests; full `~/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit -q` -> 848 passed, 41 subtests, 26 pre-existing warnings in 110.86s. A new formal-RPC test proves the nested contracts reach the service and an empty PIT event scope causes zero provider/retrieval calls. `scripts/dev/export_prompts.py`, `py_compile`, and `git diff --check` were clean. An unscoped `pytest` was intentionally discarded because it traversed vendored `external_references/` projects with undeclared optional dependencies; `tests/unit` is the project suite. System memory was 368GiB available before and 367GiB after; GPUs were occupied by unrelated workloads and unused here.

## 2026-07-16 Host-only Environment progress after Agent Session completion

- **Problem**: once `session_end` appeared, the live panel replaced the Agent budget countdown with one undifferentiated “Environment is accepting, evaluating, or persisting” message. This can remain visible through artifact quiescence/hash checks, acceptance/freeze, hidden `test_000`, artifact collection/ledger persistence, and optional Fold analysis. The current lzp-test18 Q1 run illustrated the blind interval: the Agent ended at 05:03:05 UTC while the worker remained alive for final evaluation and post-Fold work.
- **Leakage constraint**: frozen Test/Held-out start, dates, per-day progress, orders, NL activity and metrics intentionally never enter the Agent-readable trace. Reusing or extending Agent SSE would violate that contract and could feed hidden-period evidence into later development. The implementation instead uses the existing worker-owned, host-only `hitl/status.json`, which is not mounted into the Sandbox or included in Prompt/Trace.
- **Minimal implementation**: `StatusReporter` gained three additive fields—`environment_stage`, `environment_stage_started_at`, and `environment_progress`. A new session resets all three; changing stage starts one timer, while same-stage progress updates preserve it. Optional internal Pipeline callbacks mark `acceptance`, `frozen_test`, `persistence`, `analysis`, `meta_finalize`, `environment_update`, and `heldout`. No Agent-facing tool, parameter or strategy interface changed.
- **Hidden replay projection**: `BacktestTool` reuses `run_main_ctx_replay`'s existing throttled callback (at most every 30 days or 30 seconds) for `frozen_eval`, publishing only `day_index`, `total_days`, percentage and replay elapsed seconds. It explicitly excludes trade date, order count, NL activity and all performance results. This adds only bounded atomic status writes; it performs no additional market/data read, model call, replay tick, service, thread or polling request.
- **Frontend**: the existing 5-second status poll renders the exact Environment stage, coarse day progress and a locally advancing stage wall clock. Held-out has a correct Environment-only prefix. Older workers without the new fields retain the generic message but now show elapsed time, so the SPA remains backward compatible. The live Agent backtest countdown and budget-credit behavior are unchanged.
- **Principles**: architecture remains a single status writer plus the existing console poll; hidden evaluation semantics and market replay are unchanged; the feature neither consumes nor constrains Agent budget/autonomy and reduces the need for researchers to interrupt or query a healthy long-running worker.
- **Validation/deployment**: focused `test_interactive_pipeline.py test_webui_backend.py test_pipeline_e2e.py test_tools_flow.py` = 251 passed + 13 subtests in 83.29s. Full `PYTHONPATH=src ... -m pytest tests/unit -q` = 849 passed + 41 subtests, 26 known warnings, in 114.65s. Tests assert stage sequencing, stable stage timestamps, new-session reset, host callback delivery, and unchanged Agent trace with no hidden dates/orders in the status projection. `py_compile`, `node --check`, and `git diff --check` passed. Available memory was 354GiB before and 353GiB after; no GPU workload was launched. `webui_stack.sh sync` installed the backward-compatible SPA on the frontend; console, tunnel, local API and nginx→tunnel health all passed. The active lzp-test18 worker remains healthy on `d2dba11`; it keeps the generic elapsed fallback, while detailed host stages begin on its next safe worker restart.

Static descriptions no longer name DeepSeek or expose implementation terms such as `thinking` and `provider`.

## 2026-07-16 Pending-approval Fold timer boundary

- **Observed case**: after `lap-test19` Q2 persisted, the manual runner moved to `epoch_001/fold_2025Q3` with `state=waiting_user`, but status retained Q2's `session_started_at=2026-07-16T06:24:53.861167Z`, `run_id=run_8730687f3cd6`, trace and deadline. The Q3 session-list row consequently kept advancing although no Q3 sandbox, cache prefetch, deadline or Agent session had begun.
- **Root cause**: `_gate()` previously updated only state/session identity. `foldDurationNode()` considered any live worker with a finite matching timestamp live; unlike in-session Step/question waits, a pre-session `waiting_user` gate has no active session clock to pause or debit.
- **Minimal fix**: one nested `announce()` path now handles both pause and manual-approval gates and clears prior run/trace/start/deadline fields. The SPA defines reusable `ACTIVE_SESSION_STATES` and `RESEARCHER_WAIT_STATES`; duration/live-panel logic shares them, so waiting approval and boundary pause cannot start or inherit a timer. The frontend guard fixes the already-running `a92defe` worker after a browser refresh; the source-side invariant applies when workers next start, without forcing an experiment restart.
- **Scope and principles**: no experiment control, ledger, artifact, cache, Agent budget or replay behavior changed. The worker remained alive and waiting for Q3 approval. The fix removes duplicate state literals while keeping timing semantics in the existing status/frontend surfaces.
- **Validation/deployment**: targeted regression tests 2 passed; complete `tests/unit/test_interactive_pipeline.py` 45 passed + 4 subtests and `tests/unit/test_webui_backend.py` 44 passed (one known Starlette deprecation warning). `node --check`, `git diff --check`, and the live UDS static response confirmed the new guard. `webui_stack.sh sync` published the SPA; the remote copy contains the guard and console/tunnel/local API/frontend end-to-end health all passed. No GPU or data workload was run.


## 2026-07-16 lzp-test18 Q4 production-path benchmark and position-exit diagnosis

- **Exit root cause**: the public `ctx.positions` rows expose `account`, `ts_code`, `side`, `quantity`, `sellable_quantity`, `entry_price`, `entry_date`, `entry_cost`, `last_price`, and `market_value`. Every inspected frozen lzp strategy derives holdings from the nonexistent `volume` key; its Q4 cost path also asks for nonexistent `cost_basis` / `avg_price` instead of `entry_price`. Frozen lap Q1–Q3 uses nonexistent `qty`. Because all paths use `.get(key, 0)`, the contract mistake becomes an empty holding set rather than an exception, so rebalance, stop-loss, take-profit, and expiry branches never submit a sell.
- **Ledger proof, not a Broker hypothesis**: accepted lzp Q4 `valid_006` contains 73 filled buys, zero strategy sells/closes, and 44 host end-of-window exits. lap Q1 contains 57 buys, zero sells, 55 host exits and two residuals; accepted lap Q2 `valid_005` contains 54 buys, zero sells, 52 host exits and two residuals. The lap Q2 diagnostic `valid_003` changed the read to `quantity` and immediately produced 237 filled sells; its only 17 sell rejects were real limit-down constraints, host exits fell to four, and one position remained. T+1 and Broker reduction therefore work. The Agent later rolled the fix back after comparing results from different fold provenance. No frozen source, result, ledger, or live experiment state was mutated during this audit.
- **Reproducible benchmark harness**: generalized `scripts/dev/replay_benchmark.py` to optionally attach the production `StrategyNLService` and file RPC, with production NL budget/deadline behavior. The harness now reproduces auction rows and prints, after-hours and intraday cadence, execution lag, corporate actions, Timeview, and per-day caps; it can explicitly avoid GPU allocation. Results record the strategy artifact hash, decision/replay snapshot IDs and hashes, sandbox spec/image identity, phase timing, NL counters/cost, order count and digest. Its default remains no-NL for existing callers.
- **Minimal event-enum path**: `candidate_evidence_state()` now optionally returns at most five records from the same candidate-linked PIT scope it already matched for the event revision. `event_filter + enum` passes those records directly to the provider, sets model tool rounds to zero, and has a structural upper bound of one provider call. It neither rescans the global index/body nor lets the model request the same candidate evidence again. Other enum calls keep one retrieval round plus a final response; generic free text retains three rounds.
- **Provider-budget correction**: live smoke tests showed reasoning-enabled direct enum calls could spend all 512 or even 1,024 output tokens on hidden reasoning before emitting a label. `complete_tools()` therefore accepts a per-call reasoning override; only the prefetched `event_filter + enum` fast path disables hidden reasoning without mutating the shared proxy. Standalone enum retrieval and free-form NL keep the configured reasoning mode. The enum cap is 512 tokens. The previously failing real `600420.SH` / litigation-conclusion case then returned canonical `PASS` in one call (5.809s, 1,140 prompt tokens, two completion tokens, two prefetched evidence records, zero retrieval calls). Provider conversations remain in the normal ignored audit directory.
- **Exact-tail equivalence gate**: the runtime-only Q4 derivative preserves full-history valuation winsor quantiles/fill, narrows daily grouping to the required 21-row tail after the universe filter, and performs one combined DuckDB event scan for margin and moneyflow. At real rolling PIT states 2025-07-01, 2025-08-01, and 2025-09-30, factor max absolute deltas were `2.20e-16`, `2.46e-16`, and `2.32e-16`; rank/candidate order was identical. Runtime fell 6.415→1.548s, 6.894→1.753s, and 7.450→2.034s (4.14x, 3.93x, 3.66x). Evidence is retained at `.runtime/bench/lzp-test18-q4-factor-equivalence.json`.
- **Full Q4 measurement**: replayed the accepted segment with decision snapshot `snap_1b306f7e867c` / `sha256:c9236e...`, replay `replay_be8b6b3f6c77` / `sha256:805add...`, and frozen image `sha256:acaf76...`. The historical accepted baseline was 1,693.590s across 65 strategy days and 18,265 ticks: NL 763.858s, strategy 538.606s, 105 provider calls / 570.338s, 112 retrieval attempts / 184.405s, and 846,904 prompt + 47,955 completion tokens. The optimized production-path run was 826.108s (**2.05x, -51.2%**): strategy 248.938s, NL 58.695s, Timeview roll 165.640s, host overhead 136.058s, IPC 120.037s, broker 67.646s, Timeview init 28.193s, and merge 0.901s. Its 28 logical NL calls yielded 25 no-evidence skips and three completed calls; provider calls fell 105→3, provider time 570.338→7.583s, retrieval 112→0, prompt tokens 846,904→3,429, and completion tokens 47,955→6. The full result is `.runtime/bench/lzp-test18-q4-optimized-live.json`.
- **Interpretation and cost/performance decision**: all predefined gates passed (`NL<=350s`, replay `<=900s`, provider `<=8`, retrieval `<=4`, zero work on no-evidence, every positive case completed). The observed return/order stream is not a quality A/B: event-driven filtering changes the NL path and the derivative intentionally retains the position-field bug to avoid mixing a correctness fix into timing, so its 5.19% return and 75-order digest must not be used for Q4 model selection. Event matching itself is now 48.819s, only ~5.9% of total replay; a body sidecar or persistent cross-backtest result cache would add invalidation/reproducibility machinery for at most that share and is not justified. If a tighter total-runtime target is later required, profile strategy compute first, then Timeview/IPC; fix position-schema usage separately as a correctness task.
- **Validation/resources**: focused NL + DeepSeek tests passed 69 + 10 subtests; tools-flow passed 109 + 9 subtests; full `PYTHONPATH=src ... pytest tests/unit -q` passed 850 + 41 subtests with 26 pre-existing warnings in 112.07s. The final benchmark used `--cpu-only`; other formal experiments were allowed to continue on the shared host, so the end-to-end number includes representative contention rather than an isolated synthetic machine. Available memory remained safe and no experiment worker, frozen artifact, or ledger was edited.

## 2026-07-16 Case-study remediation batch (agent-environment feedback, cron not-ready contract, NL correctness, console return semantics)

- **Scope**: implements the reviewed remediation plan derived from the round-1 repository audit plus the lap-test19/lzp-test18 agent-trace case study, on branch `fix/case-study-remediation` (local only, not pushed). The other agent's batch `4c493bf..457b299` was reviewed first (13 commits, PIT-clean, safe base; none of the plan items had landed there).
- **Pre-open margin not-ready contract**: `download_event_flow` gains `--zero-rows-not-ready`; with zero writes it prints `not_ready_no_mutation` and exits `NO_MUTATION_RETRY_EXIT_CODE` (75), and `cron_update` now honors 75 for `download_event_flow` single-command jobs (restores the prior committed generation, records `not_ready`). Partial writes commit normally and defer the empty partitions to the distinct retry job. Flag added to the four pre-open margin jobs in `configs/tushare_update_schedule.json`; backfills without the flag keep failing fast. Tests: `test_event_flow_zero_rows_not_ready_exits_75_without_mutation`, `..._partial_write_commits`, plus the pre-existing raise test unchanged.
- **Position-row schema + advisory**: template `README.md` and the fold system prompt now enumerate the exact `ctx.positions` row keys with quantity/sellable_quantity semantics; `modification_check` adds the warn-only `unknown_position_row_key` advisory (AST: loop/comprehension targets over `.positions`-rooted iterables incl. simple aliases; constant keys outside the 10-key schema). Verified against real artifacts: flags lap frozen `trading.py:94` (`qty`) and lzp frozen `candidate.py:244` (`volume`); zero findings on the template and on epoch_003's variable-key fallback pattern (which is runtime-correct).
- **Exit-path observability**: replay stats add `order_lifecycle` (entry/exit/cash × submitted/filled/rejected/cancelled; host end-of-region liquidations bypass the book by design) and `strategy_exit_fill_count`; both are agent-visible on probes and full runs. `_diagnostic_warnings` adds the warn-only "退出路径检查" when `trade_count>0` with zero strategy exit fills, and an incomplete-liquidation note. Fold development history persists `trade_count`, `strategy_exit_fill_count`, `liquidation_complete` and the compact `benchmark` block so later epochs see exit health and benchmark-relative performance.
- **Probe failures**: `_public_failure` now appends exception class + agent-file `file:line` (parsed from the host-retained detail; wrapper classes filtered; messages stay host-only because they can embed replay-window values). Probe summaries carry `result_path=null` (probes write no artifacts; the audited failure mode sent agents to empty directories).
- **Reject-reason fidelity**: `reject_submission` gains `strategy_reason` so submission-time rejects (incl. `_resolve_close`d sells) keep the caller's reason instead of the resolved verb — the `reason='sell'` string that misled the lzp Q4 17,893-order forensics.
- **Wrap-up prompt**: now instructs re-reading `steps/tree.txt` before choosing the restore target and states the real `finish_fold` contract (run-local complete validation; cross-run/cross-fold restores need one full validation — restore a run-local validated Step when time is short). PROMPTS.md re-exported.
- **NL correctness**: general body grep (`_grep_bodies`) semi-joins PIT-visible text ids inside the DuckDB query so LIMIT counts visible matches only (the candidate-scoped path was already fixed in `3921857`; the logbook justification for reverting the general path — "no future rows to skip" — was wrong because replay shards span the whole slot). Regression test with 500 future-dated matches crowding one visible tail row fails on the old code. Enum parsing (`_first_enum_value`) uses per-choice boundaries: CJK-bearing values must stand alone (`不减持` no longer parses as `减持`; ambiguity becomes an auditable parse failure), ASCII values keep the ASCII guard (`结论PASS` still parses) — fixes the batch-review MEDIUM finding.
- **Console**: `/trace*` endpoints validate `run_id` as one safe path segment (mirrors the style-endpoint guard; traversal test added). Cumulative valid/test metrics are per-epoch (`metrics.epoch_id` = latest; `metrics_by_epoch` side-by-side) — the previous code compounded the same fold calendar once per epoch. `/equity` accepts `epoch_id`, returns `epochs`/`epoch_id` for the SPA switcher, and adds server-computed full-cycle stats per chained series (cum/annualized return, annualized vol, Sharpe, max drawdown, daily win rate, β, excess vs CSI300, tracking error, information ratio; benchmark-relative legs compounded over date-matched days). SPA renders the epoch switcher + stats table on full-size charts and tags KPI tiles with the epoch. SIGCHLD `SIG_IGN` returncode caveat documented at the handler; frozen-eval progress payload key-set regression pin added; `ops/qmt/README.md` aligned with code (confirm-mismatch/off-window are errors not dry_run; payload dedup is trade-day-scoped, cross-day replays blocked by the trade_date gate).
- **Living docs**: environment_design (summary fields, probe error identity, advisory list), agent_design (schema contract + advisory), data_documentation (pre-open not-ready contract), pipeline_design (no cross-epoch aggregation + full-cycle stats), parameters_reference (`--zero-rows-not-ready`).
- **Validation/resources**: full `PYTHONPATH=src ~/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit -q` → 860 passed + 41 subtests (114.63s, 26 pre-existing warnings); `node --check app.js`, `git diff --check` clean. CPU-only work; free memory 281GiB before the suite; no experiment worker, frozen artifact, ledger or runtime state touched — running experiments pick the changes up on their next session start.

## 2026-07-16 Repo-wide design-principles compliance scan and remediation

- **Frontend deployment**: static SPA synced to the frontend server via `webui_stack.sh sync`; console stopped/started to load the per-epoch metrics API (workers are detached and unaffected). Verified live over the UDS: `/api/experiments` serves `metrics.epoch_id` + `metrics_by_epoch` (lzp-test18 E1 +14.87% / E2 +11.51% / E3 +0.08% listed separately), `/equity?epoch_id=epoch_001` returns the epoch selector plus full-cycle stats (244 days, Sharpe 0.60, max drawdown 24.8%, beta 0.88, excess −0.64%).
- **Scan setup**: four parallel read-only scanners (Fable 5: environment core, agent+pipeline; Opus 4.8: data layer, console/ops/scripts) judged the repo against the three design principles, with all recently-landed remediations excluded. Verdicts: broadly compliant everywhere; findings triaged below.
- **Fixed — agent session loop** (`agent/runner.py`, `agent/compact.py`): (1) main-conversation provider calls clamp internal retries by remaining deadline (`max_retries=0` when a second attempt cannot fit), restoring the "worst overrun is one bounded call" invariant; (2) three consecutive `LLMProxyError`s trip a circuit ending the session as `llm_unavailable` instead of burning the 200-call budget on a fast-failing provider; (3) a provider context-length error forces one compaction via a new `force=` path that bypasses only the token-threshold heuristic (CJK undercount ~2x) while keeping the failure-circuit/structural guards; (4) backtest observations now echo `steps_used/steps_limit/steps_remaining`; (5) `step_rollback` spec+handler register only when the step tree is enabled; (6) meta prompt tool table gained the missing `ask_user` row; PROMPTS.md re-exported.
- **Fixed — environment core**: slipped fills clamp into the daily limit band (`broker_core.clamp_to_limit_band`, threaded through `project_open` and both reduce paths incl. host region-end exits) — slippage is a liquidity assumption and must saturate at the band edge; deleted the producer-less `_ACTION_ALIASES` compatibility map (driver emits canonical verbs only) and the dead `Order.source_artifacts` field plus its `orders.parquet` serialization branch; `style_analysis._rank_cross_section` vectorized (was a per-row Python loop over the ~5k-code cross-section on every replay day); NL `_visible_index` cache now keyed by the per-dataset cutoff signature so every tick between refresh nodes reuses the same materialized slice.
- **Fixed — data layer**: `download_fundamental_ann_month_dataset` passes `allow_key_removal_overwrite=True` like every sibling whole-partition path; a `forecast_vip` update_flag supersede or retraction no longer wedges the month's nightly refresh forever (the >20-key/>20% shrink guard still blocks mass truncation).
- **Fixed — console**: `read_trace_page` skips past a single event line larger than the page budget instead of stalling the SSE stream/replay loader at the same offset forever (mirrors the tail reader; emits an `<oversized event skipped>` placeholder); `_create_experiment` checks the running cap before `mkdir` so a cap rejection leaves no half-created directory (was: retry dead-ends on "already exists"); both under `self._mutate`, race-free.
- **Reverted during validation**: the reviewer-suggested removal of the "duplicate" `host_peak_rss_bytes` assignment broke `test_backtest_failure_does_not_leave_empty_result_directory` — the finally-block assignment is the only one reachable on error paths; restored with a comment.
- **Explicitly skipped (cost > benefit), with evidence**: 09:20 event/flow audit scope-down — actual runtime 2m38–2m42s across the last two trading days (cron logs), finishing before the 09:25 gate, and dataset scoping would overwrite the whole-domain `event_flow_status.json` consumed by research-release pinning; snapshot minute-build streaming refactor (503GiB host, concat path is correct today, refactor risks the builder before a new experiment round); auction data-gap market-order asymmetry (rare path, conservative direction); NL legacy `keywords`→pattern mapping (tested, supported robustness behavior); blind-auction market orders not reserving cash during the 09:15→09:30 submit lag (small window, conservative to fills; follow-up candidate: reserve at up-limit like real counters — documented, not changed); runner trim-knob and dead `status="rejected"` ledger branch (harmless, documented by scanners).
- **Docs**: environment_design (band clamp realism note; transfer 09:14 tick-granularity caveat), agent_design (session-loop guards, step counter echo, step_rollback gating).
- **Validation/resources**: full `tests/unit` 868 passed + 41 subtests (113s) after fixes (two intermediate failures caught the RSS revert and the spec/handler lock-step gate, both resolved); `git diff --check` clean; CPU-only, no experiment worker, ledger or frozen artifact touched.

## 2026-07-17 Derivatives market context ingestion (futures / options / convertible bonds / CN treasury curve)

- **Scope and design**: nine non-tradable context datasets ride the existing macro-domain machinery end to end (spec -> nightly update -> audit -> research release -> snapshot -> Timeview evening node), keeping zero Broker/tool/prompt-protocol changes. Signals (index-futures basis, option PCR/IV, CB conversion premium, redemption events, term spread) are computed by the Agent itself via DuckDB on snapshot files. Daily tables use the trade_date strategy with EOD-stamped `available_at` (visible next trading morning after the evening node); registries use a new ~30-line `static_full` strategy (whole-table refresh per exchange through the revision-aware overwrite, key-removal accepted, shrink guard retained); snapshot exempts the three instrument registries from the macro window floor while per-row `available_at` (list date) still enforces the PIT wall (`MACRO_REGISTRY_DATASETS`, regression-tested).
- **Scale corrections found by live smokes** (both fail-fast, both fixed and committed separately): whole-market `opt_daily` is ~27k rows/day of mostly commodity options — scoped to SSE/SZSE/CFFEX via a per-exchange loop on the trade_date strategy (~1.5-2k rows/day, 15x smaller), with per-venue listing dates (SZSE/CFFEX options 2019-12-23) honoured in both the downloader task list and audit expected paths; `yc_cb` is a dense ~500-term grid (~1,010 rows/day) whose API rejects offset paging (50101 whenever a window needs page 2) — switched to one un-paged call per trade date (fixed ts_code loop), coverage floor 2016-06-20 probed live; the interim month-window variant was fully reverted.
- **Backfill (production lock discipline)**: manual `tushare_download.py` runs under the exclusive updater flock; single writer verified. Landed: fut_mapping / fut_daily 4,014 partitions each (2010->), opt_daily 5,957 files / 2,170,274 rows (2015->), cb_daily 2,070 partitions / 739,553 rows (2018->), yc_cb 2,447 days, four registries; ~615MB total. Macro audit over 20260601-20260716: status ok, 0 errors, 0 warnings.
- **Case study** (`.runtime/bench/derivatives_case_study{,2}.py`, summary json alongside): real decision snapshot at 2026-06-30 09:25 built twice (macro tuple with vs without the nine datasets, intraday off): build 824.7s -> 886.0s (+7.4%); macro.parquet 1,058,369 -> 3,043,177 rows / 64.8 -> 114.0 MB; worst-case whole-frame pandas load 5.76s -> 18.6s — confirmed OFF the hot path (Timeview hardlinks the frozen macro file after reading only its parquet footer, per-roll writes slice only newly-visible replay rows, agent queries use DuckDB column projection; the +61s build cost is one-time and shared via the replay content cache). Replay slot 20260623-20260630 built in 40.0s (82,719 macro rows). PIT assertions all passed: decision snapshot carries fut_daily/cb_call only up to T-1; inside the replay window the 06-29 settle stays invisible for all of 06-29 and appears from 06-30 pre-open; 2010-listed registry contracts visible (window exemption) while future-listed contracts stay hidden. Agent-style signal verified end to end: IF main contract resolved daily via fut_mapping, basis vs 000300.SH computed by a single DuckDB query (late-June 2026 discount to -2.89%, main-contract roll IF2606->IF2609 visible on 06-18->06-22).
- **Resources**: 341-344GiB RAM free throughout; disk 1.5T free; CPU-bound downloads at the official rate limit; no experiment worker, ledger, or frozen artifact touched.

## 2026-07-18 Frontend fixes + end-to-end engineering performance batch

- **Dropdown root cause (fixed earlier this session, commits `...490d190`/`4581b03`)**: the creation form's dataset choices come from `SnapshotConfig().macro_datasets` (live) + `_dataset_labels()` reading the `interfaces` registry in `configs/tushare_update_schedule.json`. Two independent causes: the console process predated the derivatives commits, and the nine new datasets had no `interfaces` entries (labels/doc_refs). Added the entries, restarted the console; verified live `/api/parameter-schema` serves 34 choices with Chinese labels.
- **Check-chip typography/alignment**: `.field label` (specificity 0,1,1: `display:block; font-weight:600`) silently overrode `.check-item` (0,1,0), so chips rendered bold with baseline-aligned text everywhere (板块范围, 事件数据子集). Selector raised to `label.check-item` (ties 0,1,1, later rule wins): inline-flex centering, weight 500, 0.875rem, `margin:0`. Synced via `webui_stack.sh sync`; verified the deployed CSS through nginx on the frontend host.
- **E2E perf review**: one Fable sub-agent audited data refresh, sandbox init, data loading and backtest replay against real logs/benchmarks; every finding was evidence-checked before implementation. Landed:
  1. **NL event-gate memoization** (`environment/nl/retrieval.py`): `_CandidateCorpus.match_cache` stores per-pattern regex verdicts keyed by corpus row label (rows/bodies immutable; labels are stable slices of the retriever's one-time RangeIndex). `_matching_candidate_rows` now regexes only never-tested rows. Evidence: the gate ran on every `ctx.nl()` call and re-scanned the full corpus — 29.5% of all replay wall time across 87 replays (8,286.7s of 28,090.8s; mean 0.97s/call); expected ~60–95s saved per full replay. Revision hashes unchanged; regression test `test_event_gate_regex_verdicts_are_memoized_per_corpus` spies the DuckDB handle via a delegating wrapper (connection methods are read-only attributes).
  2. **Nightly call-volume restructure** (`configs/tushare_update_schedule.json` + `ops/cron/tushare_update.cron`): `cn_evening_full` fundamental refresh windows 90→10 days (announcement-driven re-pulls keep ~10 nightly retries), `namechange`/`ths_index` full per-code sweeps removed from nightly; new Saturday jobs `cn_weekly_reference_deep` (12:35) and `cn_weekly_fundamental_deep` (14:00, 90-day windows, period-count 6, ann-month 3) preserve the deep repair cadence. Nightly job measured 2h35m–2h50m → expected ~1h10m, restoring margin before the 02:30 full audit. Crontab reinstalled (`install_tushare_cron.py`, backup `logs/cron_backups/crontab-20260718-000107.bak`; `crontab -l | grep -c cn_weekly` = 2). Drift guard updated: `_CRON_LINE` accepts a day-of-week field; the weekly jobs are exempted from the "landing job must be a node" rule with the documented rationale (visibility stays governed by the evening/PIT-event-build nodes and row-level `available_at`; Saturday launches precede no trading session).
  3. **Shared snapshot cache** (`pipelines/experiment.py`): cache root moved from `experiment_dir/snapshot_cache` to `experiments_root/.snapshot_cache`. Keys are fully content-derived (parts + provider config + raw generation + format version) and builds are cross-process single-flighted, so sharing is safe; measured duplication between lzp-test20/21: 8.1GB and ~6.5min per shared fold. Dot-dir stays invisible to the console experiment listing. `docs/pipeline_design.md` path-role table updated.
  4. **Close-fallback key set** (`environment/replay_market.py`): every `close_fallback` candidate has `minute_key == "15:00"` by construction, so the collision key set now only covers the day's 15:00 rows instead of the full ~700k-row day frame (measured 0.21s/day → ~0.001s/day of `host_replay_overhead`).
  5. **check.md correction**: the "max 2-way parallel domain updates" line was false (the updater is strictly sequential; step-time sum ≈ total wall to within 4s) — replaced with the erratum + the call-volume restructure entry; new optimizations appended per the file's maintenance convention.
- **Deferred with reasons**: pre-normalized minute cache at build time (medium confidence, requires a cache format-version bump and ~+26s contended CPU per build; revisit if replay profiles show `_minute_columns` hot again). Verified-optimal areas left untouched: pre-open chain, container lifecycle (56–60ms), prefetch (≤0.3ms wait), Timeview roll, broker/state phases.
- **Session-diff principle re-check (user-requested)**: all changes local/additive, no new fallback branches, fail-fast preserved (DuckDB errors still raise); NL revisions byte-identical (tested); replay semantics unchanged; shared-cache keys content-complete so no cross-experiment contamination; no agent-visible contract changed — the Agent only sees faster NL gates.
- **Validation/resources**: full `tests/unit` 873 passed + 41 subtests (112.9s) after fixing three test-side failures (DuckDB read-only method spy, cron drift-guard weekly exemption, shared-cache path); `git diff --check` clean. Config/CSS/cron + CPU-light code paths only — no RAM/GPU-intensive run; no experiment worker, ledger or frozen artifact touched; running experiments pick up pipeline changes on next worker start.

## 2026-07-18 lzp-test21 root-cause review (meta-learning / fold-agent / held-out forensics)

- **Setup**: 3 Fable sub-agents in parallel over `experiments/lzp-test21`: meta traces (run_28d46556635b / a881fdb64f5a / 73a643a76a16 + taste.md ×3), fold traces (9 runs incl. all-1-step epoch_003 folds and the 8/10-step epoch_002 folds), held-out runs (run_3cf67db01fd0/45916b9bd5de/5d8311c3fced) vs dev winner run_09aa530fac2d; plus own ledger/hash-chain/regime analysis.
- **Outcome facts**: dev chained test −11.0% → +11.0% → +23.6% per epoch; held-out 2026-04..06 −0.18/−2.46/−5.15% vs CSI300 +8.03/+1.76/+1.78 (excess ≈ −19.3pp, Sharpe to −8.4), long-only beta 0.08–0.31. Held-out market was benign (CSI300 +10.0%, CSI1000 +13.5%); May–Jun was narrow (advancers 43.3/41.3% = 16-month lows, limit-up rate 2.19/2.76% = 16-month highs).
- **Structural findings**: epoch_003 froze only 2 distinct strategy hashes across 13 folds; valid(N) == test(N−1) exactly for 1-step folds (validate-parent-unmodified-and-freeze); dev months identical across epochs so cross-epoch improvement = progressive selection on the same window, meta-learning as the conduit (boundaries respected — meta saw only validation results, no test/heldout, verified by string search of meta workspaces).
- **Meta layer**: moneyflow thesis founded on one same-day Spearman (mechanical); epoch_003 taste certified "真实 alpha，不是样本内过拟合" untested, banned new factor families, and its 收敛期 directive (quoted verbatim by agents at finish_fold) suppressed exploration to 11/13 zero-diff folds.
- **Fold layer**: all 40 runs hill-climbed a single ~20-day month (sub-0.2pp deltas decided features; same edit rejected in 202503, accepted in 202511 on different noise); 0 train-snapshot accesses experiment-wide; acceptance gates warn-only on single-month return/Sharpe; one wrong-rollback freeze misattributed to "NL non-determinism" (engine deterministic); models/ empty by Taste steering, never revisited.
- **Held-out forensics (mechanism)**: within-universe signal did NOT decay (picks vs screened-universe median fwd5: +0.13/+0.33/+0.53pp, better than dev −0.02pp). Loss decomposition of −19.3pp excess: structural low exposure −7.3pp (09:25 buys sized on available_cash before same-tick sells settle → ~40% avg exposure ceiling; month-end dom≥25 full liquidation = 4–8 flat days; June margin-gap gate whipsaw), universe/style divergence −7.9pp (mid-cap pb≤6 pond lagged HS300 by −5.4/−8.5/−12.0pp per month), costs −2.3pp (~10x monthly turnover), stale-reference-price stop bug ≈ −0.7pp net but killed April winners (74/78 morning dd_exits were false triggers off frozen pre-month `_load_ref_prices`; true losers escaped to −8..−11% auction stops), data quality ≈ −1.1pp.
- **Data quality**: `margin` exchange-level rows 20260529/20260605/20260626 have 1 of 3 exchanges (Σrzye −49% craters; first such days in 12 months) → June friendliness whipsaw 0.27–0.35; moneyflow/daily/limit fields clean; NL filter 0 errors but its 利空 vetoes removed winners (vetoed names fwd5 excess +2.4pp Apr / +3.8pp Jun).
- **Dev success was illusory**: 2025-12 winner fold: exposure-weighted benchmark capture −0.16%, per-pick edge ≈ 0, +6.9% driven by 2 outlier trades (+5.1pp); reported β 0.747 a regression artifact of the saw-tooth.
- **Verdict**: primary = evaluation design (single-month validation × chained inheritance × same dev window reused 3 epochs) amplified by meta doctrine-hardening; secondary = agent-authored latent strategy defects (stale price fallback, pre-sell cash sizing, month-end liquidation) that single-month validation could never catch; tertiary = data quality (margin gaps, NL veto quality); model capability least — agents executed the flawed scheme faithfully, never challenged the objective. Follow-up candidates (not implemented): multi-window/stability-binding acceptance gates, dev-window rotation or embargo across epochs, partial-exchange-row guard in margin ingestion, NL veto calibration feedback, exposure/capture diagnostics in backtest feedback.

## 2026-07-18 Approved optimization batch, margin repair, lzp-test22 release-pin fix

- **Margin completeness (item 1)**: `margin_missing_exchanges` (SSE+SZSE always, +BSE from 20230213 — probed: exactly 4 violating days in 1,583 partitions); download refuses partial responses via the existing zero-rows not-ready contract, committed partial partitions are re-attempted by covering non-force runs, `audit_margin_exchange_completeness` errors on any committed partial day. Repair ran under the updater lock without `--force`: 21 complete days skipped, exactly the 4 partial days re-pulled (1→3 rows, REVISION_ALERTs logged), post-check zero incomplete days.

- **Meta/fold prompt hardening (item 4 + category neutrality)**: meta Taste contract — no proven-generalization claims from dev evidence, no factor-family bans (must keep a differently-sourced fallback), convergence keeps ≥1 testable hypothesis per fold, evidence-strength bullet on repeated dev windows; both prompts enumerate supported strategy categories per check.md's coverage matrix and bound Taste to the tradable scope (A-share cash + margin; derivatives data read-only). PROMPTS.md re-exported twice (16 prompt tests green).

Item 3 resolved as config-only: quarterly WF adopted, no validation_periods knob.
- **lzp-test22**: created 02:11 CST during cn_evening_full's lock → `_select_existing_release` picked the newest complete release f0e702a8 (materialized 2026-07-16 13:34, pre-derivatives, 112 datasets) → first snapshot build crashed on missing `fut_basic` (state=failed, one attempt_failed meta record, code_version 4531d86). Fix: `pin_research_release(required_raw_datasets=...)` from assembly (macro+events+text tuples) — selection skips non-covering releases with an actionable no-release error, fresh/live paths assert coverage, stale pins fail at pin time with the remedy. Tests: 3 new cases in test_research_release (15 passed); wiring assertion updated. Recovery: experiment archived (`.runtime/bench/lzp-test22-failed-archive-20260718.tar.gz`), deleted + recreated via console API with identical params; auto-spawned worker pinned generation 4175cdd8 (121 datasets, fut_basic present); stop requested pending the compliance audit, restart to follow.
- **Resources/validation**: full tests/unit 879 passed + 41 subtests; git diff --check clean; commits 12b3727..d460dcc local only (not pushed). RAM 236Gi free at repair time; no GPU work.

## 2026-07-18 Repo-wide read-through + cost-performance triage (storage hygiene, stale processes)

- **Setup**: full repository read-through (6 living docs first-hand; 7 parallel sub-agents over environment core, environment data/NL/tools, pipelines/agent, tushare layer, webui/QMT/ops, full test suite, experiments state). All known issues then ranked by benefit ÷ introduced-redundancy; only the top two (both zero-code) were executed.
- **Retired dead raw datasets**: `data/raw/{slb_sec,slb_sec_detail,stk_auction_c,stk_auction_o}` (91M/31M/618M/552M ≈ 1.29G). Deactivated from specs/schedule in June (see 2026-06-03/04 entries); `rg` over src/scripts/configs/ops/tests/docs confirmed zero active references; user confirmed data will never be used again. Moved whole directories under the updater's exclusive `flock` (`.runtime/tushare/locks/tushare_update.lock`, 60s wait) to `archive/data_raw/20260718_retired_datasets/` — reversible rename, no inode mutation, so the hardlinked copies inside all 7 existing `data/research_releases/<gen>/raw/` remain intact; future pins no longer clone them. Post-check: `ls data/raw | grep` → 0 matches.
- **Killed stale test processes**: PIDs 461828/467925 (`python -m unittest tests.unit.test_interactive_pipeline[...]`, started 2026-07-14, ELAPSED 3d23h, S-state in `hrtimer_nanosleep` — the interactive gate's control.json poll loop with no approver; normal suite wall is ~113s). Clean SIGTERM exit, no SIGKILL needed.
- **Evaluated and deliberately NOT fixed (benefit < redundancy, with verification evidence)**:
  - Snapshot full-directory hash: `verify_snapshot_hash` is test-only (grep); backtest binding (`tools/backtest.py:880`) compares recorded `snapshot_id`/`snapshot_hash` strings; `_snapshot_hash` runs once per cache-miss build, amortized by the cross-experiment `.snapshot_cache`.
  - `_read_dataset_window` pyarrow predicate pushdown: PIT-correctness-sensitive path (string `available_at` + `to_cn_timestamps` mixed-format handling); no profiling evidence it dominates; builds cache-amortized.
  - `revision_events.jsonl` rotation (56.7M): harmless size; rotation code would touch an audit contract and data_documentation §4 for no current pain.
  - `share_float_complete` nightly full-union rebuild: shrink-guard semantics make incremental logic a correctness risk; nightly window already fits after the 07-18 pruning batch.
  - `detailed_return.json` full broker-event embedding: result-contract change across docs/tests/consumers; recent full-quarter replays show no actual pain.
  - Reveal-gate middleware, console `SIGCHLD=SIG_IGN`, frontend nftables fail-open: each replaces a simple, documented, tested mechanism with more machinery — kept minimal per principle 1.
  - NL `candidate_evidence_state` per-call digest: per-code candidate rows are small; NL wall already meets targets after the 07-16 optimization batch (763.9s→58.7s).
- **Resources/validation**: pure data moves + process signals, no code or config changed, no tests to run; `git status` clean before and after. Working tree untouched.

## 2026-07-18 lzp-test23: fold_2025Q3 snapshot-build wedge + valid style-rollup scoping

- **Wedge diagnosis (fold_2025Q3, stuck 4h16m in "sandbox/data prep")**: worker PID 1077963 held `.snapshot_cache/.locks/decision_ffa88c9a0a9f6e13.lock` since 18:01:44 with thread `snapshot-domain_1` pinned at ~99% CPU; py-spy (installed into `quant`) showed the stack in `_apply_screen` → `frame[mask]` → pandas `take_nd`, unchanged across 20s samples, RSS 65.6G, no run dir created, log silent since 20:11. Events-window row counts from the pinned release (`7e10fdfa`) per quarter: 2024Q3 2.77M / 2024Q4 2.73M / 2025Q1 6.42M / 2025Q2 7.09M — the jump is `dc_member` (3.1M+3.4M), `kpl_concept_cons`, `moneyflow_ths` etc., ingested by commit `b652403` with raw backfill landing 2026-07-18 00:39; lzp-test23's morning pin is the first release containing it, and fold_2025Q3's test-decision window (2024-07..2025-06, anchor 2025-06-30) the first with two full quarters. Union-then-screen materialized a ~19M-row × union-wide mostly-NaN object frame in a single take.
- **Fix**: `snapshot.py::_build_available_at_domain` gains `screen=` and applies `_apply_screen` per dataset (narrow frames) before the union; both `build_events` call sites (decision + replay slot) pass `screen=screened` and drop the post-union screen. Output rows identical (screen commutes with concat); fully-screened datasets still contribute columns (typed empty frames appended).
- **Recovery ops**: SIGTERM→SIGKILL old worker; removed orphaned 2.0G staging `.decision_ffa88c9a0a9f6e13.9c1e0899`; restarted worker detached (new PID 2494704) — resumed `epoch_001/fold_2025Q3`, rebuild wrote daily/intraday/auction/fundamentals/macro to staging within ~3 min at peak RSS 12.4G (was 65G+ and 0 files in 4h16m).
- **fold_2024Q2 α question**: not a calculation error. Selected window `valid_014`: `alpha_annualized=+1.3295, beta=2.345, r2=0.194, n_days=58` — consistent with total_return +36.09% vs benchmark +3.10% (ledger compact block matches). The low α came from `style_valid.json`: `write_style_rollup` merges ALL `valid_*` sidecars with per-date `setdefault`, so 15 overlapping attempt windows (mostly −19%..−34% discarded versions; fold ended `no_update` on drawdown 0.361>0.25) stitched a first-seen chimera series → α=−1.289, β=0.535, excess −30.8% on the console card while the fold headline showed +36.09%.
- **Fix**: `write_style_rollup(..., windows=)` restriction + `_write_style_rollups(..., only=)`; `run_fold` passes the ledger-selected window (`selected["result_name"]`) for the `valid` prefix; test/heldout unchanged. Regenerated `style_valid.json` for all 6 recorded lzp-test23 folds from their accepted windows (2024Q1 α .411/β .804; 2024Q2 α 1.3295/β 2.345; 2024Q3 α −1.134/β .837; 2024Q4 α −.174/β 1.053; 2025Q1 α .833/β .539; 2025Q2 α .753/β 1.018) — per-window `style_analysis.json` files untouched.
- **Validation**: `test_style_analysis` 6 passed (incl. new `test_rollup_windows_restriction_uses_only_named_window`), `test_snapshot_builder` 42 passed, `test_pipeline_e2e` 53 passed (46.7s). Docs: `environment_design.md` §3.7 Barra-lite bullet + `parameters_reference.md` §9 style-endpoint row updated. Resources: host 236G+ free RAM before restart; rebuild peak 12.4G.

## 2026-07-18 Four-analyst review: strategy-generation procedure + replay performance batch

- **Evidence**: 78 backtest summaries (test22+23): full valid 5.85 s/day; phases timeview_roll 34% / host_replay_overhead 26% / strategy_ipc 19% / compute 12.7% / init ~10s per backtest; every backtest re-wrote identical per-day Timeview parts (~16×/fold). Fold traces: backtest wall ≈75% of fold elapsed; ≥6/34 full validations behaviorally identical (float-identical returns, different hashes); 2024Q2/2024Q3 ended `step_limit_reached` with `write_locked=False`. Meta run_364b286f970b: effort mostly data profiling; Taste steered folds verbatim; lzp-test21 epoch_003 hash-diversity 13 folds→2 hashes (doctrine collapse).
- **Landed**: (1) Timeview per-run part stash (`timeview.py::_write_part`; backtest passes `runtime/timeview_stash/<sha16(snapshot|replay)>`; hardlink reuse, footer row-count fail-fast; probes are strict prefixes); (2) RangeIndex guard in `_DomainView.__init__` — `reset_index(drop=True)` copied the whole 2025Q3 replay events union and pinned fold_2025Q3's first backtest in "first-day processing" 30+ min (live py-spy stack: timeview.py:239); (3) threaded partition reads in `_read_dataset_window` (`_PARTITION_READ_WORKERS=8`, pool.map preserves sorted order → byte-identical concat); (4) `STEP_WRAP_UP_PROMPT` injected when the last budgeted step completes (+`step_wrap_up_sent` in session summary); (5) `_orders_signature` on full validations + `behaviorally_identical_validation` diagnostic warning + `orders_signature`/`turnover` in summaries; (6) `_compact_fold_history` projects `exposure`/`turnover`; (7) `_development_history` adds per-epoch `fold_hash_diversity`.
- **Deliberately deferred**: decision-grid coarsening (researcher's behavioral lever), cross-run rolled-part cache & pre-normalized minute cache (new cache layers), prefetch/session overlap (documented deliberate serialization; substep wall-clock contention), meta-memory digest, no-improvement nudge (overlaps step wrap-up), pyarrow.dataset scan (threaded reads first; PIT filter stays in pandas).
- **Validation**: full `tests/unit` 881 passed + 41 subtests (116.3s). Follow-up owed: dedicated stash unit test (currently covered by row-count fail-fast + live end-to-end re-run). Docs: agent_design (advisory + step wrap-up), environment_design (stash bullet), pipeline_design (dev-history fields). lzp-test23 worker restarted to load the fixes; fold_2025Q3 re-runs (no ledger record existed).

## 2026-07-19 lzp-test23 Q3/Q4 snapshot-construction diagnostics

- **Evidence and live status**: re-read the current snapshot/cache path, tests, living docs, lzp-test23 ledger/status, and the relevant cached manifests. Decision anchors 2025-03-31, 2025-06-30, and 2025-09-30 grew from 9,745,071 → 14,025,086 → 19,468,023 event rows; their event-domain build walls were 323.036 → 402.189 → 1,104.299s and total snapshot walls 402.996 → 499.669 → 1,222.843s. The corresponding quarterly replay builds stayed comparatively flat at 249.314/257.837/258.293s (event builds 182.673/170.325/133.817s), so the useful missing attribution was inside the large decision events union rather than another backtest cache. At 2026-07-18 18:14 UTC, lzp-test23 had completed fold_2025Q3 (ledger wall 6,052.9s) and was healthy in fold_2025Q4 `frozen_test` (day 13/60, 234.6s elapsed); its running code version remained 14151db and the worker/cache were not restarted or mutated.

The same existing path covers decision and replay manifests; text construction discards the internal profile. No per-file timing, log emission, additional frame scan, predicate-pushdown change, new cache, Agent tool field, or format-version bump was introduced.
- **Semantics and principles**: data frames retain the pre-change ordering and schema behavior, including a typed empty schema contribution when a previously non-empty dataset is fully screened. Metrics are primitive manifest values gathered from work already performed; `data_summary.json`'s explicit compact-domain whitelist excludes the new key. Parquet bytes, PIT filtering, snapshot hash, cache key, replay behavior, and Agent interaction protocol are unchanged. Existing cache entries legitimately lack the optional key and remain reusable; rebuilding the real 2025Q4 cache solely to populate diagnostics was rejected as needless cost and experiment interference.
