## 2026-07-19 lzp-test23 epoch_001 fold_2025Q3 LLM prefix-cache collapse

- **Evidence scope**: read-only compared the first-Epoch Agent traces `experiments/lzp-test23/artifacts/run_f38aa1a0f812/agent_trace.jsonl` (2025Q2), `run_b64db35611a9/agent_trace.jsonl` (2025Q3), and `run_17c487c7ad5a/agent_trace.jsonl` (2025Q4), then scanned all epoch_001 sessions for message-cap crossings. Q2 had 68 main calls, 4,527,104 cache-hit and 353,540 miss tokens (92.76%); Q3 had 145 calls, 5,955,840 hit and 4,590,710 miss (56.47%); Q4 had 24 calls and 81.64%. The report's "total collapse" therefore describes the long dynamic prefix after the cap, not a literal aggregate zero.
- **Exact boundary/root cause**: Q3 calls 1–67 were healthy at 93.78% hit (3,637,760 hit / 241,480 miss). Call 67 had 147 messages, 78,080 hit and 544 miss tokens. The immediately following deterministic `context_summary` kept 150 messages and reported zero dropped by net length; call 68 fell to 27,520 hit / 52,728 miss. Calls 68–145 aggregated only 34.77% hit and 4,349,230 misses, with most calls retaining merely the stable ~27.5K-token system prefix. `_trim` reserved system+summary but retained `max_history_messages - 2` raw tail slots, so the dynamic summary at message index 1 was regenerated after almost every new assistant/tool batch: 76 summary events, generally net-dropping 0 or 2 while returning to 149–150 messages. Changing that early dynamic message invalidated all provider prefix-cache entries after it.
- **Exclusions**: all 145 calls used provider `deepseek`, model `deepseek-v4-pro`, and `status=ok`; there were no trace-visible main-call error loops or semantic context compactions (`context_compaction_calls=0`). The session's five Steps and 145 calls reflect substantial autonomous work (63 shell events, 32 reads, 17 formal backtests, plus exploration/search), rather than a stuck retry. The discontinuity at the local message cap and summary event excludes provider TTL/routing as the causal mechanism. Across epoch_001, only 2024Q2, 2025Q2, and 2025Q3 reached the cap; Q3 produced 92.6% of their post-cap miss tokens, because the other two ended after only four/one additional calls.
- **Cost/benefit**: fixing is justified because the supported session budget is 200 main calls and one legitimate long Fold already paid 4.35M post-cap miss tokens. A no-change policy would preserve a repeatable cost/latency cliff precisely when Agent autonomy is most heavily used. Raising the 150-message cap risks real context overflow; forcing semantic compaction adds a model call and discards all but 12 recent messages, often wastefully for folds that stop immediately after crossing; an application cache adds invalidation machinery and makes the environment less representative of the real provider. The lowest-complexity remedy is therefore to batch the already-required deterministic trim.
- **Implementation**: `AgentSessionConfig.trim_message_headroom=30` leaves system + deterministic summary + 118 recent raw messages at the default cap (or 117 when preserving an LLM compaction anchor). `_trim` clamps headroom for small test/custom caps, preserves at least one raw tail message where possible, emits the effective headroom in the existing `context_summary` trace, and fails fast on a negative configured value. The hard 150-message bound, 60K estimated-token policy, semantic-compaction policy, provider, prompts, tools, and interaction protocol are unchanged. `_session_config_summary` exposes the code-level value for reproducibility; the environment and derived-parameter living docs record it. No CLI knob, cache layer, service, dependency, provider call, or extra frame/token scan was added. With ordinary two-message turns one rewrite can now amortize over about 15 turns; using Q3's observed roughly three new raw messages per call suggests about an order-of-magnitude fewer summary rewrites, but the exact cache ratio requires a future natural long session and is intentionally not claimed from a counterfactual replay.
- **Regression/validation**: `test_deterministic_trim_leaves_cacheable_turn_headroom` drives an overflow at a 10-message cap, verifies the batched six-message result and trace headroom, then appends a normal assistant/tool pair and proves `_trim` returns the unchanged list without a second summary event. The LLM-summary preservation test and session-manifest projection test also pass. Focused command (3 tests) completed in 0.099s; `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests/unit -t .` completed 881 tests in 115.776s with only existing warnings. Available host RAM was ~201GiB before and ~199GiB after; no GPU work was launched.
- **Operational boundary**: the active experiment worker PID 2711608 remained alive and unmodified, and after validation reported `epoch_002/fold_2024Q1`, `frozen_test`, with no error. Because it loaded source before this edit, the remedy begins with a future worker/session restart; restarting an otherwise healthy experiment merely to load an optimization was rejected.

## 2026-07-19 lzp-test23 epoch_002 fold_2024Q2 runtime investigation and recovery

- **Scope and baseline**: inspected the live status/control state, process tree, worker log, experiment ledger, current and prior Agent traces, strategy revisions, per-backtest manifests, phase/data-load telemetry, orders signatures, host memory/CPU, and the relevant replay implementation. The original Q2 run `run_3e7be249fdbc` persisted with `run_wall_seconds=25,600.0`. Its 17 `budget_exclusion` records totaled 23,959.844s (93.6% of the Fold wall), versus seven backtests in Q1; Q1's six full replays were 469.120–516.721s. Q2 loaded 43,024,284 minute rows, fewer than Q1's 44,250,733, excluding input growth as the cause.
- **Measured contributors**: three failed old-run replays consumed 1,543.764s (research substep exceeded its declared budget), 837.599s (ConversionException), and 539.196s (BinderException), before the repeated probes/full validations. Successful full Q2 replays then took 5,247.686s (`valid_002`), 3,734.902s (`valid_004`), and 1,963.752s (`valid_006`). The first paid 2,827.721s in Timeview roll and 1,641.249s in residual host work; subsequent Timeview reuse fell to about 51s, while minute normalization/prefetch/host work declined from roughly 1,949/936/2,573s through 1,194/669/1,165s. Identical data getting progressively faster, followed by a normal terminal replay, identifies transient host/lifecycle contention but does not justify naming an unobserved external workload as the exact producer.
- **Reproducible strategy regression**: between `valid_006` and `valid_008`, the Agent added `trading.market_crash_guard(ctx)` to the unconditional per-tick management path. The helper opened `duckdb.connect(":memory:")` and rescanned macro Parquet on every tick. Strategy compute rose from the normal ~155s to 1,534.944s and 1,438.751s in `valid_008`/`valid_010`; both emitted the exact same `9462f0...a542` orders signature as `valid_006`, proving the expensive edit was behaviorally ineffective. The Agent later moved the work to sparse 08:00/14:50 paths and rolled back to the selected `valid_006` strategy; `valid_012` then completed in 468.492s with host 95.966s, normalization 50.801s, Timeview 48.258s, and strategy 155.131s. Thus there was no persistent repository engine or data defect to patch.
- **Cost/benefit decision**: no executable repository change was approved. A global cross-file AST call graph would be noisy and complex, while a scheduler, cross-run Timeview/minute cache, or altered replay cadence would add invalidation/locking machinery and reduce real-environment fidelity. Existing phase telemetry, minute-load timings, backtest progress, and behaviorally-identical signature warning exposed every material contributor. The lowest-complexity remedy was an experiment-specific directive: start with a Probe, inspect `phase_seconds`, treat a full replay above 900s as anomalous, never scan Parquet/open DuckDB on each minute tick, schedule heavy research sparsely, and rollback or change hypothesis when orders signatures match. It preserves PIT, real minute replay, and Agent autonomy.
- **Operational recovery**: the original Q2 record completed while it was being inspected and the worker entered Q3. A supported `stop` was not observed during Q3 setup, so the supported `terminate` action sent a graceful SIGTERM (`escalated=false`); no Q3 ledger record or frozen artifact existed. The Q2 directive was set through the existing WebUI control API and `rerun_fold` started PID 1264264, run `run_99a60a001cd2`, on code version `d813958` with the normal Q1 parent and no parent override. The runtime state is intentionally ignored and was not added to Git.
- **Recovery verification**: `valid_000` replayed three dates in 24.921s and `valid_001` replayed five dates in 41.744s. The first complete `valid_002` then replayed the real 57-partition quarter (43,024,284 source rows; 42,282,486 loaded) in 523.981s and completed publication in 550.070s, below the 900s acceptance threshold and at Q1 scale. Its phases were host overhead 99.262s, minute normalization 53.296s (prefetch wait 0.000864s), strategy compute 151.374s, strategy IPC 89.954s, Timeview roll 95.772s, Timeview init 6.771s, NL 39.395s, broker 40.010s, and state merge 1.443s. Peak host replay RSS was 20,903,952,384 bytes; host available RAM stayed around 200GiB. The worker was left running so the Agent can autonomously finish the restarted Fold.
- **Repository/remote boundary**: only `LOGBOOK.md` and this detailed audit record changed; no implementation, data contract, prompt, cache, or test was modified. `git diff --check` is the proportional verification, with unit tests intentionally skipped for a documentation-only commit. `origin` is exclusively `git@github.com:LeoZippon/CornerHead.git`; no old remote remains configured.

## 2026-07-19 Explicit units, Meta Test feedback, and interval Meta cadence

- **Requested contract and review scope**: implemented the three approved directions after tracing the batch Pipeline, HITL scheduler/resume path, Web control plane, Agent facts/prompts, snapshot unit conversion, experiment ledger projections, and the current lzp-test23-derived failure modes. Three independent read-only audits covered unit semantics, Test isolation/methodology, and interval scheduling/rollback/preview behavior; the primary review consolidated and rechecked every reported boundary. No running experiment, control file, ledger, frozen artifact, or shared raw dataset was changed.
- **Explicit units**: added one compact `AGENT_UNIT_CONTRACT`, projected through `data_summary.json` and runtime experiment facts, and reused by the prompt exporter. Identity is explicitly `(file, dataset, column)`, not a bare column name. Stable executable examples are now repeated in the Agent prompt/template and living docs: normalized `daily.parquet` price=CNY/share, volume/shares=shares, amount/MV=CNY, ratio 5%=`0.05`; `events.parquet` `moneyflow.*_vol`=hands and `*_amount`=10k CNY (`500`=CNY 5m); `macro.parquet` `index_daily.pct_chg` is a percent-number (`5%=5.0`). Unknown source-domain units must be verified and explicitly converted. Compact domain metadata now retains existing `units` and `unit_conversions` instead of discarding them.
- **Unit correctness and cache decision**: the unit audit found that raw `daily_basic.dv_ttm` has the same percent-number semantics as `dv_ratio`, but only `dv_ratio` was normalized. Labeling all daily yield ratios as decimals without changing `dv_ttm` would create a 100x silent error; documenting a special exception would preserve an unnecessary strategy-side trap. Added the missing `0.01` conversion and a regression assertion, then raised `SNAPSHOT_CACHE_FORMAT_VERSION` from 3 to 4 so no old snapshot can be interpreted under the new contract. This deliberately accepts one future cache rebuild; a per-column migration/cache compatibility layer would add more code and mixed-worker risk than it saves.
- **Meta-visible Test feedback**: only Meta workspace generation calls the new projection mode. `development_history.json` and `experiment_ledger_full.jsonl` may now include completed Fold `test_result` values through a strict whitelist: scalar total/long/short return, Sharpe, max drawdown, margin rejection count, order count, plus compact benchmark label/return/excess/beta/day-count/size-tilt. Non-numeric or unexpected nested fields are dropped. Default/Fold projections remain Test-free; Test data, schedules, decision times, snapshot identities, result paths, traces, orders, Broker/NL logs and held-out records remain unavailable. Runtime facts state raw `test_visible=false`, Meta-only historical compact metrics, and `heldout_visible=false`.
- **Methodological contract**: completed Fold Test was out-of-sample at that Fold's freeze, but becomes adaptive meta-development evidence once a later Meta consumes it; it is therefore suitable for cross-Fold Validation/Test-gap and stability diagnosis, not a final unbiased estimate. Meta prompt rules forbid tuning to one Test or copying exact Test numbers, identities, and periods into Taste. The human reveal copy now explains that reveal opens an uncontrolled detailed feedback channel and therefore seals the experiment. Held-out remains the sole untouched final evaluation.
- **Interval semantics and minimal architecture**: introduced one pure scheduling module and `meta_learning_fold_interval` with default `0`. Zero retains exactly one Epoch-start Meta. Positive `N` adds a Meta after completed-Fold counts `N, 2N, ...` only when another Fold remains; there is no useless trailing Meta. A session runs immediately before the exact upcoming Fold, sees that Fold's normal visible validation inputs and the latest frozen parent, and its Taste/optional regularization affects only later Folds. Epoch-start IDs/keys remain legacy-compatible; periodic sessions use stable unique IDs such as `epoch_001_after_fold_003` across ledger rows, artifact/Taste paths, traces, registry details, environment rebuild identity, and HITL keys.
- **HITL causality and recovery**: deterministic schedules interleave Meta and Fold sessions. Resume restores each unique Meta in plan order; latest records, completed counts, frontend labels, directives, prompt preview, prefetch, rerun validation and rollback all use the same identity helpers. Raw Meta memory remains bounded by Epoch count: only the latest prior Meta trace from each recent distinct Epoch is injected, including the immediate same-Epoch predecessor. Prompt preview stops at the nearest planned preceding Meta and refuses to manufacture a full override until that Meta has produced Taste. A Fold cannot rerun after a later Meta has consumed it without rollback. Rollback drops later periodic Meta records but archives only artifacts actually owned by frozen Folds or `meta_regularized` sessions; taste-only records that merely reference a kept parent cannot remove it.
- **Complexity/performance assessment**: the default schedule and interaction count are unchanged. Enabling the interval explicitly trades more Meta LLM/sandbox sessions for faster feedback; no new daemon, dependency, cache layer, provider call inside an existing session, data scan, or raw Test mount was added. Scheduling is integer arithmetic, metric projection is over already-loaded ledger dicts, and unit labels are small static metadata. The only data-construction cost is the intentional one-time v4 cache rebuild required by corrected `dv_ttm` bytes; steady-state construction and replay paths are otherwise unchanged.
- **Validation**: focused and expanded regressions reached 116, 185, then 223 passing tests after audit fixes. `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests/unit -t .` passed all 889 tests in 115.057s with only existing warnings. Docker daemon 29.6.1 and `autotrade-sandbox:latest` were available, so the full run's guards were true and it exercised real containerized Fold execution plus sandbox start/exec/bind/stop lifecycle. Modified Python files passed `py_compile`; `node --check src/autotrade/webui/static/app.js` passed; `git diff --check` passed. Two prompt exports produced identical SHA-256 `364f1d35832a6af9aa42eae0a88f692132b71bdfaec30c71827e243db5ff7109`. Host available RAM was about 429GiB before and 427GiB after the full suite; swap was already nearly full but RAM remained ample, and no GPU work was needed.
- **Operational boundary**: no real market experiment was launched because deterministic coverage plus the real Docker path exercised the changed orchestration boundaries without spending API/backtest budget. No active worker was restarted, paused, or modified. A future worker that loads this tree will use the new schedule/config and v4 cache semantics; an already-running process continues with the code and cache contract it loaded at startup.

## 2026-07-20 Unit-documentation scope and stale console deployment

- **Unit coverage evaluation**: compared the 597-line authoritative data document, snapshot manifests/data summaries, the Agent unit projection, TuShare audit unit families, the currently visible lzp-test23 dataset unions, and frozen strategy usage. A representative lzp-test23 snapshot exposes roughly 37 event, 10 fundamental, 34 macro, nine text, plus normalized daily/minute/auction datasets; many columns are identifiers, dates, text, categories, counts, source-specific scores, or upstream fields that can expand. A manual all-raw-table/all-column unit encyclopedia would duplicate provider schemas, drift without changing runtime truth, enlarge Agent context, and obscure the few 10x/100x/10,000x conversion hazards. It is therefore not justified.
- **Accepted documentation boundary**: normalized Agent files require complete file-level unit families and conversion factors. Source unions require dataset-qualified rules for fields already used in trading research or likely to be mixed across tables. Raw-only/non-numeric fields retain local audit official references instead of Prompt duplication. An unmapped source field is not implicitly unitless and cannot feed an absolute threshold or cross-dataset calculation until its upstream contract is verified and it is explicitly converted. Promoting a new field family to formal strategy research must add its Agent `unit_contract` rule, data-document rule, and regression coverage in one change. Runtime precedence is snapshot normalization/`unit_conversions`, then repository dataset-specific rules, then official upstream contracts; value-range guesses are only validation evidence.
- **Minimal implementation**: `AGENT_UNIT_CONTRACT.coverage` now makes the complete-normalized/bounded-source distinction machine-readable, and the unknown-source policy states the fail-closed arithmetic boundary. `docs/data_documentation.md` records the same durable decision. No exhaustive catalog, new file format, loader, dependency, scan, cache, or extra Agent interaction was introduced; the small metadata is projected through the existing summary/facts path.
- **Frontend root cause**: source already defined `meta_learning_fold_interval`, and the SPA dynamically renders every `/api/parameter-schema` field. The live API process PID 828115 had started 2026-07-18 and returned no such field because Python modules are loaded once per uvicorn process. Separately, local `app.js` SHA-256 was `c50550e5...bee72` while `/opt/cornerhead/static/app.js` was the older `29b4e8d5...e4985`. Browser/proxy cache was excluded: local FastAPI returns `Cache-Control: no-store`, frontend nginx returns `no-cache`, and the API itself lacked the field. This was a two-sided deployment omission, not form-rendering code.
- **Live recovery**: verified `/api/health` reported no running experiments, synchronized the SPA, and gracefully recycled only the console API. The reverse-tunnel PID remained 828388 and experiment workers were outside the service boundary. Local and frontend end-to-end health returned OK; remote/local `app.js` now share SHA-256 `c50550e59b72686578998c4b26370f5b5d7531c57a783e79231658d5fbdbee72`. The frontend-proxied schema now returns `{key: meta_learning_fold_interval, label: 元学习 Fold 间隔, type: int, min: 0, default: 0}`.
- **Durable deployment fix**: added `ops/webui/webui_stack.sh deploy`, which takes the existing stack lock, syncs static assets, waits up to five seconds for a graceful console SIGTERM, starts the API on the Unix socket, and leaves a healthy existing tunnel untouched. It fails instead of escalating to SIGKILL if graceful stop does not complete. Deployment documentation distinguishes static-only `sync` from Python/API/schema `deploy` and adds the exact stale-parameter troubleshooting path. A live invocation changed console PID 3837021→3866204 while tunnel PID stayed 828388, then passed local and frontend health checks.
- **Validation**: `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest tests.unit.test_webui_backend tests.unit.test_pipeline_e2e` passed 107 tests in 52.272s. `bash -n ops/webui/webui_stack.sh`, `node --check src/autotrade/webui/static/app.js`, and `git diff --check` passed. Prompt export was idempotent at SHA-256 `b0db0910cc224bb350aa95df7c8c1b1af7025fc3b71c0abaab189b97d55387d3`. This was lightweight code/test/deployment work; no training, inference, evaluation, data processing, GPU allocation, or market experiment was run, so resource probes were not required by the repository workflow.

## 2026-07-20 Complete auction units and compact Test diagnostics

- **Auction evidence**: the declared normalized-file coverage was incomplete because `auction.parquet` also exposes `turnover_rate`, `volume_ratio`, and `float_share`. A read-only 2026-07-17 raw-lake comparison found auction `float_share / daily_basic.float_share` median `1.0`; `turnover_rate / (vol / float_share)` median `0.01`. For `000001.SZ`, raw `vol=237400`, `turnover_rate=0.00122336`, and `float_share=1940560` therefore describe a percent-number turnover and ten-thousand-share float base; `volume_ratio=0.244341` is already a dimensionless multiple. Merely adding source-unit labels or weakening the coverage sentence would leave a needless cross-file conversion trap in a file explicitly promised as normalized.
- **Minimal unit fix**: introduced two auction conversions in the existing units module: `turnover_rate × 0.01` and `float_share × 10000`; `volume_ratio` is unchanged. `_build_auction` applies the same helper to decision and replay views after its price/quantity integrity checks and records the exact conversion list in domain metadata. `AGENT_UNIT_CONTRACT` now declares all auction numeric families, including the unchanged ratio. The Data, Environment and Agent living docs plus both Agent prompts state the same contract; no comprehensive raw-field encyclopedia was added.
- **Cache correctness and cost**: raised `SNAPSHOT_CACHE_FORMAT_VERSION` from 4 to 5 because auction Parquet bytes change. This incurs one future rebuild per content-derived cache key, but avoids silently interpreting existing v4 auction fields under the new units. A migration layer, compatibility branch, or strategy-side conversion would add persistent complexity and mixed-worker risk for negligible savings. Existing running processes keep their already-loaded code/cache version; no experiment was started, stopped, or restarted.
- **Test diagnostic path**: raw non-probe summaries already contain `trade_count`, `turnover`, and `exposure`, but `_metrics()` omitted them when forming Fold/Step/Held-out ledger summaries. The existing allowlist now retains the two scalars and only `avg_gross`, `max_gross`, `zero_position_days`, and `replay_days` from the exposure block. `_agent_visible_metrics()` independently revalidates numeric non-boolean values and the same four nested keys before Meta projection. This lets Meta distinguish low-exposure returns and trading intensity without exposing orders, weekly/daily series, raw Test data, paths, schedules, Broker/NL logs, or Held-out results; ordinary Fold Agent visibility is unchanged.
- **Regression evidence**: snapshot tests assert normalized auction values and manifest conversion metadata. The compact-history test injects an extra synthetic daily exposure series and proves it is dropped while all four aggregate fields, turnover and trade count survive. The real-lake smoke build produced `turnover_rate=0.0000122336`, unchanged `volume_ratio=0.244341`, `float_share=19,405,600,000`, and the two expected conversion records for `000001.SZ`.
- **Validation/resources**: focused snapshot + Pipeline E2E coverage passed 93 tests in 52.169s. `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests/unit -t .` passed all 889 tests in 114.303s with only existing warnings. Modified Python files passed `py_compile`; two prompt exports were byte-identical at SHA-256 `fcd5faf575041a8aed9d3a42c3ff552a4480ffc5e283b0b809db816ed23d3c53`; `git diff --check` passed. Host available RAM was about 427GiB before and after; GPU allocations were unchanged and no GPU work ran.

## 2026-07-20 Force-termination approval recovery and lzp-test24 live handoff

- **Failure contract and root cause**: the force-terminate path stopped the worker but previously left the current session in `approved_sessions`. Ledger resume could therefore reuse an approval granted for an interrupted, unsettled prompt, leaving the frontend badge immutable from the researcher's perspective. The required durable distinction is not process state: a worker can exit after committing its record. The implementation therefore resolves the status session key against the deterministic `hitl/schedule.json` and authoritative experiment ledger after the process has died. Meta records use `meta_record_session_key`; Fold records match epoch/fold and, when applicable, the active `rerun_id`; Held-out is settled only when every planned period is recorded. Missing/unknown plans fail closed as unsettled.
- **Minimal recovery behavior**: `_revoke_unsettled_session_approval` removes only an approved, unrecorded current session and writes control atomically after either graceful SIGTERM exit or SIGKILL escalation. It retains the per-session directive, verbatim Prompt override, GPU/parent/step settings and all audit artifacts. Because the runner intentionally ignores `approved_sessions` in `auto`, a revoked auto-mode session also changes to the existing `manual` mode; otherwise recovery would immediately start and never display the requested approval gate. Existing `manual` and `step` modes are preserved. This reuses the single established gate instead of adding a second pending-session state structure. A session that landed its business record during the termination grace window keeps both approval and mode. Pause, boundary stop and the separate restart action are unchanged.
- **Frontend and documentation**: the destructive confirmation now states that an unrecorded current session loses approval while its drafts remain editable, and the completion toast names the return to pending approval when the backend reports `approval_revoked_session`. The normal detail refresh updates the mode, badge and buttons without new client state. Pipeline design, deployment operations and the parameter/control reference document the ledger race rule and auto-to-manual transition.
- **Regression coverage**: `test_terminate_returns_after_graceful_exit` constructs an unsettled periodic Meta session, explicitly starts from auto mode, approves an old direction, terminates a real sleeping process, and asserts graceful exit, approval removal, directive retention and the auto-to-manual transition. It then previews the exact revised English directive and reapproves it. `test_terminate_preserves_approval_for_a_settled_session` points a live status at an already-recorded Fold and proves the approval remains. The focused pair passed in 1.156s; the interactive/WebUI set passed 98 tests in 4.455s.
- **Deployment**: `ops/webui/webui_stack.sh deploy` synchronized the SPA and recycled only the console/API, finally yielding console PID 4058977 on `/Data/lzp/MacroQuant/.runtime/webui/console.sock`; the existing tunnel stayed PID 828388. Local API and nginx-to-tunnel end-to-end checks passed. Detached experiment workers were not affected by console deployment.
- **Live-state race and conservative handling**: while implementation and regression work ran, lzp-test24's original `epoch_001/meta_learning` completed naturally and wrote run `run_d2a389194224` (`recorded_at=2026-07-20T10:39:32.129263+00:00`, 926.1s). The experiment then entered `epoch_001/fold_2024Q1`, run `run_68a78fdb6976`, worker PID 3977272. That Meta is settled, so the new rule correctly must not revoke or rewrite its historical approval/directive. Force-restarting the active Fold would discard useful unrecorded work without testing the original scenario, so no pause, terminate or restart was issued.
- **Revised direction handoff**: through the deployed frontend API, lzp-test24 was changed from auto to manual without interrupting its current Fold. Prompt preview for the next scheduled Meta, `epoch_001/meta_learning_after_fold_001`, assembled 11,587 characters, contained exactly `Within this experiment, explore event-driven strategies built upon knowledge graphs, GNNs and natural language analysis.`, and contained no `test_period`. The same directive was saved with `set_directive` but deliberately not approved. At the final check, the current Fold remained healthy with a fresh heartbeat, and the next Meta will enter the editable preview/reapproval gate at the natural session boundary.
- **Final validation/resources**: `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests/unit -t .` passed all 890 tests in 116.969s with only existing warnings. Modified Python files passed `py_compile`; `node --check src/autotrade/webui/static/app.js` and `git diff --check` passed. The host had 407GiB available RAM afterward. GPUs 0-3 retained their pre-existing 37,149MiB allocations and GPUs 4-7 remained at 3MiB; this task launched no GPU workload. No commit or push was performed because this request did not authorize one and the worktree already contains the user's larger uncommitted implementation batch.

## 2026-07-20 lzp-test26 Meta theme and prompt-contract remediation

- **Audit method and scope**: traced the stopped lzp-test26 experiment from `hitl/params.json`, its first Meta host/public manifests, `data_summary.json`, compact workspace inputs, 679KiB Agent Trace, persisted LLM request, generated Taste, template/runtime contracts and Broker matching implementation. One read-only high-demand prompt-audit sub-agent independently covered the same five hypotheses; it made no file or experiment-state changes. The primary agent consolidated the evidence and implemented all critical changes.
- **Theme-wiring root cause**: lzp-test26 persisted `fold_exploration_directive=请探索基于事件驱动的策略，技术栈角度可以探索知识图谱、图神经网络、自然语言分析等等。`, but run `run_38821f671230` had an empty `meta_learning_directive` and no Fold exploration field in its Meta manifest, ledger or system Prompt. The theme was absent from the entire Meta Trace, so the Agent freely selected a conventional daily reversal/multi-factor direction. This is a host wiring defect, not an Agent reasoning failure.
- **Minimal wiring fix**: the existing config field now travels distinctly through Meta run manifest, public manifest, ledger, production assembly, `AgentSessionRunner`, `build_meta_learning_prompt`, experiment facts and frontend prompt preview. The existing per-Meta `meta_learning_directive` remains separate. The new compact Meta section treats the default Fold direction as the Taste research spine while preserving autonomy to select a smallest falsifiable implementation, downgrade it on data/execution evidence, or reject it with a short reason. Full system-Prompt overrides retain their established verbatim-replacement semantics.
- **Offline-resource diagnosis and decision**: regular Folds are `network=none` and cannot install packages. `sandbox_environment.json` builds only declared Python/npm/apt packages; it does not transfer HuggingFace weights, datasets or repositories. The old Taste template nevertheless allowed Meta to recommend downloads, and the generated lzp-test26 Taste did so. An automatic downloader was rejected: it would add license/security/storage/cache/reproducibility complexity, and first-Epoch Meta output/model edits are not currently inherited. The contract now permits only packages present in the effective later `runtime_env` and complete runtime files actually adopted into inherited `output/models`; ordinary Folds must never be told to download/install and must autonomously degrade unavailable Taste suggestions. The current lzp-test26 runtime already contains Torch, NumPy/pandas/sklearn and controlled `ctx.nl()`, so an event→relation-graph→lightweight message-passing/ranking hypothesis remains executable without Transformers or PyG.
- **Test-feedback policy defect**: old text characterized compact Test as adaptive meta-development feedback and encouraged Validation/Test-gap comparison while only weakly prohibiting tuning to a single Test. The Taste amplified this ambiguity into rollback to the artifact with the smallest Sharpe gap, which is Test-target selection. Prompt, Taste contract and living docs now state that compact Test is diagnostic only for multi-Fold failure patterns, direction consistency and exposure; it may never rank/select/rollback artifacts or select factors, thresholds, models or hyperparameters by Test level or Validation/Test gap. All selection remains Validation- and mechanism-based. A semantic keyword linter was deliberately not added because it would have high false positives and poor paraphrase coverage.
- **Minute-coverage evidence and fix**: the same Meta `data_summary.json` shows the decision snapshot minute view at five trading days / 3,688,505 rows, while the visible Validation replay spans its complete Fold interval at about 60 trading days / 44,250,733 rows. The Agent inspected the larger Valid table but later generalized the five-day snapshot limit to all minute data. Runtime facts and both prompts now label `intraday_trade_days` as decision-snapshot historical lookback only and the replay as the full visible Validation period when source minutes exist, requiring separate `snapshot`/`valid` view checks and no hardcoded day count.
- **09:25 semantics evidence and fix**: Broker and replay tests were already correct. A 09:15 blind order participates in the 09:30 opening auction at the auction price without slippage; a new 09:25 order matches from the first continuous bar with taker slippage and cannot cancel the earlier auction order. The Meta prompt's generic `09:15/09:25 下单` wording caused the generated Taste to call 09:25 the primary opening-auction window. Meta manifests/facts now receive the existing centralized replay knobs, and Meta/Fold prompt plus template README state the exact distinction and forbid describing 09:25 as the opening-auction order-entry window. No Broker code changed.
- **Prompt-size trade-off**: complete experiment-facts objects and source JSON remain unchanged for audit and direct Agent inspection, but rendered system Prompts no longer duplicate the full data profile/unit map, package/CLI inventory or static path table. The stable prompt tells the Agent to read the referenced authoritative JSON. The recorded lzp-test26 first request carried a 40,196-character / 1,062-line system Prompt; reconstructing the corrected prompt against the same persisted manifest/runtime/data summary produces about 17,216 characters / 297 lines, a 57.2% character reduction while adding the theme and corrected policies. Export-sample Fold/Meta prompts also fell by about 16%/20% despite the new contracts.
- **Regression and generated audit**: focused Step-tree, Sandbox session, Pipeline E2E and WebUI coverage passed 186 tests in 64.014s. The full command `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests/unit -t .` passed all 895 tests in 121.457s with only existing warnings. Modified Python files passed `py_compile`, frontend JavaScript passed `node --check`, and `git diff --check` passed. Two prompt exports were byte-identical at SHA-256 `f9f056207c3ad380c03ec5c722375c32ed49755af2ba0c162fd433cb648aaf27`.
- **Resources and operational boundary**: host available memory was 421GiB before and after the full suite. GPU allocations were unchanged: GPUs 0–3 remained at 37,149MiB used and GPUs 4–7 at 3MiB. No real experiment, training, inference or backtest was launched; no lzp-test26 worker, control file, approval, Taste, ledger, artifact or frozen result was rewritten. Its historical Taste remains valid audit evidence; applying the corrected contract requires a future Meta rerun or new Meta session after the updated code is loaded. No frontend/API deployment, commit or push was performed because the user did not request those external state changes.

## 2026-07-20 Fold Prompt stable-prefix and core-contract refactor

- **External-framework audit**: fully inspected the relevant local `external_references/claude-code-main` prompt assembly (`constants/prompts.ts`, `constants/systemPromptSections.ts`, `utils/queryContext.ts`) and representative per-tool/sub-agent prompts. A requested independent high-demand sub-agent audited the same comparison read-only. The applicable lessons were stable policy before session variation, native schemas as the tool-parameter source of truth, explicit instruction layering and deliberate cache-breaking variation. Claude Code's section registry, dynamic memoization, provider-specific boundary marker, feature/plugin/memory layers and attachment deltas were rejected because MacroQuant constructs one Fold Prompt once per session and does not need that machinery.
- **Agent-Trace evidence and cost/benefit**: the pre-refactor Fold assembly inserted run facts/Taste/directives after `FOLD_ENV_SECTION` but before roughly 9.9k characters of static action/submission policy. First calls in the inspected lzp-test23 Folds commonly reused only about 6,144 of roughly 27,300 prompt tokens, consistent with cross-session prefix reuse ending around the first varying block. The lzp-test24 Q1 Trace also skipped `output/README.md`, searched undeclared roots and violated the already-present `state_dir`/`substep` rule, demonstrating a salience problem rather than a missing exhaustive reference. The change is therefore justified chiefly for reliability and cross-Fold prefix reuse; the existing within-session cache already makes raw token reduction a secondary benefit.
- **Minimal architecture**: `FOLD_STATIC_SECTIONS` now composes role → concise core execution contract → environment → action/workflow → submission → prohibitions, and `PROTOCOL_INSTRUCTION` is reused directly. `build_system_prompt()` appends one `# 本 Fold 动态上下文` tail containing facts/fallback info, optional Step tree, Taste, experiment/Fold directions and phase guidance. Different facts, Taste, directions, acceptance rules and phase now leave every byte before that heading identical. No registry, loader, new file, provider branch, extra model call or new interaction state was added.
- **De-duplication with retained safety**: the system Prompt no longer repeats native tool parameter tables, the 18-row path table, exhaustive uncommon Broker economics, corporate-action prose or a stale-order code sample. Cross-tool sequencing/error principles remain; common `ctx`/Broker signatures remain compact; uncommon details point to the existing read-only `output/README.md`. The newly prominent core contract still directly contains Test/Held-out/formal-root boundaries, PIT and snapshot-vs-Valid minute scope, unit examples, exact position keys, state/Broker/NL substep rules, 09:15-vs-09:25 semantics, lag/T+1/two-account truth, offline resource degradation and same-hash complete-Valid finish requirements. Native `ActionSpec` remains the single tool/field schema source.
- **Measured result**: the stable protocol fell from 21,520 to 15,466 characters (28.1%). Rebuilding the real lzp-test24 Q1 prompt against persisted run manifest/runtime/data/Taste produced 26,203 characters versus about 32,487 after the preceding facts projection; the original recorded Fold system Prompt was 64,276 characters. The generated human-audit Prompt was regenerated twice with identical SHA-256 `83ab3874a7b29ff4e87baea770fcba943b828546fc82514375ed42197b5abf2c`.
- **Regression and operating boundary**: a new test proves byte-identical stable prefixes across changing dynamic inputs, verifies section order and the continued presence of critical invariants, and confirms the native shell table is absent. Focused Step-tree/Sandbox/Pipeline/WebUI coverage passed 187 tests in 64.659s. `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -B -m unittest discover -s tests/unit -t .` passed all 896 tests in 122.034s with only existing warnings; `git diff --check` passed. This is a text/assembly change, so Docker and a real experiment would spend time/API budget without testing an additional boundary; no experiment, frontend process or deployment state was modified.

## 2026-07-20 Remove redundant auction configuration knobs

- **Redundancy evidence**: searched the complete visible persisted experiment state before editing. All 196 unique run/host manifests—and 408 JSON occurrences when Sandbox copies are included—carry the same tuple: `auction_enabled=true`, `auction_preopen_time="09:15"`, `auction_decision_time="09:25"`, `auction_close_time="14:57"`. None of the nine persisted experiment `params.json` files overrides these values. The CLI, `PARAM_DEFAULTS`, HITL assembly and frontend creation schema never exposed them. They therefore did not represent a supported experiment degree of freedom; they only copied fixed exchange/replay semantics through `ExperimentConfig` into every manifest and back into the engine.
- **Removal boundary and compatibility**: removed all four fields from `ExperimentConfig`, `_replay_config_fields`, the Agent-visible manifest allowlist and broker facts, prompt-export fixtures, formal `BacktestTool` and `replay_benchmark` callers. `run_main_ctx_replay` and `_day_tick_plan` no longer accept the four overrides or carry an unused enabled/disabled branch. Three private engine constants preserve the effective production schedule exactly: 09:15 blind orders clear in the 09:30 opening auction, 09:25 blind submissions first match in continuous trading, and 14:57 decisions clear at the 15:00 close auction. Old visible manifests remain behaviorally identical because every persisted value equals those constants; a legacy-value branch was rejected because no real workflow needs it and it would preserve the redundancy being removed.
- **Frontend and contract synchronization**: the generated frontend form continues to omit the removed names, and the existing `intraday_decision_minutes` help now calls out the fixed 09:15/09:25/14:57 ticks. Regression tests pin absence from `ExperimentConfig`, `PARAM_DEFAULTS`, creation schema, Fold/Held-out/Meta manifests and Agent facts. `docs/environment_design.md` and `docs/parameters_reference.md` now describe auction times as a fixed market contract rather than code-level configuration. The generated human-readable Prompt no longer contains the obsolete manifest fields.
- **Independent audit**: the requested lightweight read-only sub-agent independently rescanned configuration, manifests, replay callers, frontend and documentation. It found no critical/high-value missed reference or behavioral regression, confirmed the same single tuple across all 196 persisted run/host manifests, and judged the removal a genuine simplification. Its own 9 focused checks and 136 replay/config/frontend tests passed; it recommended no compatibility layer.
- **Validation and operational boundary**: the primary focused command (`test_pipeline_config`, `test_main_ctx_replay`, `test_step_tree`, `test_pipeline_e2e`, `test_webui_backend`) passed 215 tests in 69.794s. `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -B -m unittest discover -s tests/unit -t .` passed all 896 tests in 123.058s with only existing warnings. `node --check src/autotrade/webui/static/app.js`, deterministic double Prompt export (SHA-256 `5e20e9485ce404e6654c7b2f5c53b470dfda1a160f7b8ceb75e83d137cbe67b0`) and `git diff --check` passed. No Docker container, real backtest, experiment worker or frontend deployment was started or changed.

## 2026-07-20 Unify replay on a fixed session-minute clock

- **Diagnosis and decision**: the reported coupling was real and broader than the missing-14:57 symptom. `_day_tick_plan()` derived its entire timeline and submit-lag indices from available `minute_key` rows. With no minute file, `_synthetic_daily_minutes()` supplied only 09:30/15:00, so disabling a data domain silently removed almost the entire Environment clock. PIT timestamps, Timeview/state merges, substep readiness, pending age, cancellations and strategy calls all jumped from open to close. This violated the declared contract and made business logic depend on data completeness. The fix is necessary; preserving a separate sparse daily engine or adding another clock-mode option would retain the defect and increase system complexity.
- **Single engine architecture**: `_day_tick_plan()` now always emits the official 242-minute A-share session schedule (09:30–11:30 and 13:00–15:00), plus the existing fixed 09:15/09:25 auction and optional off-session/after-hours ticks. `_Tick.is_session` represents time; `_Tick.has_market_event` independently represents attached data. `activate_index` indexes fixed session minutes, so an order reaches the Broker after the configured latency even when that minute has no row. Incoming actions are submitted on every session tick; `broker.match_bar()` runs only for attached events. Empty ticks therefore expose no fresh `ctx.price`/`ctx.bar`, but advance Timeview, staged state, substeps, transfers, pending age and order/cancel lifecycle exactly like any other minute.
- **Daily and partial-data semantics**: daily data remains an event source rather than a timeline. It contributes a look-ahead-safe 09:30 open event and 15:00 close event; intermediate minutes stay price-less. A 09:15 auction order can clear at the daily open; a normal intraday order activates on schedule and remains working until the close; 14:57 always exists and its close-auction order clears against the daily close without revealing that close early. Partial minute data now receives missing 09:30 as well as 15:00 per-code events, so the two official daily anchors are symmetric. `intraday_decision_minutes` filters only Agent calls; 09:15/09:25/14:57 remain mandatory decisions. Public `replay_granularity` is always `minute`; Broker price labels can still identify daily-derived events internally.
- **Complexity/performance trade-off**: no second engine, scheduler, event bus, cache layer, dependency or new configuration was added. One constant tuple and two orthogonal tick flags replace source-derived timing. Full-minute production folds still traverse the same 242 session minutes. Daily-only runs intentionally perform the full clock work required by the contract; users can reduce expensive Agent calls with the existing `intraday_decision_minutes` while lifecycle work remains minute-correct. The same empty DataFrame is reused within each plan, and minute Parquet prefetch/loading remains unchanged.
- **Contracts, regressions and deployment**: Agent facts now declare `fixed_session_minute_clock`, optional minute events and the two daily event times. Fold/Meta Prompts, template strategy docs, Environment/parameter references and live frontend help use the same terminology. Tests cover the 242-minute plan, empty-tick prices, daily-open auction fill, intraday working-order persistence through 14:57, daily-close fill, decision-grid independence, partial-data open/close supplementation and fixed `replay_granularity`. Focused replay/broker/tool/frontend/prompt coverage passed 385 tests; the final full command passed all 902 tests in 144.148s. Modified Python compiled, frontend JavaScript parsed, Prompt export was byte-stable at SHA-256 `821a608bd1b114f50f0fe7b50012e229bd8f37dcedfac6c7edc9c8e0c4d6a3fe`, and `git diff --check` passed. The console alone was deployed (PID 1716086), preserving tunnel PID 828388; local and frontend end-to-end health and the three updated schema fields were verified. `/api/health` reported no running experiment, so no worker or experiment state was changed.

## 2026-07-20 Make finalized console code loading verifiable

- **Observed failure and root cause**: the live console resolved to `/Data/lzp/MacroQuant`, used the intended quant Python, and answered through both the local UDS and nginx/tunnel. Local and remote `index.html`, `app.js` and `style.css` SHA-256 values matched, while nginx returned `Cache-Control: no-cache`; neither path resolution nor frontend caching explained the report. Process PID 1716086 started at `2026-07-21 03:37:44 +0800`, but the last finalized edit to `src/autotrade/environment/main_ctx_engine.py` landed at `03:41:04`. The old `/api/health` had no code identity and `/api/experiments` recomputed only Git HEAD (`77979f5`), which stayed unchanged throughout dirty-worktree edits. It therefore asserted the repository commit without proving what the process had imported. A raw marshal comparison initially suggested a stale pyc, but recursive code-object comparison showed the inspected cache was semantically identical to current source; it was ruled out as this incident's cause.
- **Minimal reliability contract**: `repo_code_version()` keeps the short HEAD for a clean tree and appends a 12-hex SHA-256 content stamp when tracked/staged/deleted or untracked non-ignored files exist. The stamp hashes the binary HEAD diff, porcelain state, untracked paths/modes and their contents. The console captures one `service_code_version` when constructing the app and separately refreshes `repo_code_version` every 30 seconds. Health, experiment list/detail/status and the SPA now expose `code_current`; the CLI status prints current vs stale, and `deploy` refuses success if the newly healthy process does not match the repository at startup.
- **Bytecode and lifecycle boundary**: managed console launch uses `-B`, `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1` and an empty cache prefix under the private runtime directory. Detached workers inherit that environment, so managed runs compile repository source and neither consume nor create repo-local `.pyc`. No autoreloader, watcher, extra daemon, worker restart, experiment mutation or periodic source scan was added. Explicit `deploy` remains the atomic operator boundary after edits are finalized; independently running experiment workers intentionally retain their start-time code and continue to use the existing stale badge/restart choice.
- **Verification**: focused runtime-version/WebUI/replay coverage passed 135 tests in 23.493s. A controlled same-size/same-mtime module reproduced CPython's timestamp-cache ambiguity: a normal child printed stale value `1`, while the managed `-B` + external-prefix environment compiled source and printed `2`. The complete unit discovery passed all 905 tests in 161.594s. `bash -n ops/webui/webui_stack.sh`, `node --check src/autotrade/webui/static/app.js` and `git diff --check` passed. A real `deploy` then restarted only the console, preserved tunnel PID 828388, and returned equal service/repository versions with `code_current=true`; health reported no running experiment. Before the full run, 428GiB host RAM was available; GPUs 0-3 retained their pre-existing 37,149/46,068MiB allocations and GPUs 4-7 remained at 3MiB. No GPU task or experiment worker was launched.

## 2026-07-20 Revision-ledger semantic deduplication and process-artifact cleanup

- **Inventory and identity evidence**: all 31,300 lines / 59,962,729 bytes in `results/data_quality/revision_events.jsonl` parsed successfully. Exact serialized duplicates were zero because observation timestamps differed, but both the existing `event_id` and a fresh canonical hash excluding only `event_id`, `detected_at` and `downstream_status` produced the same 15,206 logical identities. Thus 16,094 rows (51.4%) were repeated observations of an identical source revision, not distinct events. The largest identities recurred 28 times. This confirms the existing `event_id` boundary is sufficient and avoids a second semantic-key contract.
- **Contamination evidence**: 71 additional unique rows referenced `/tmp` paths, all from `share_float_complete` unit-test union rebuilds between July 15 and July 21. The public download entrypoint already resolves temp raw roots to a local ledger, but direct calls to `write_share_float_union()` used the relative formal default. The helper now resolves the ledger itself, closing that bypass without broad path inference in the generic writer. A full data-source suite and the later full suite left the formal ledger byte-identical.
- **Prevention design and trade-off**: formal revision writers and the revision sentinel now use a shared `append_jsonl_unique(..., key="event_id")`. A kernel `flock` serializes the check/append boundary across processes; a small process-local set scans the file once and thereafter only consumes bytes appended since its prior offset. It detects atomic replacement/truncation by device, inode and size. The initial scan of the compacted 40.5MiB production ledger measured 0.36 seconds and later checks are tail-only. This is materially cheaper and simpler than a persistent ID sidecar, SQLite ledger, daemon, or periodic repair, while retaining current real-world JSONL storage and alert output.
- **One-time data actions and recovery**: while the TuShare updater lock was free, the original ledger was copied and gzip-compressed to `archive/data_quality/20260720_revision_ledger_dedup/revision_events_pre_dedup.jsonl.gz`. Decompression reproduces the original SHA-256 `6841d5b08bc959a5ded50a0aeeb99d14d429921196358c6d0f783552a02ab3af`. Atomic compaction preserved the first observation for each `event_id`, removed 16,094 repeated rows plus 71 `/tmp` rows, and produced 15,135 valid rows / 40,526,797 bytes with zero duplicate IDs and SHA-256 `6e2afa72e7e80001f5c28fb19555a438b473e09c3e188a92d0507e4e94d38be3`.
- **Process cleanup**: all 14 files under `results/data_quality/process/` were dated June 4 or June 19, had no reader, and were regenerable audit outputs. Their durable findings were already recorded in this logbook, matching `docs/data_documentation.md`'s temporary-process retention contract, so they were deleted rather than archived and the empty directory was removed.
- **Contract and regression coverage**: the data document now states that the first occurrence of a stable `event_id` is the formal semantic record while repeated probes may still alert. New tests pin unique append behavior, repeated blocked-write suppression and temp share-float routing. The TuShare module passed 89 tests; `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -B -m unittest discover -s tests/unit -t .` passed all 906 tests in 162.175 seconds. The formal ledger SHA remained unchanged across both runs. Modified data-source Python compiled, and `git diff --check` passed. Available RAM was 428GiB; existing GPU allocations were unchanged and no GPU work was launched.

## 2026-07-20 Unified data-quality contracts and bounded runtime logs

- **Schema evidence and decision**: the eight retained JSON reports had four incompatible top-level families: six raw-domain reports, a five-field fundamental report, and an unrelated revision-summary shape. `revision_events.jsonl` had three generations of record keys. Two concrete within-file collisions were also present: `event_flow` used `pit_rules` as both string and object, while the duplicated text/base report used `exact_common_limit_row_count_partitions` as both list and integer. Flattening every check into a large nullable encyclopedia was rejected. A single small envelope plus check-discriminated `details` gives consumers a strict boundary without erasing domain semantics.
- **Unified contract**: every retained JSON report now has exactly `schema_version`, `report_type`, `created_at`, `status`, `scope`, `finding_counts`, `datasets`, `findings`, and `metadata`. Scope always includes data root/window/datasets; dataset summaries always contain status/counts/checks; findings always contain severity/check/message/details. The shared builder validates these shapes and atomically publishes JSON. Fundamental consumers moved to `finding_counts` with an explicit legacy-read fallback for already-pinned research releases. Revision summary emits one finding per sampled dataset. New and migrated revision ledger rows have exactly 35 fields with `schema_version=1` and `record_type=revision_event`; observation-only write fields are explicit nulls, and the event-ID hash remains byte-semantically compatible by excluding envelope/nullable fields.
- **Text audit optimization**: the formal text report formerly ran `base --include-text`, repeating all reference/daily/fundamental scans and producing 75 findings / 123KiB. Nightly audit now calls a dedicated `text` subcommand, producing 20 text-only findings / 24KiB. A real production-equivalent full audit over 20200101–20260720 completed in 15m49s versus the prior 21m23s (about 26% faster). Results stayed semantically identical: base warning 0/15, macro warning 0/4, intraday ok 0/0, event-flow error 1/9 (pre-existing data finding), board warning 0/2; text is warning 0/7 after removing unrelated base warnings. The independent fundamental audit is warning 0/3 over 153,180 rows.
- **Live quality migration and recovery**: before mutation, all nine live artifacts were archived to `archive/data_quality/20260720_schema_v1_migration/pre_schema_v1.tar.gz` (SHA-256 `54a940e79d7f9db2c9cba4838e2db19497b91a9c79ab7ad7de27eb5a81e7a802`, ten tar entries). The six raw reports and fundamental report were regenerated by their real producers; revision summary was structurally migrated without network calls; all 15,135 ledger events were atomically normalized while preserving 15,135 unique IDs. A final exhaustive check found the exact nine/35-field contracts in every record and zero same-name detail type collisions in every report.
- **Log root cause and minimal policy**: `logs/` held 673 files / 74MiB, mostly flat. TuShare created roughly 10–15 permanent run logs per day (453 files / 62.9MiB), and 98.6% of the 71,618-line WebUI console was health/API access noise. Existing WebUI size rotation already bounded bytes, so no new logging framework was added. Uvicorn access logging is disabled while startup/warnings/errors/tracebacks remain; full `nvidia-smi` tables became one row per GPU; TuShare logs now live under `logs/tushare/cron`, the single cron stdout stream under `logs/tushare/dispatch.log`, and backups under `logs/tushare/cron_backups`. Best-effort 14-day pruning never blocks a data job and always preserves paths referenced by current state.
- **Log migration and cron deployment**: all closed logs were first archived to `archive/logs/20260720_pre_retention_cleanup.tar.gz` (SHA-256 `611f1d88c7d25a4491fce76cac53e963a89552e1fb29f7e78acfe96f8db66f45`, 683 entries; the actively open console was excluded). Of 454 organized TuShare run logs, 302 expired and 152 remained; 164 archived loose one-off files and the `api_smoke`, `audit_sessions`, and `experiments` log subtrees were retired. `logs/` fell to 29MiB / 198 files. All 14 state paths were atomically rewritten, a real audit wrote successfully at the new path, and the managed crontab was backed up, reinstalled, and post-install verified with all 16 dispatches using the new directory.
- **Validation and resources**: focused data/WebUI/cron coverage passed 197 tests; `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -B -m pytest -q tests` passed 911 tests plus 45 subtests in 156.42s with only existing warnings. `git diff --check` passed. Before the real audit, 428GiB RAM was available; after migration the workload left the existing GPU allocations unchanged (GPUs 0–3 at 37,149/46,068MiB; GPUs 4–7 at 3MiB). Generated Python caches were removed. The finalized console is deployed through the managed lifecycle so access-log suppression is active without touching experiment workers; the canonical tunnel is preserved.

## 2026-07-20 Strict schema-v2 quality reports and complete log bounds

- **Priority assessment**: dataset-key divergence, ambiguous schema v1 reuse, torn concurrent publication and unvalidated execution gates were correctness defects and were fixed first. Dispatch growth and nonexistent state paths were operational integrity issues with a small bounded solution. No broader logging framework, database, compatibility converter, or schema dependency was introduced.
- **Single v2 source of truth**: `build_quality_report()` no longer accepts a separately assembled dataset-summary mapping. It initializes exactly every unique name in `scope.datasets`, attributes matching findings, and preserves zero-count `ok` summaries for clean datasets. `validate_quality_report()` verifies the nine top-level fields, schema v2, expected `report_type`, timezone-aware timestamp, scope, exact dataset-key equality, finding and summary shapes, non-negative counts, and status/count/finding consistency. `read_quality_report()` is now used by snapshot domain/fundamental gates and both research-release copy and hash-verification paths.
- **Compatibility decision**: the unified format now emits and accepts only `schema_version=2`. Schema v1 is rejected because it already identified the unrelated legacy revision-summary shape; unversioned and unknown versions are also rejected with a rebuild instruction. The user confirmed there are no production-ready strategies and no historical experiment needs compatibility, so no legacy parsing branch was retained.
- **Concurrent publication**: each writer creates `.<output>.<uuid>.tmp` beside its destination and finalizes with `os.replace()`. A synchronized two-writer regression proves both publishers can reach replace concurrently, the result is always one complete validated report, and no temporary remains. This keeps last-complete-writer semantics without locks or a publishing service.
- **Live migration**: with no TuShare audit process active, the eight live JSON reports were archived to `archive/data_quality/20260720_schema_v2_strict_migration/pre_v2_reports.tar.gz` (SHA-256 `9882376b335f5a5128fc5976b028f23d915156c42de7eb88a0038a05ad714ef5`) and rebuilt through the v2 builder without API calls or raw-data changes. All eight validate; every `datasets` key set equals `scope.datasets` (base 29, board 13, event 24, fundamental 10, intraday 1, macro 39, revision 6, text 9). Existing semantic states remain base/board/fundamental/macro/revision/text warning, intraday ok, and event-flow error from the known data finding.
- **Bounded cron logging**: managed cron passes `--dispatch-log` instead of shell append redirection. A tiny line-buffered stream uses `flock`, rotates at 5 MiB, and retains two backups, bounding the dispatch family to about 15 MiB while keeping multi-process rotation safe. Detailed run logs retain the existing 14-day policy. Prospective log paths were removed from the pre-run payload; state/output attaches the new path only after `is_file()`, otherwise reuses an existing previous path or omits it. Dry-run, already-ok, non-trading and lock-failure paths cannot advertise a future filename. The managed installer backed up the prior crontab to `logs/tushare/cron_backups/crontab-20260721-144354.bak`, installed all 16 updated jobs, and verified zero old redirects.
- **Historical-state retirement**: `/api/health` reported no running experiment. Per explicit user authorization, nine historical experiment directories, their 74 GiB `.snapshot_cache`, and all nine shared research releases were physically removed (about 199 GiB under `experiments` plus 51 GiB under `data/research_releases` before deletion). The operation is not recoverable from the workspace; the empty experiment root and `data/research_releases/.registry.lock` remain so the next experiment can bootstrap the current committed generation cleanly.
- **Validation/resources**: focused quality/release/snapshot/TuShare/cron coverage passed 176 tests plus 7 subtests in 4.96s. `/home/lzp/miniconda3/envs/quant/bin/python -B -m pytest -q tests` passed 919 tests plus 45 subtests in 154.65s with 27 existing warnings. Before the full run, 427 GiB RAM was available; GPUs 0–3 retained their pre-existing 37,149/46,068 MiB allocations and GPUs 4–7 remained at 3 MiB, with no GPU workload launched.

## 2026-07-21 Environment package restructuring and naming unification

- **Evidence and scope**: a six-way full read of docs/code/tests confirmed the `src/autotrade` layer graph is strictly acyclic (`webui → pipelines → agent → environment → data_sources/data_quality`) and that the real defects were naming/categorization inside `environment/`: 28 top-level entries mixing five subsystems, and the same-basename pairs `environment/web_fetch.py`/`environment/web_search.py` (host transport services) vs `environment/tools/web_fetch.py`/`web_search.py` (Agent tool contracts). Living design docs contain no internal module paths (single exception `src/autotrade/live/qmt_monitor.py`, unmoved), and all module-path string literals live in `tests/unit` mock targets, so the rename surface was fully enumerable in advance.
- **New layout rule**: subpackages are multi-module subsystems; top-level modules are single-purpose components. Added `environment/web/` (`fetch.py`, `search.py` — host-side services, re-exported via `web/__init__`) and `environment/replay/` (`engine.py` ← main_ctx_engine, `driver.py` ← main_ctx_driver, `market.py` ← replay_market, `stats.py` ← replay_stats, `timeview.py`, `state_staging.py`; docstring-only `__init__` since the engine stack is heavy and the driver must stay import-free). The misnamed `features/` (PIT data prep, not ML features) merged into `data/` (`auction.py`, `fundamental_events.py`, `units.py`), and `data/` also absorbed `snapshot.py`, `research_release.py`, and `data_summary.py` → `summary.py`, becoming the complete PIT data subsystem. Kept top-level by design: `broker.py`/`broker_core.py` (clean pair), `sandbox.py`/`sandbox_images.py`/`executor.py`/`gpu.py`, cross-cutting `runtime.py`/`identity.py`/`artifacts.py`/`step_tree.py`, and single-module `explore.py`/`managed_proxy.py`/`style_analysis.py`. No hot-file internal splits, no compatibility shims, no behavior change. `environment/__init__` now documents the layout map.
- **Mechanics**: `git mv` (rename detection intact; 48 files, +212/−153) plus one sed pass of 12 dotted-path rules over the 35 affected files — string-literal mock targets in tests (`autotrade.environment.state_staging.*` ×3, `autotrade.environment.web_fetch._build_proxy_opener`, etc.) are textual and were rewritten by the same pass. Manual fixes: `_DRIVER_PATH = Path(__file__).with_name("driver.py")`, two `test_main_ctx_replay` driver path literals, `.dockerignore`/`broker_core.py`/`replay/market.py` comments, `from autotrade.environment import snapshot as ...` alias in `test_snapshot_builder`. `to_cn_timestamps` moved from `snapshot.py` to `data/pit.py` (pandas-based PIT `available_at` parsing; `contracts.py` stays stdlib-only), so `replay/timeview.py` and `nl/retrieval.py` no longer import the 2000-line snapshot module for one helper. The `tests/unit/test_pipeline_e2e.py` architecture-boundary guard (`rglob` over `src/autotrade/environment`) remains structurally valid.
- **Boundary hygiene in the same batch**: `agent/experiment_facts._compact_mapping` → public `compact_mapping` (was imported underscore-private by `prompts.py`); `agent/compact._drop_leading_orphan_tools` → public `drop_leading_orphan_tools` (was imported by `runner.py`); removed unused `sanitize_for_log` import from `llm/deepseek.py`; added `ArtifactIOTool` to `tools/__init__` lazy exports for parity with the other tool classes. Historical logbook references to old filenames were deliberately not rewritten.
- **Sandbox image**: `ops/docker/sandbox.Dockerfile` now bakes `src/autotrade/environment/replay/driver.py` as `/opt/at_runtime/driver.py`. Rebuilt `autotrade-sandbox:latest` (`sha256:dbdd239f3f4b...`, cached layers reused); in-container `/opt/at_runtime/driver.py` SHA-256 equals the source file (`4666a61bc367202c...`). No experiments exist and none are running; the managed console (PID 2774672, `-B`, repo-external pycache prefix) keeps serving its loaded code and switches only on the next explicit `deploy`. After validation the shared `autotrade-sandbox:latest` was rebuilt once more from the checked-out pre-merge base branch so the live machine stays self-consistent while the PR is under review; run one cached `docker build -f ops/docker/sandbox.Dockerfile -t autotrade-sandbox:latest .` again right after the merge.
- **Validation**: `python -m compileall src scripts tests` clean; import smoke of 35 modules across all layers OK (driver import also verified stdlib-safe); full `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python -m pytest tests/ -q` → 919 passed + 45 subtests in 161.66s with only pre-existing warnings (real Docker fold/sandbox lifecycle tests ran against the rebuilt image, exercising the renamed driver end-to-end); `scripts/dev/export_prompts.py` double export left `configs/prompts/PROMPTS.md` byte-identical (SHA-256 `821a608bd1b114f50f0fe7b50012e229bd8f37dcedfac6c7edc9c8e0c4d6a3fe`, unchanged from the previous session); `git diff --check` clean; stray `src/**/__pycache__` removed before commit. RAM available 427→430GiB around the runs; GPUs 0–3 unchanged at 37,149/46,068MiB with no GPU work launched. Next TuShare mutating cron fires 23:20 CST; the branch work window did not overlap any scheduled job.
